- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1	"""LLM Concurrency Throttle (Fix R5-#3).
2	
3	When LangGraph fires 15 parallel Section Writer nodes via Send, all 15 will
4	call the LLM API simultaneously, blasting ~200,000 tokens at OpenRouter in
5	a single millisecond. This instantly exceeds Tokens-Per-Minute (TPM) limits,
6	triggering a wall of 429 errors that crashes asyncio.gather.
7	
8	This module provides a shared semaphore + retry wrapper for ALL LLM calls
9	in the v2 synthesis and verification pipeline.
10	
11	Usage:
12	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.llm_throttle import throttled_llm_call
13	
14	    result = await throttled_llm_call(
15	        client.generate,
16	        prompt="Write a section about...",
17	        system="You are a research writer...",
18	        max_tokens=4096,
19	    )
20	"""
21	
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	import asyncio
25	import logging
26	import os
27	import random
28	from typing import Any, Callable, Coroutine
29	
30	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph")
31	
32	# Max concurrent LLM calls — prevents TPM burst
33	# 3-4 is safe for most OpenRouter model tiers
34	LLM_CONCURRENCY = int(os.getenv("PG_LLM_CONCURRENCY", "4"))
35	
36	# Retry config for 429/502 (TPM exceeded, gateway errors)
37	LLM_MAX_RETRIES = int(os.getenv("PG_LLM_MAX_RETRIES", "5"))
38	LLM_RETRY_BASE = float(os.getenv("PG_LLM_RETRY_BASE", "3.0"))
39	LLM_RETRY_MAX = float(os.getenv("PG_LLM_RETRY_MAX", "60.0"))
40	
41	# Per-call timeout
42	LLM_CALL_TIMEOUT = int(os.getenv("PG_LLM_CALL_TIMEOUT", "300"))
43	
44	# Module-level semaphore (lazy-init inside event loop)
45	_llm_semaphore: asyncio.Semaphore | None = None
46	
47	
48	def _get_semaphore() -> asyncio.Semaphore:
49	    """Lazy-init semaphore (must be inside event loop)."""
50	    global _llm_semaphore
51	    if _llm_semaphore is None:
52	        _llm_semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(LLM_CONCURRENCY)
53	        logger.info("LLM throttle initialized: max %d concurrent calls", LLM_CONCURRENCY)
54	    return _llm_semaphore
55	
56	
57	# HTTP status codes that warrant retry
58	_RETRYABLE_CODES = {"429", "502", "503", "504", "rate limit", "too many requests"}
59	
60	
61	async def throttled_llm_call(
62	    fn: Callable[..., Coroutine[Any, Any, Any]],
63	    *args: Any,
64	    **kwargs: Any,
65	) -> Any:
66	    """Execute an async LLM call with semaphore throttling and retry.
67	
68	    Fix R5-#3: Prevents TPM burst by limiting concurrent LLM calls
69	    to PG_LLM_CONCURRENCY (default 4). Retries on 429/5xx with
70	    exponential backoff + jitter.
71	
72	    Args:
73	        fn: Async LLM function (e.g., client.generate, client.generate_structured).
74	        *args, **kwargs: Passed through to fn.
75	
76	    Returns:
77	        Whatever fn returns.
78	
79	    Raises:
80	        Exception: After LLM_MAX_RETRIES exhausted.
81	    """
82	    sem = _get_semaphore()
83	
84	    last_error: Exception | None = None
85	    for attempt in range(1, LLM_MAX_RETRIES + 1):
86	        async with sem:
87	            try:
88	                result = await asyncio.wait_for(
89	                    fn(*args, **kwargs),
90	                    timeout=LLM_CALL_TIMEOUT,
91	                )
92	                return result
93	
94	            except asyncio.CancelledError:
95	                # Fix R6-#3: HARD STOP from graph timeout or shutdown.
96	                # NEVER retry — the graph is killing this node. If we
97	                # catch and retry, we create an unkillable zombie that
98	                # hangs the entire graph forever.
99	                logger.warning("LLM call cancelled by graph (attempt %d)", attempt)
100	                raise
101	
102	            except asyncio.TimeoutError:
103	                # This is OUR per-call timeout (LLM_CALL_TIMEOUT).
104	                # Safe to retry — the individual call timed out, but the
105	                # graph hasn't cancelled us yet.
106	                last_error = TimeoutError(
107	                    f"LLM call timed out after {LLM_CALL_TIMEOUT}s (attempt {attempt})"
108	                )
109	                logger.warning(
110	                    "LLM timeout (%ds), attempt %d/%d",
111	                    LLM_CALL_TIMEOUT, attempt, LLM_MAX_RETRIES,
112	                )
113	
114	            except Exception as e:
115	                last_error = e
116	                error_str = str(e).lower()
117	                is_retryable = any(code in error_str for code in _RETRYABLE_CODES)
118	
119	                if is_retryable and attempt < LLM_MAX_RETRIES:
120	                    delay = min(
121	                        LLM_RETRY_BASE * (2 ** (attempt - 1)),
122	                        LLM_RETRY_MAX,
123	                    )
124	                    jitter = random.uniform(0, delay * 0.5)
125	                    total_delay = delay + jitter
126	                    logger.info(
127	                        "LLM %s, retrying in %.1fs (attempt %d/%d): %s",
128	                        "429/TPM" if "429" in error_str else "5xx",
129	                        total_delay, attempt, LLM_MAX_RETRIES,
130	                        str(e)[:120],
131	                    )
132	                    await asyncio.sleep(total_delay)
133	                elif not is_retryable:
134	                    # Non-retryable error — fail immediately
135	                    raise
136	
137	    # All retries exhausted
138	    if last_error:
139	        raise last_error
140	    return None
141

1	"""I-cap-002 feature 4/4 (#1060): NLI entailment as an ADDITIVE, advisory benchmark annotation.
2	
3	strict_verify is regex/numeric — it can pass a qualitative-negation hallucination ("drug X did NOT
4	reduce mortality") whose numbers/content-words still match the cited span. NLI is the **second validator
5	path**: for each delivered sentence it scores whether the CITED evidence span entails the sentence; a low
6	entailment prob is a hallucination the regex missed. This is ADVISORY only — it annotates the manifest, it
7	never changes ``release_allowed``/``status`` (the 4-role D8 seam remains the single binding gate).
8	
9	**No silent degrade (LAW II + operator no-downgrade directive):** ``annotate_nli_entailment`` raises
10	``NliUnavailableError`` when the model/deps cannot load — it NEVER returns an empty "clean" result that
11	reads as "NLI verified". The caller records ``nli_status:"unavailable"`` LOUDLY in the manifest. (This is
12	the deliberate difference from ``nli_verifier.verify_evidence_nli``, which returns ``[]`` on a missing model.)
13	
14	Heavy deps (``torch`` / ``transformers`` / ``minicheck``) are imported LAZILY inside the async function, so
15	importing this module pulls nothing heavy; offline tests mock the scorer (no torch). The live model runs on
16	the VM (CLAUDE.md §8.4).
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import os
22	import re
23	from typing import Any
24	
25	from src.polaris_graph.clinical_generator.provenance import strip_tokens
26	
27	# Codex diff-gate iter-1 P2.1: the benchmark's provenance offsets index into the row's
28	# ``direct_quote`` / ``statement`` (the field strict_verify validates against), so those MUST be tried
29	# FIRST — slicing ``full_text`` for a row that carries both would pick the wrong byte range.
30	_SPAN_TEXT_FIELDS = ("direct_quote", "statement", "full_text", "snippet", "text", "source_text")
31	
32	# Codex diff-gate iter-1 P2.3: strip_tokens removes only ``[#ev:...]``; a pre-resolve sentence can still
33	# carry ``[#calc:model:hash:field]`` calc tokens and ``(atom_NNN)`` markers that are cleaned from the
34	# final delivered prose elsewhere. Strip those too, or they create advisory false NLI disputes.
35	_RESIDUAL_ARTIFACT_RE = re.compile(r"\[#calc:[^\]]*\]|\(?\batom_\d+\b\)?")
36	
37	# I-wire-012 (#1326): bounded-parallel entailment scoring for the POST-RELEASE advisory NLI annotation.
38	# The entailment judge is a SYNCHRONOUS ~6-40s network call; scoring N delivered sentences SEQUENTIALLY
39	# blew past the ``PG_NLI_ANNOTATION_WALL_S`` (default 420s) daemon wall in run_honest_sweep_r3, so the
40	# whole advisory annotation was dropped (``nli_status:"skipped_wall_timeout"``, sentences_checked=0). Run
41	# the per-pair judge calls CONCURRENTLY (bounded) so the annotation COMPLETES within the wall. EVERY pair
42	# is still scored — NO sampling (§-1.1 bans sample-based audits) — so faithfulness coverage is intact;
43	# only the wall-clock shrinks. Default 12 matches the proven ``PG_CREDIBILITY_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY`` pool
44	# (I-arch-002 #1251). 1 forces the byte-identical sequential in-context path (off-mode / single pair / tests).
45	_ENV_NLI_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY = "PG_NLI_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY"
46	_DEFAULT_NLI_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY = 12
47	
48	
49	def _score_pairs_parallel(judge: Any, pairs: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
50	    """Score every ``(span ⊨ sentence)`` pair with the entailment judge CONCURRENTLY, order-preserving.
51	
52	    I-wire-012 (#1326). Mirrors the PROVEN I-arch-002 (#1251) credibility-judge pool
53	    (``authority/credibility_skill.py``): the judge is a SYNC ~6-40s LLM call, so over N delivered
54	    sentences run SEQUENTIALLY on one thread it overran the post-release NLI wall and the advisory
55	    annotation was dropped wholesale. With ``PG_NLI_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY`` > 1 the per-pair calls run on a
56	    bounded ``ThreadPoolExecutor``; EVERY pair is still scored (no sampling — §-1.1) so coverage is intact,
57	    only wall-clock shrinks. Returns the per-pair ``(verdict, reason)`` list in INPUT order.
58	
59	    **Budget (preserves the old SYNC loop's two guarantees: spend counts against PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN AND
60	    BudgetExceededError propagates).** Each worker runs in its OWN ``contextvars.copy_context()`` snapshot
61	    (inherits the parent run_id + Path-B capture sink), ``reset_run_cost()``s that copy, and returns
62	    ``current_run_cost()`` as its per-pair spend DELTA; the collector threads the delta into the single run
63	    counter (``_add_run_cost``) and re-checks the cap (``check_run_budget``) as each future completes —
64	    DURING-compute enforcement, overspend bounded to ~workers in flight. A worker ``BudgetExceededError`` /
65	    exception PROPAGATES through ``future.result()`` (fail-closed — a dropped future never silently reduces
66	    the annotation), so Core Invariant §9.1.6 and ``NliUnavailableError`` are unchanged. Because
67	    ``_add_run_cost`` bumps THIS task's ``_RUN_COST_CTX`` (the one the daemon-wall wrapper's
68	    ``_annotate_capturing`` reads and reconciles to the parent), the caller needs NO change.
69	
70	    **Exit-safety — does NOT regress the I-wire-011 daemon wall.** The daemon wall in
71	    ``run_honest_sweep_r3._nli_annotation_with_wall`` still fires on the main event loop independent of this
72	    pool, so the primary deadlock fix is untouched. On a wall TIMEOUT the annotation worker is abandoned
73	    mid-``shutdown(wait=True)`` and this non-daemon pool keeps DRAINING the remaining pairs — BOUNDED by the
74	    I-wire-008 per-call entailment deadline (``PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S``); it is one of the "OTHER non-daemon
75	    lingering pools" the ``PG_TEARDOWN_WALL`` watchdog (now armed on both entrypoints, run_honest_sweep_r3
76	    module note ~L2504) force-reaps at exit, and the annotation *worker thread* itself stays the unchanged
77	    daemon. Post-wall drained calls still cost money — bounded and recorded in the process-global cost
78	    ledger, but NOT abortable (the run already released) — an accepted tradeoff for an advisory last step.
79	    ``shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)`` on any exception so a failing batch never blocks.
80	    """
81	    try:
82	        workers = max(1, int(
83	            os.environ.get(_ENV_NLI_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY, _DEFAULT_NLI_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY)
84	            or _DEFAULT_NLI_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY
85	        ))
86	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
87	        workers = _DEFAULT_NLI_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY
88	    n = len(pairs)
89	    # SEQUENTIAL fast path (1 worker or <=1 pair): byte-identical to the pre-#1326 in-context sync loop,
90	    # so off-mode / single-pair / cost-stays-in-context semantics are exactly preserved with no thread hop.
91	    if workers == 1 or n <= 1:
92	        return [judge.judge(p["sentence"], p["span"]) for p in pairs]
93	
94	    import concurrent.futures  # noqa: PLC0415 — kept out of the module/off-mode import path
95	    import contextvars  # noqa: PLC0415
96	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (  # noqa: PLC0415
97	        _add_run_cost,
98	        check_run_budget,
99	        current_run_cost,
100	        reset_run_cost,
101	    )
102	
103	    def _score_one(
104	        idx: int, pair: dict[str, Any], ctx: "contextvars.Context"
105	    ) -> tuple[int, tuple[str, str], float]:
106	        def _run() -> tuple[tuple[str, str], float]:
107	            reset_run_cost()  # isolate THIS pair's spend in the copied context (parent re-adds the delta)
108	            out = judge.judge(pair["sentence"], pair["span"])
109	            return out, current_run_cost()
110	        out, delta = ctx.run(_run)
111	        return idx, out, delta
112	
113	    results: list[tuple[str, str] | None] = [None] * n
114	    pool = concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(
115	        max_workers=workers, thread_name_prefix="nli_judge",
116	    )
117	    try:
118	        futures = [
119	            pool.submit(_score_one, i, pair, contextvars.copy_context())
120	            for i, pair in enumerate(pairs)
121	        ]
122	        for future in concurrent.futures.as_completed(futures):
123	            idx, out, delta = future.result()  # re-raises BudgetExceededError / worker exc (fail closed)
124	            results[idx] = out
125	            _add_run_cost(delta)   # thread the per-pair spend into the single run counter
126	            check_run_budget(0)    # raises BudgetExceededError -> bounded overspend (~workers in flight)
127	    except BaseException:
128	        pool.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)
129	        raise
130	    else:
131	        pool.shutdown(wait=True)
132	    for i, out in enumerate(results):
133	        if out is None:
134	            raise RuntimeError(
135	                f"annotate_nli_entailment: pair index {i} produced no verdict from the judge pool "
136	                f"(fail-closed — a dropped future must never silently reduce the advisory annotation)."
137	            )
138	    return results  # type: ignore[return-value]  # all-None replaced above; fail-closed if any remained
139	
140	
141	class NliUnavailableError(RuntimeError):
142	    """The NLI model/deps could not be loaded. Raised (never silently swallowed) so the caller can
143	    surface ``nli_status:"unavailable"`` rather than report a false clean pass."""
144	
145	
146	def _resolve_span_text(row: dict[str, Any], start: int, end: int) -> str:
147	    """Slice the cited [start:end] span out of an evidence row's text (get_span_text semantics)."""
148	    text = ""
149	    for field in _SPAN_TEXT_FIELDS:
150	        value = row.get(field)
151	        if isinstance(value, str) and value:
152	            text = value
153	            break
154	    if not text or start < 0 or end > len(text) or start > end:
155	        return ""
156	    return text[start:end]
157	
158	
159	def build_nli_pairs(
160	    kept_sentences: list[dict[str, Any]],
161	    ev_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
162	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
163	    """Build ``[{sentence, span, section, evidence_id}]`` NLI pairs from delivered kept sentences.
164	
165	    Per Codex brief-gate P2: (1) the sentence is CLEANED of ``[#ev:...]`` / atom artifacts via
166	    ``strip_tokens`` so citation tokens don't create false NLI disputes; (2) ALL cited spans of the
167	    sentence are concatenated as the premise (not first-token-only), so a sentence grounded by multiple
168	    spans is not over-disputed. A sentence with no cleanable text or no resolvable span is skipped.
169	
170	    ``kept_sentences``: ``[{"sentence": str, "tokens": [{"evidence_id","start","end"}], "section": str}]``.
171	    Pure (no model, no network).
172	    """
173	    pairs: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
174	    for ks in kept_sentences or []:
175	        raw = ks.get("sentence") or ""
176	        # P2.1: drop [#ev:...] tokens; P2.3: also drop [#calc:...] + (atom_NNN) residuals; collapse ws.
177	        claim = _RESIDUAL_ARTIFACT_RE.sub(" ", strip_tokens(raw))
178	        claim = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", claim).strip()
179	        if not claim:
180	            continue
181	        spans: list[str] = []
182	        first_ev_id = ""
183	        for tok in ks.get("tokens", []) or []:
184	            ev_id = tok.get("evidence_id") or ""
185	            row = ev_pool.get(ev_id)
186	            if not isinstance(row, dict):
187	                continue
188	            span = _resolve_span_text(row, int(tok.get("start", -1)), int(tok.get("end", -1)))
189	            if span:
190	                spans.append(span)
191	                if not first_ev_id:
192	                    first_ev_id = ev_id
193	        premise = " ".join(spans).strip()
194	        if not premise:
195	            continue  # no resolvable cited span -> nothing to entail against; skip
196	        pairs.append({
197	            "sentence": claim,
198	            "span": premise,
199	            "section": ks.get("section", ""),
200	            "evidence_id": first_ev_id,
201	        })
202	    return pairs
203	
204	
205	async def annotate_nli_entailment(
206	    pairs: list[dict[str, Any]],
207	) -> dict[str, Any]:
208	    """Score each (span ⊨ sentence) pair with the frontier LLM entailment judge; ADVISORY annotation.
209	
210	    I-cap-003 (#1066): the scoring backend is now the project's LLM entailment judge
211	    (``src.polaris_graph.llm.entailment_judge``) — a frontier OPEN-weight model (default
212	    ``google/gemma-4-31b-it``) via OpenRouter, family-segregated from the generator, already used by
213	    strict_verify. This REPLACES the old flan-t5/minicheck path (an old/weak F1-62.1 encoder that was
214	    git-only and unavailable on the CPU VM → the previous ``nli_status:unavailable`` bug).
215	
216	    Each pair yields a verdict ``ENTAILED | NEUTRAL | CONTRADICTED``. ``disputed`` = NEUTRAL ∪
217	    CONTRADICTED (strict_verify's contract is "no unsupported additions"; the per-sentence ``verdict``
218	    distinguishes them). Returns ``{nli_status:"ok", judge, model, sentences_checked, entailed_count,
219	    neutral_count, contradicted_count, disputed_count, disputed:[…], advisory:True}``.
220	
221	    FAIL-LOUD only on a genuine config error: a missing ``OPENROUTER_API_KEY`` raises
222	    ``NliUnavailableError`` (surfaced as ``nli_status:"unavailable"``, never a silent pass). I-wire-012
223	    (#1326): the per-pair judge calls run BOUNDED-PARALLEL via ``_score_pairs_parallel`` (``PG_NLI_JUDGE_
224	    CONCURRENCY``, default 12) so the post-release annotation finishes inside the daemon wall instead of
225	    being skipped — every pair is still scored (no sampling). Per-pair cost is reconciled into the run's
226	    ``_RUN_COST_CTX`` ContextVar (so PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN stays inclusive) and ``BudgetExceededError`` (raised
227	    inside ``judge.judge`` / re-checked per completed future) propagates out to abort the run on a cap
228	    breach. A family-segregation error from ``_get_judge()`` PROPAGATES (not masked as "unavailable").
229	    """
230	    if not pairs:                                   # fast path — needs no API key / no judge
231	        return {
232	            "nli_status": "ok", "judge": "llm_entailment", "sentences_checked": 0,
233	            "sentences_scored": 0, "entailed_count": 0, "neutral_count": 0, "contradicted_count": 0,
234	            "disputed_count": 0, "disputed": [], "judge_error_count": 0, "judge_errors": [],
235	            "advisory": True,
236	        }
237	
238	    # Fail-loud on a genuine config error BEFORE constructing the judge (the judge __init__ raises a
239	    # RuntimeError for a missing key that is indistinguishable from a family-collision RuntimeError —
240	    # check the key here so only the real missing-key case maps to NliUnavailableError).
241	    if not os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY", "").strip():
242	        raise NliUnavailableError(
243	            "OPENROUTER_API_KEY missing — the NLI entailment judge cannot run"
244	        )
245	
246	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.entailment_judge import _get_judge
247	
248	    judge = _get_judge()   # family-segregation RuntimeError propagates (NOT masked as unavailable)
249	    # I-wire-012 (#1326): score ALL pairs with BOUNDED-PARALLEL judge calls so this POST-RELEASE annotation
250	    # COMPLETES within the daemon wall. The old per-pair SYNC loop (one ~6-40s network call at a time)
251	    # overran PG_NLI_ANNOTATION_WALL_S and the whole advisory annotation was skipped. Every pair is still
252	    # scored (NO sampling — §-1.1); the run-budget gate + BudgetExceededError propagation are preserved by
253	    # the per-worker copy_context reconciliation inside _score_pairs_parallel (mirrors the proven I-arch-002
254	    # #1251 credibility pool). Results are INPUT-ORDER so the aggregation below maps each verdict to its own
255	    # sentence regardless of completion order.
256	    scored_pairs = _score_pairs_parallel(judge, pairs)
257	    entailed = neutral = contradicted = judge_errors = 0
258	    disputed: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
259	    errors: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
260	    for pair, (verdict, reason) in zip(pairs, scored_pairs):
261	        # I-cap-005 (#1068): the judge FAILS OPEN to ("ENTAILED", "judge_error: ...") on an API/parse
262	        # error to keep the run alive. Counting that as a genuine ENTAILED would silently report a
263	        # DEGRADED judge as "NLI clean" — a silent downgrade (LAW II / operator no-downgrade directive).
264	        # Detect it, count it as an ERROR (NOT entailed), and surface it loudly in the annotation.
265	        if isinstance(reason, str) and reason.startswith("judge_error:"):
266	            judge_errors += 1
267	            errors.append({
268	                "section": pair.get("section", ""),
269	                "evidence_id": pair.get("evidence_id", ""),
270	                "reason": reason,
271	                "sentence": pair["sentence"],
272	            })
273	            continue
274	        if verdict == "ENTAILED":
275	            entailed += 1
276	            continue
277	        if verdict == "CONTRADICTED":
278	            contradicted += 1
279	        else:
280	            verdict = "NEUTRAL"   # normalize any unexpected verdict to NEUTRAL (still disputed)
281	            neutral += 1
282	        disputed.append({
283	            "section": pair.get("section", ""),
284	            "evidence_id": pair.get("evidence_id", ""),
285	            "verdict": verdict,
286	            "reason": reason,
287	            "sentence": pair["sentence"],
288	        })
289	    scored = len(pairs) - judge_errors
290	    # If EVERY call errored, nothing was actually entailment-checked — surface nli_status:"error" LOUDLY
291	    # (not "ok" with a misleading zero-dispute count). Partial errors keep "ok" but expose the count so
292	    # an audit sees the degradation. Errored sentences are excluded from entailed/neutral/contradicted.
293	    nli_status = "error" if (pairs and scored == 0) else "ok"
294	    return {
295	        "nli_status": nli_status,
296	        "judge": "llm_entailment",
297	        "model": judge._model,
298	        "sentences_checked": len(pairs),
299	        "sentences_scored": scored,
300	        "entailed_count": entailed,
301	        "neutral_count": neutral,
302	        "contradicted_count": contradicted,
303	        "disputed_count": len(disputed),
304	        "disputed": disputed,
305	        "judge_error_count": judge_errors,
306	        "judge_errors": errors,
307	        "advisory": True,
308	    }
309

1	"""Pooled Embedding (Fix R4-#1 — Embedding Context Truncation).
2	
3	all-MiniLM-L6-v2 has a hard max_seq_length of 256 tokens. Our CRAG chunks
4	are 1024 tokens + 150-token L6 context header = 1174 tokens. The sentence-
5	transformers library SILENTLY truncates past the limit:
6	
7	    - The embedding model reads: Title, Abstract, first ~100 tokens of chunk
8	    - It NEVER sees the remaining ~900 tokens of empirical data
9	    - All chunks from the same document share the same prefix
10	    - Result: near-identical embeddings (0.87 cosine) for completely
11	      different content from the same paper
12	
13	Empirically verified on 2026-03-16:
14	    - Prefix (91 tokens) occupies 36% of the 256-token window
15	    - 408/664 tokens truncated from enriched chunks
16	    - Same-doc different-content similarity: 0.87 (should be <0.5)
17	    - Blueprint routing was completely blind to chunk-level differences
18	
19	Fix: Mean Pooling Sub-chunking.
20	    1. Split long text into max_seq_tokens-sized blocks
21	    2. Embed each block individually (single batch call for efficiency)
22	    3. Mean-pool the sub-chunk vectors into one per-text vector
23	    4. Re-normalize the averaged vector (unit sphere for cosine sim)
24	
25	This is model-agnostic — works with any embedding model regardless of
26	its context window. If/when we upgrade to a long-context model (e.g.,
27	nomic-embed-text-v1.5 at 8192 tokens), the sub-chunking will simply
28	pass texts through unchanged (no performance penalty).
29	"""
30	
31	from __future__ import annotations
32	
33	import logging
34	import os
35	from typing import Any, Callable
36	
37	import numpy as np
38	
39	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph")
40	
41	# Model's max sequence length in tokens — verified for all-MiniLM-L6-v2
42	# Override via env var if using a different model
43	MAX_SEQ_TOKENS = int(os.getenv("PG_EMBED_MAX_SEQ_TOKENS", "256"))
44	
45	# Conservative chars-per-token ratio (English prose ≈ 4 chars/token)
46	# We use 3.5 to be safe — better to sub-chunk unnecessarily than to truncate
47	_CHARS_PER_TOKEN = 3.5
48	
49	
50	def embed_with_pooling(
51	    texts: list[str],
52	    embed_fn: Callable[[list[str]], list[list[float]]],
53	    max_seq_tokens: int = MAX_SEQ_TOKENS,
54	) -> list[list[float]]:
55	    """Embed texts with mean pooling for texts exceeding model's max_seq_length.
56	
57	    Fix R4-#1 (Embedding Context Truncation): Prevents silent truncation
58	    by splitting long texts into sub-chunks, embedding them individually
59	    in a single batch call, and mean-pooling back to one vector per text.
60	
61	    Args:
62	        texts: List of texts to embed (can be any length).
63	        embed_fn: The embedding function (e.g., embed_texts from embedding_service).
64	                  Must accept list[str] and return list[list[float]].
65	        max_seq_tokens: Model's maximum sequence length in tokens.
66	
67	    Returns:
68	        List of embeddings (one per input text), same order as input.
69	        Vectors are L2-normalized for cosine similarity.
70	    """
71	    if not texts:
72	        return []
73	
74	    max_chars = int(max_seq_tokens * _CHARS_PER_TOKEN)
75	
76	    # Phase 1: Build flat list of sub-chunks with group tracking
77	    sub_chunks: list[str] = []
78	    group_boundaries: list[tuple[int, int]] = []  # (start_idx, count) per text
79	
80	    for text in texts:
81	        start = len(sub_chunks)
82	        text_stripped = (text or "").strip()
83	
84	        if not text_stripped:
85	            # Empty text — pass through (embedding service handles this)
86	            sub_chunks.append("")
87	        elif len(text_stripped) <= max_chars:
88	            # Short enough — no sub-chunking needed
89	            sub_chunks.append(text_stripped)
90	        else:
91	            # Split into sub-chunks at word boundaries
92	            _split_into_subchunks(text_stripped, max_chars, sub_chunks)
93	
94	        count = len(sub_chunks) - start
95	        if count == 0:
96	            # Safety: ensure at least one sub-chunk per text
97	            sub_chunks.append(text_stripped[:max_chars] if text_stripped else "")
98	            count = 1
99	        group_boundaries.append((start, count))
100	
101	    # Phase 2: Single batch embedding call (efficient — one GPU pass)
102	    all_embeddings = embed_fn(sub_chunks)
103	
104	    # Phase 3: Mean pool back to per-text embeddings
105	    result: list[list[float]] = []
106	    for start, count in group_boundaries:
107	        if count == 1:
108	            result.append(all_embeddings[start])
109	        else:
110	            vecs = np.array(
111	                all_embeddings[start:start + count], dtype=np.float32
112	            )
113	            mean_vec = np.mean(vecs, axis=0)
114	            # Re-normalize after averaging (embeddings must be unit vectors
115	            # for cosine similarity to equal dot product)
116	            norm = np.linalg.norm(mean_vec)
117	            if norm > 1e-8:
118	                mean_vec = mean_vec / norm
119	            result.append(mean_vec.tolist())
120	
121	    return result
122	
123	
124	def _split_into_subchunks(
125	    text: str,
126	    max_chars: int,
127	    out: list[str],
128	) -> None:
129	    """Split text into sub-chunks at word boundaries.
130	
131	    Appends sub-chunks to `out` in-place for efficiency.
132	    Minimum sub-chunk size: 50 chars (skip tiny remainders).
133	    """
134	    pos = 0
135	    text_len = len(text)
136	
137	    while pos < text_len:
138	        end = min(pos + max_chars, text_len)
139	
140	        # If not at the end, try to break at a word boundary
141	        if end < text_len:
142	            # Look back for the last space within the window
143	            space_pos = text.rfind(" ", pos, end)
144	            if space_pos > pos + max_chars // 2:
145	                # Found a space in the second half — break there
146	                end = space_pos
147	
148	        sub = text[pos:end].strip()
149	        if len(sub) >= 50:
150	            out.append(sub)
151	
152	        pos = end
153	        # Skip whitespace at boundary
154	        while pos < text_len and text[pos] == " ":
155	            pos += 1
156

1	"""
2	Pre-fetch off-topic filter — HONEST-REBUILD Phase 2d.
3	
4	Filters search results BEFORE full-page fetching so we don't waste
5	network / parse budget on results whose title+snippet is already
6	clearly unrelated to the research question.
7	
8	WHY THIS EXISTS (PG_LB_SA_02_CONTENT_AUDIT Section E-03):
9	- The pre-rebuild pipeline fetched every search result, extracted
10	  evidence, then filtered off-topic evidence during synthesis.
11	- The legacy PG_OFFTOPIC_THRESHOLD=0.15 was so low that near-zero-
12	  similarity evidence (e.g., Japan health insurance general articles
13	  when the query was semaglutide CV risk) survived.
14	- The "risk axis retained below threshold" path further defeated
15	  tightening attempts because its floor was pinned at 0.15.
16	
17	PHASE 2D APPROACH:
18	- Filter snippets BEFORE fetch using a looser threshold
19	  (PG_OFFTOPIC_PREFETCH_THRESHOLD, default 0.25).
20	- Filter evidence AFTER fetch + extraction using a tighter threshold
21	  (PG_OFFTOPIC_THRESHOLD, default 0.35).
22	- Both stages use the scope-protocol's research_question as the
23	  canonical anchor, not a free-form state.query that could drift.
24	
25	This module is cheap to call — it uses the embedding service we
26	already load for evidence deduplication, so there's no new model
27	cost.
28	"""
29	
30	from __future__ import annotations
31	
32	import logging
33	import os
34	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
35	from typing import Any, Optional
36	
37	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.prefetch_offtopic")
38	
39	
40	@dataclass
41	class SearchCandidate:
42	    """Minimal shape of a search result for off-topic filtering.
43	
44	    Callers wrap Serper / OpenAlex / Semantic Scholar results into
45	    this shape. All fields may be empty; `snippet_text` is the only
46	    one actually used for similarity scoring.
47	    """
48	
49	    url: str
50	    title: str = ""
51	    snippet: str = ""
52	    source: str = ""            # "serper" / "openalex" / "s2" / "exa"
53	    metadata: dict[str, Any] | None = None
54	    # I-meta-002-q1d (#951): the query that surfaced this candidate, so the fetch-time
55	    # rerank can reserve at least one slot per sub-query (no single query monopolizes the
56	    # cap). Empty for legacy callers / a stable fallback bucket.
57	    query_origin: str = ""
58	
59	    @property
60	    def snippet_text(self) -> str:
61	        """Text used for embedding-based similarity."""
62	        parts = [self.title.strip(), self.snippet.strip()]
63	        return " ".join(p for p in parts if p)
64	
65	
66	@dataclass
67	class FilterResult:
68	    """Return value of filter_search_results().
69	
70	    §-1.3 DEMOTE-NOT-DROP (operator-locked 2026-06-13 WEIGHT-and-CONSOLIDATE):
71	    ``kept`` carries EVERY candidate — cosine is a WEIGHT and an ordering signal,
72	    NOT a gate. ``kept`` is sorted by cosine DESCENDING so the most-relevant are
73	    fetched first; below-threshold candidates are KEPT at the tail (DEMOTED) and
74	    recorded in ``demoted`` (the disclosed drop->demote conversion), NEVER moved to
75	    ``rejected``. ``rejected`` is reserved ONLY for genuine structural errors, so
76	    ``total_rejected == 0`` on the normal path. The ONLY sanctioned hard DROP in
77	    the whole pipeline is the downstream faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI /
78	    4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding) — which is untouched here.
79	    """
80	
81	    kept: list[SearchCandidate]
82	    rejected: list[tuple[SearchCandidate, float, str]]  # structural errors ONLY (cand, sim, reason)
83	    threshold_used: float
84	    total_in: int
85	    total_kept: int
86	    total_rejected: int
87	    # §-1.3 telemetry: the below-threshold-but-KEPT (demoted) candidates with their
88	    # cosine sim. Surfaced so the drop->demote conversion is DISCLOSED, not silent.
89	    demoted: list[tuple[SearchCandidate, float]] = field(default_factory=list)
90	    total_demoted: int = 0
91	
92	
93	# I-deepfix-001 B1 (2026-06-28): the relevance embedder is the LOCKED slate
94	# Qwen3-Embedding-8B, NOT MiniLM. `evidence_selector._get_semantic_embedder`
95	# reuses THIS loader, so repointing it here repoints the entire B1/B4 relevance
96	# story (one embedder = one relevance scale). Env-overridable (LAW VI); MiniLM
97	# was the silent off-topic-survival root (a 384-dim MiniLM cosine cannot resolve
98	# clinical topicality the way the slate 8B model does).
99	_DEFAULT_EMBED_MODEL = "Qwen/Qwen3-Embedding-8B"
100	
101	
102	def _embed_model_name() -> str:
103	    """The relevance embedder id — LOCKED slate Qwen3-Embedding-8B by default,
104	    env-overridable via ``PG_EMBED_MODEL`` (LAW VI)."""
105	    return (os.getenv("PG_EMBED_MODEL", _DEFAULT_EMBED_MODEL).strip()
106	            or _DEFAULT_EMBED_MODEL)
107	
108	
109	def _load_embedder() -> Any:
110	    """Lazy-load the relevance embedding model. Returns None on load failure
111	    (the caller fails OPEN — keeps all candidates — never a silent off-topic
112	    keep at a wrong scale).
113	
114	    The model id is ``PG_EMBED_MODEL`` (default: the locked slate
115	    ``Qwen/Qwen3-Embedding-8B``). The ``EmbeddingService`` indirection is kept
116	    as an optional primary for callers that wire it, but the SentenceTransformer
117	    path on the env-pinned model is the production loader."""
118	    try:
119	        # I-deepfix-001 P0-2a (2026-06-28): EmbeddingService is defined in
120	        # `src.utils.embedding_service`, NOT `nli_verifier` (which never defined
121	        # or re-exported it). The old import targeted the wrong module → it ALWAYS
122	        # raised ImportError, silently knocking out the EmbeddingService primary
123	        # path and forcing the SentenceTransformer fallback every run. Repointed
124	        # to the real definition so the EmbeddingService path is reachable again.
125	        from src.utils.embedding_service import (  # type: ignore
126	            EmbeddingService,
127	        )
128	        return EmbeddingService()
129	    except Exception as exc:
130	        try:
131	            # Production path: sentence-transformers on the env-pinned slate model.
132	            from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer
133	            model_name = _embed_model_name()
134	            # I-deepfix-001 P0-3 (2026-06-28): honor PG_EMBED_DEVICE so the run
135	            # launcher can pin the 8B embedder to a specific card (static 2-GPU
136	            # split). A LAUNCH-ENV read only (not a slate force-on). Wrapped so an
137	            # installed sentence-transformers that rejects `device=` falls back to
138	            # the no-arg constructor with a LOUD warning (never a silent device
139	            # drop). Empty/unset PG_EMBED_DEVICE => no-arg constructor (unchanged).
140	            device = (os.getenv("PG_EMBED_DEVICE", "") or "").strip()
141	            if device:
142	                try:
143	                    model = SentenceTransformer(model_name, device=device)
144	                    logger.info(
145	                        "[prefetch_offtopic] loading relevance embedder model=%s "
146	                        "device=%s (B1 locked-slate default; PG_EMBED_MODEL / "
147	                        "PG_EMBED_DEVICE override)",
148	                        model_name, device,
149	                    )
150	                    return model
151	                except TypeError:
152	                    logger.warning(
153	                        "[prefetch_offtopic] installed sentence-transformers "
154	                        "rejected device=%r — falling back to no-arg constructor "
155	                        "(model will land on its default device). model=%s",
156	                        device, model_name,
157	                    )
158	                    return SentenceTransformer(model_name)
159	            logger.info(
160	                "[prefetch_offtopic] loading relevance embedder model=%s "
161	                "(B1 locked-slate default; PG_EMBED_MODEL overrides; "
162	                "PG_EMBED_DEVICE unset → default device)",
163	                model_name,
164	            )
165	            return SentenceTransformer(model_name)
166	        except Exception:
167	            logger.warning(
168	                "[prefetch_offtopic] Embedder not available: %s — "
169	                "skipping filter (fail-open)",
170	                str(exc)[:200],
171	            )
172	            return None
173	
174	
175	def _similarity_scores(
176	    embedder: Any,
177	    query: str,
178	    snippets: list[str],
179	) -> Optional[list[float]]:
180	    """Compute cosine similarity between query and each snippet.
181	
182	    Uses whichever interface the embedder exposes (`embed_batch` or `encode`).
183	
184	    I-deepfix-001 B1 (Codex wave-1 P0): a SCORER/INFRA FAILURE must be SIGNALLED,
185	    never laundered into below-threshold 0.0 scores that the caller hard-drops.
186	    Returns ``None`` on a whole-batch scoring failure — (a) the embedder exposes no
187	    ``embed_batch``/``encode`` interface, (b) the QUERY vector is degenerate
188	    (zero-norm), or (c) the encode call raises — so EVERY consumer fails OPEN
189	    (keeps candidates / falls back to the lexical scorer), never drops a source on a
190	    scoring error (§-1.3: an embedder error must not drop a source).
191	
192	    A genuinely empty/degenerate SNIPPET vector still scores 0.0 (the documented
193	    "no embeddable text -> not relevant" path), distinct from an infra failure.
194	    Returns ``[]`` for an empty snippet list.
195	    """
196	    if not snippets:
197	        return []
198	    try:
199	        import numpy as np  # type: ignore
200	
201	        if hasattr(embedder, "embed_batch"):
202	            query_vec = embedder.embed_batch([query])[0]
203	            snippet_vecs = embedder.embed_batch(snippets)
204	        elif hasattr(embedder, "encode"):
205	            query_vec = embedder.encode(query, normalize_embeddings=True)
206	            snippet_vecs = embedder.encode(
207	                snippets, normalize_embeddings=True,
208	            )
209	        else:
210	            logger.warning(
211	                "[prefetch_offtopic] embedder exposes no embed_batch/encode "
212	                "interface — FAIL-OPEN (None), caller keeps candidates."
213	            )
214	            return None
215	
216	        q = np.asarray(query_vec, dtype="float32").reshape(-1)
217	        q_norm = np.linalg.norm(q)
218	        if q_norm < 1e-9:
219	            logger.warning(
220	                "[prefetch_offtopic] degenerate (zero-norm) query vector — "
221	                "FAIL-OPEN (None), caller keeps candidates."
222	            )
223	            return None
224	        q = q / q_norm
225	
226	        sims: list[float] = []
227	        for vec in snippet_vecs:
228	            s = np.asarray(vec, dtype="float32").reshape(-1)
229	            s_norm = np.linalg.norm(s)
230	            if s_norm < 1e-9:
231	                # Genuinely empty/degenerate SNIPPET text -> "no relevance" (a real
232	                # 0.0), NOT an infra failure. Documented no-embeddable-text path.
233	                sims.append(0.0)
234	            else:
235	                sims.append(float(q @ (s / s_norm)))
236	        return sims
237	    except Exception as exc:
238	        logger.warning(
239	            "[prefetch_offtopic] similarity scoring FAILED: %s — FAIL-OPEN "
240	            "(None), caller keeps candidates / falls back to lexical.",
241	            str(exc)[:200],
242	        )
243	        return None
244	
245	
246	def filter_search_results(
247	    candidates: list[SearchCandidate],
248	    research_question: str,
249	    threshold: Optional[float] = None,
250	) -> FilterResult:
251	    """Order search candidates by semantic similarity to the research question —
252	    DEMOTE-NOT-DROP (§-1.3 WEIGHT-and-CONSOLIDATE, operator-locked 2026-06-13).
253	
254	    Cosine similarity to the canonical research question is a WEIGHT and an ordering
255	    signal, NEVER a gate. EVERY candidate flows through to the fetch stage: the
256	    returned ``kept`` list is sorted by cosine DESCENDING (most-relevant first) and
257	    the below-threshold tail is DEMOTED (kept at the end), recorded in ``demoted``
258	    for disclosure — it is NOT hard-dropped. The downstream fetch BUDGET (the
259	    disclosed cost bound) decides how far down the cosine-ordered list we actually
260	    fetch; the off-topic THRESHOLD only orders, it no longer filters. This removes
261	    the §-1.3-banned hard FILTER at the search-candidate boundary.
262	
263	    Args:
264	        candidates: List of SearchCandidate. Must have .snippet_text.
265	        research_question: The canonical question from protocol.json.
266	            This is the ONLY anchor — do not use state.query or
267	            amplifier-derived variants here.
268	        threshold: Override for PG_OFFTOPIC_PREFETCH_THRESHOLD. None
269	            uses the env var (default 0.25). NOTE: this is now a DEMOTION
270	            boundary (below it => demoted, kept at the tail), not a drop gate.
271	
272	    Returns:
273	        FilterResult with ``kept`` (ALL candidates, cosine-DESC ordered),
274	        ``demoted`` (the below-threshold tail, disclosed), an empty ``rejected``
275	        (reserved for genuine structural errors), and telemetry counts.
276	        FAIL-OPEN: if the embedder fails to load or similarity raises, we keep
277	        everything in arrival order and log a warning. Retrieval continues with
278	        the looser post-fetch filter as the second line of defense.
279	    """
280	    if not candidates:
281	        return FilterResult(
282	            kept=[], rejected=[], threshold_used=0.0,
283	            total_in=0, total_kept=0, total_rejected=0,
284	        )
285	
286	    if threshold is None:
287	        threshold = float(
288	            os.getenv("PG_OFFTOPIC_PREFETCH_THRESHOLD", "0.25")
289	        )
290	
291	    # Anchor must be non-empty
292	    anchor = (research_question or "").strip()
293	    if not anchor:
294	        logger.warning(
295	            "[prefetch_offtopic] empty research_question — FAIL-OPEN, "
296	            "passing all %d candidates",
297	            len(candidates),
298	        )
299	        return FilterResult(
300	            kept=list(candidates), rejected=[], threshold_used=threshold,
301	            total_in=len(candidates), total_kept=len(candidates),
302	            total_rejected=0,
303	        )
304	
305	    embedder = _load_embedder()
306	    if embedder is None:
307	        return FilterResult(
308	            kept=list(candidates), rejected=[], threshold_used=threshold,
309	            total_in=len(candidates), total_kept=len(candidates),
310	            total_rejected=0,
311	        )
312	
313	    snippets = [c.snippet_text or "" for c in candidates]
314	    sims = _similarity_scores(embedder, anchor, snippets)
315	    if sims is None:
316	        # I-deepfix-001 B1 (Codex wave-1 P0): a scorer/infra failure must FAIL OPEN
317	        # — keep every candidate, never hard-drop on a scoring error. The looser
318	        # post-fetch filter remains the second line of defense.
319	        logger.warning(
320	            "[prefetch_offtopic] scorer unavailable (infra failure) — FAIL-OPEN, "
321	            "passing all %d candidates",
322	            len(candidates),
323	        )
324	        return FilterResult(
325	            kept=list(candidates), rejected=[], threshold_used=threshold,
326	            total_in=len(candidates), total_kept=len(candidates),
327	            total_rejected=0,
328	        )
329	
330	    # §-1.3 DEMOTE-NOT-DROP: cosine is a WEIGHT/ordering signal, NOT a gate. Keep
331	    # EVERY candidate; below-threshold ones are DEMOTED (kept, recorded), never
332	    # moved to `rejected`. `rejected` stays reserved for genuine structural errors
333	    # (there are none on this path now), so `total_rejected == 0` by default.
334	    scored: list[tuple[SearchCandidate, float]] = []
335	    demoted: list[tuple[SearchCandidate, float]] = []
336	    for cand, sim in zip(candidates, sims):
337	        sim_val = float(sim)
338	        scored.append((cand, sim_val))
339	        # A candidate WITH embeddable text scoring below the threshold is DEMOTED
340	        # (kept at the tail), never dropped. Empty-snippet candidates carry no
341	        # relevance signal ("fetch to know") so they are NOT counted as demoted —
342	        # they simply sort by their ~0.0 sim toward the tail like before they were
343	        # interspersed, but they still SURVIVE for the fetch stage.
344	        if cand.snippet_text.strip() and sim_val < threshold:
345	            demoted.append((cand, sim_val))
346	
347	    # Sort the FULL kept list by cosine DESCENDING (stable: equal sims keep arrival
348	    # order). The consumer fetches `kept` in list order up to the fetch budget, so
349	    # this puts the most-relevant first and lets the below-threshold demoted tail
350	    # SURVIVE at the end — fetched only if the budget reaches it (a disclosed bound),
351	    # never hard-dropped here.
352	    kept: list[SearchCandidate] = [cand for cand, _sim in sorted(scored, key=lambda t: -t[1])]
353	    rejected: list[tuple[SearchCandidate, float, str]] = []  # structural errors only
354	
355	    logger.info(
356	        "[prefetch_offtopic] DEMOTE-not-drop threshold=%.2f kept=%d "
357	        "(demoted_below_threshold=%d, dropped=0) total=%d",
358	        threshold, len(kept), len(demoted), len(candidates),
359	    )
360	
361	    return FilterResult(
362	        kept=kept, rejected=rejected, threshold_used=threshold,
363	        total_in=len(candidates),
364	        total_kept=len(kept),
365	        total_rejected=len(rejected),
366	        demoted=demoted,
367	        total_demoted=len(demoted),
368	    )

1	"""M-35 (2026-04-21): primary-trial-name query expansion.
2	
3	Companion to `regulatory_expander` (M-28). Where M-28 adds
4	`{question} site:{anchor_host}` queries from a per-domain anchor list,
5	M-35 adds `"{anchor_trial}" {question}` queries from a per-SLUG anchor
6	list. Trial names are query-specific (tirzepatide's pivotals differ
7	from metformin's) so they live per-sweep-slug rather than per-domain.
8	
9	The Codex DR pass-11 gap #1 on V23: "Replace the citation mix with
10	primary SURPASS-1..SURPASS-6, SURPASS-CVOT, SURMOUNT-2/4 trial papers
11	as first-class sources." V23's corpus had 62 rows mentioning those
12	trial names but mostly conference abstracts and post-hoc pooled
13	analyses — the NEJM/Lancet primaries for SURPASS-1/2/3 and SURMOUNT-1
14	were missing. Targeted `"SURPASS-1" {question}` queries surface the
15	primary trial publication directly.
16	
17	Design invariants (same discipline as M-28):
18	  - Zero trial names, zero drug names, zero domain terms in this
19	    module. All such content lives in YAML templates.
20	  - Template-driven: the caller chooses the anchors per sweep slug
21	    by writing them into the template. This module has no baked-in
22	    knowledge of SURPASS, SURMOUNT, or any other trial name.
23	  - Backwards-compatible: missing key / empty dict / slug not found
24	    all return an empty list, so the expander is safe to call
25	    unconditionally.
26	
27	Template schema (in `config/scope_templates/{domain}.yaml`):
28	
29	    per_query_primary_trial_anchors:
30	      clinical_tirzepatide_t2dm:
31	        - SURPASS-1
32	        - SURPASS-2
33	        - ...
34	
35	Design:
36	  - Caller (sweep orchestrator) loads the scope template once and
37	    calls `expand_primary_trial_queries(question, template, slug)`.
38	  - Extra queries merge into the amplified list and travel through
39	    the same scope-validator de-drift + Serper/S2 fanout as every
40	    other amplified query.
41	"""
42	from __future__ import annotations
43	
44	import logging
45	import os
46	from typing import Any
47	
48	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.primary_trial_expander")
49	
50	
51	# PG_SWEEP_MAX_PRIMARY_TRIAL_ANCHORS: soft upper bound on how many
52	# anchor queries this function will emit from a single slug. Defaults
53	# to 15 (larger than M-28's 10 because drug-pivotal programs can have
54	# 6-8 trials). Set to 0 to disable the cap.
55	_DEFAULT_MAX_ANCHORS = 15
56	
57	
58	def _max_anchors() -> int:
59	    """Read the max-anchor cap from env each call (so a test can
60	    monkey-patch without importing a module-level constant)."""
61	    raw = os.getenv("PG_SWEEP_MAX_PRIMARY_TRIAL_ANCHORS")
62	    if raw is None:
63	        return _DEFAULT_MAX_ANCHORS
64	    try:
65	        val = int(raw)
66	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
67	        return _DEFAULT_MAX_ANCHORS
68	    return max(0, val)
69	
70	
71	def _extract_anchors(
72	    template: dict[str, Any] | None,
73	    slug: str,
74	) -> list[str]:
75	    """Pull the trial-name anchors for a given sweep `slug` out of a
76	    loaded scope template.
77	
78	    Returns an empty list for:
79	      - missing template
80	      - missing `per_query_primary_trial_anchors` key
81	      - malformed (non-dict) value
82	      - slug not present in the dict
83	      - empty list for the slug
84	      - non-list value for the slug
85	
86	    Trims whitespace, skips non-string entries and empties. Rejects
87	    entries containing whitespace (a trial name with spaces would
88	    produce a malformed quoted query).
89	    """
90	    if not isinstance(template, dict):
91	        return []
92	    if not isinstance(slug, str) or not slug.strip():
93	        return []
94	    by_slug = template.get("per_query_primary_trial_anchors")
95	    if not isinstance(by_slug, dict):
96	        return []
97	    raw = by_slug.get(slug)
98	    if not isinstance(raw, list):
99	        return []
100	    anchors: list[str] = []
101	    for entry in raw:
102	        if not isinstance(entry, str):
103	            continue
104	        stripped = entry.strip()
105	        # Reject entries containing ANY whitespace (not just literal
106	        # space — tabs / newlines / vertical-tab / form-feed /
107	        # carriage-return would all break the outer `"{anchor}"`
108	        # quoting downstream) or a double quote (ASCII U+0022 would
109	        # break the outer quoting directly) or a backslash (could
110	        # survive as `"BAD\" q` and escape-eat the closing quote in
111	        # some downstream search-query parsers). M-35 pass-2 (Codex
112	        # blocker): `str.strip()` removes leading/trailing whitespace
113	        # but NOT interior whitespace, so `"BAD\tENTRY"` would pass
114	        # the pre-pass-2 `" " in stripped` check. isspace() closes
115	        # that.
116	        if (
117	            not stripped
118	            or any(ch.isspace() for ch in stripped)
119	            or '"' in stripped
120	            or "\\" in stripped
121	        ):
122	            continue
123	        anchors.append(stripped)
124	    # Deduplicate while preserving declared order.
125	    seen: set[str] = set()
126	    unique: list[str] = []
127	    for a in anchors:
128	        if a not in seen:
129	            seen.add(a)
130	            unique.append(a)
131	    return unique
132	
133	
134	def _is_valid_doi(d: str) -> bool:
135	    """A DOI is 10.<registrant>/<suffix>: registrant is 4-9 digits, suffix is
136	    non-empty, and the whole string has no whitespace/quote/backslash (which
137	    would break the doi.org URL or downstream quoting). iter-1 P2 (Codex):
138	    reject empty-component forms like '10./x' or '10.1056/'."""
139	    if not d.startswith("10.") or "/" not in d:
140	        return False
141	    prefix, _, suffix = d.partition("/")
142	    registrant = prefix[len("10."):]
143	    return (
144	        registrant.isdigit()
145	        and 4 <= len(registrant) <= 9
146	        and bool(suffix)
147	        and not any(ch.isspace() for ch in d)
148	        and '"' not in d
149	        and "\\" not in d
150	    )
151	
152	
153	def expand_primary_trial_dois(
154	    template: dict[str, Any] | None,
155	    slug: str,
156	) -> list[str]:
157	    """I-bug-776 (#817) layer-4 (Codex decision b): return direct doi.org
158	    candidate URLs for the anchored primary trials of `slug`, read from
159	    `per_query_primary_trial_dois` (a {ANCHOR: DOI} dict) in the scope template.
160	
161	    These are injected as DIRECT retrieval candidates so the pivotal OA primaries
162	    reliably enter the corpus even when guideline-dominated search ranking buries
163	    them. They pass the SAME fetch / OA / extraction / tier / adequacy gates as
164	    every other source (no laundering).
165	
166	    Backwards-compatible: missing template / key / slug / malformed -> []. Only
167	    well-formed DOIs are accepted; slug-scoped (no global fallback).
168	    """
169	    if not isinstance(template, dict):
170	        return []
171	    if not isinstance(slug, str) or not slug.strip():
172	        return []
173	    by_slug = template.get("per_query_primary_trial_dois")
174	    if not isinstance(by_slug, dict):
175	        return []
176	    raw = by_slug.get(slug)
177	    if not isinstance(raw, dict):
178	        return []
179	    urls: list[str] = []
180	    seen: set[str] = set()
181	    for _anchor, doi in raw.items():
182	        if not isinstance(doi, str):
183	            continue
184	        d = doi.strip()
185	        if not _is_valid_doi(d):
186	            continue
187	        url = f"https://doi.org/{d}"
188	        if url not in seen:
189	            seen.add(url)
190	            urls.append(url)
191	    return urls
192	
193	
194	def get_primary_trial_anchors_for_slug(
195	    template: dict[str, Any] | None,
196	    slug: str,
197	) -> list[str]:
198	    """M-42e (2026-04-22): public accessor for the raw anchor list
199	    for a given sweep slug. Used by the evidence selector to apply
200	    a T1 primary-paper floor. Same validation rules as `_extract_anchors`
201	    — returns cleaned, deduplicated anchors or empty list."""
202	    return _extract_anchors(template, slug)
203	
204	
205	def _extract_variants(
206	    template: dict[str, Any] | None,
207	    slug: str,
208	) -> dict[str, str]:
209	    """M-48 (2026-04-22): pull per-anchor first-author + journal variant
210	    strings for a given sweep slug.
211	
212	    Schema:
213	        per_query_primary_trial_variants:
214	          <slug>:
215	            <anchor>: <free-text variant>
216	
217	    Returns an empty dict for:
218	      - missing template
219	      - missing `per_query_primary_trial_variants` key
220	      - malformed (non-dict) value at any level
221	      - slug not present
222	      - variant value not a string
223	      - variant string contains ANY whitespace-only content or is empty
224	
225	    The variant string is used as-is (wrapped in the outer query with
226	    the base question appended). Example:
227	        anchor = "SURPASS-2"
228	        variant = "Frías NEJM tirzepatide semaglutide"
229	        emitted query = `"SURPASS-2" Frías NEJM tirzepatide semaglutide {question}`
230	    """
231	    if not isinstance(template, dict):
232	        return {}
233	    if not isinstance(slug, str) or not slug.strip():
234	        return {}
235	    by_slug = template.get("per_query_primary_trial_variants")
236	    if not isinstance(by_slug, dict):
237	        return {}
238	    raw = by_slug.get(slug)
239	    if not isinstance(raw, dict):
240	        return {}
241	    out: dict[str, str] = {}
242	    for anchor, variant in raw.items():
243	        if not isinstance(anchor, str) or not isinstance(variant, str):
244	            continue
245	        a = anchor.strip()
246	        v = variant.strip()
247	        # Reject anchor with interior whitespace / double quote /
248	        # backslash (same invariant as _extract_anchors) and reject
249	        # variant containing a double quote that would break the
250	        # outer `"{anchor}"` quoting.
251	        if (
252	            not a or any(ch.isspace() for ch in a)
253	            or '"' in a or "\\" in a
254	        ):
255	            continue
256	        if not v or '"' in v:
257	            continue
258	        out[a] = v
259	    return out
260	
261	
262	def get_trial_population_scope_for_slug(
263	    template: dict[str, Any] | None,
264	    slug: str,
265	) -> dict[str, str]:
266	    """M-48 (2026-04-22): public accessor for the per-anchor population-
267	    scope labels.
268	
269	    Schema:
270	        per_query_trial_population_scope:
271	          <slug>:
272	            <anchor>: "direct" | "indirect_for_t2d" | "indirect"
273	
274	    Used by `label_rows_with_population_scope` to tag evidence rows
275	    after retrieval. Missing entry → row stays unlabeled
276	    (generator treats as "direct" by default).
277	    """
278	    if not isinstance(template, dict):
279	        return {}
280	    if not isinstance(slug, str) or not slug.strip():
281	        return {}
282	    by_slug = template.get("per_query_trial_population_scope")
283	    if not isinstance(by_slug, dict):
284	        return {}
285	    raw = by_slug.get(slug)
286	    if not isinstance(raw, dict):
287	        return {}
288	    valid_labels = {"direct", "indirect_for_t2d", "indirect"}
289	    out: dict[str, str] = {}
290	    for anchor, label in raw.items():
291	        if not isinstance(anchor, str) or not isinstance(label, str):
292	            continue
293	        a = anchor.strip()
294	        l_ = label.strip().lower()
295	        if not a or any(ch.isspace() for ch in a):
296	            continue
297	        if l_ not in valid_labels:
298	            continue
299	        out[a] = l_
300	    return out
301	
302	
303	def label_rows_with_population_scope(
304	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
305	    template: dict[str, Any] | None,
306	    slug: str,
307	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
308	    """M-48 (2026-04-22): annotate evidence rows with population-scope
309	    metadata derived from per-anchor labels.
310	
311	    For each row, scan title for any configured anchor token (case-
312	    insensitive substring match). If match found, add keys:
313	      - `population_scope`: one of "direct" / "indirect_for_t2d" /
314	        "indirect"
315	      - `indirect_for_t2d`: bool (True iff label == indirect_for_t2d)
316	
317	    Rows with no anchor match are unchanged. This function mutates
318	    rows in place AND returns the list (convenience).
319	
320	    Example usage in the sweep orchestrator, after `retrieval.evidence_rows`
321	    is populated:
322	
323	        rows = label_rows_with_population_scope(
324	            retrieval.evidence_rows, template, slug
325	        )
326	    """
327	    labels = get_trial_population_scope_for_slug(template, slug)
328	    if not labels or not rows:
329	        return rows
330	    # Build case-insensitive lookup: {anchor_lower: (anchor_original, label)}
331	    lookup = {a.lower(): (a, l_) for a, l_ in labels.items()}
332	    for row in rows:
333	        if not isinstance(row, dict):
334	            continue
335	        # M-48 pass-2 (Codex blocker): live retriever rows populate
336	        # `statement` with the candidate title (not `title`). Read
337	        # from title / statement / source_title in that order so the
338	        # labeler works on both live rows and fixture rows.
339	        title = ""
340	        for key in ("title", "statement", "source_title"):
341	            v = row.get(key)
342	            if isinstance(v, str) and v:
343	                title = v
344	                break
345	        title_l = title.lower()
346	        for anchor_l, (_anchor, label) in lookup.items():
347	            if anchor_l in title_l:
348	                row["population_scope"] = label
349	                row["indirect_for_t2d"] = (label == "indirect_for_t2d")
350	                row["_m48_anchor_match"] = _anchor
351	                break
352	    return rows
353	
354	
355	def expand_primary_trial_queries(
356	    question: str,
357	    template: dict[str, Any] | None,
358	    slug: str,
359	) -> list[str]:
360	    """Return anchor + variant queries for a given sweep slug.
361	
362	    For each configured anchor:
363	      - emits `"{anchor}" {question}` (original M-35 form)
364	      - IF the anchor has a variant in `per_query_primary_trial_variants`,
365	        also emits `"{anchor}" {variant} {question}` (M-48 form)
366	
367	    The variant query carries first-author surname + target journal to
368	    raise primary-publication landing probability. V27 hit 4/11 primary
369	    trials with anchor-only queries; M-48 aims for ≥9/11.
370	
371	    Args:
372	        question: the user's research question (or any base query text).
373	        template: the loaded scope template dict (or None if none set).
374	        slug: the sweep slug used to key into
375	            `per_query_primary_trial_anchors` and
376	            `per_query_primary_trial_variants`.
377	
378	    Returns:
379	        A list of query strings; order is `[anchor1, anchor1_variant,
380	        anchor2, anchor2_variant, ...]` for anchors with variants, else
381	        `[anchor1, anchor2, ...]`. Total capped by
382	        `PG_SWEEP_MAX_PRIMARY_TRIAL_ANCHORS` applied to the ANCHOR count
383	        (variants do not count against the cap so that capped runs
384	        retain their variant queries).
385	
386	    Pure and deterministic — no network, no state.
387	    """
388	    base = (question or "").strip()
389	    if not base:
390	        return []
391	    anchors = _extract_anchors(template, slug)
392	    if not anchors:
393	        return []
394	    cap = _max_anchors()
395	    if cap > 0 and len(anchors) > cap:
396	        logger.info(
397	            "[primary_trial_expander] slug=%r has %d anchors; capped "
398	            "to %d via PG_SWEEP_MAX_PRIMARY_TRIAL_ANCHORS",
399	            slug, len(anchors), cap,
400	        )
401	        anchors = anchors[:cap]
402	    variants = _extract_variants(template, slug)
403	    queries: list[str] = []
404	    variant_count = 0
405	    for anchor in anchors:
406	        queries.append(f'"{anchor}" {base}')
407	        variant = variants.get(anchor)
408	        if variant:
409	            queries.append(f'"{anchor}" {variant} {base}')
410	            variant_count += 1
411	    logger.info(
412	        "[primary_trial_expander] emitted %d queries (%d anchors + "
413	        "%d variants) for slug=%r base=%r",
414	        len(queries), len(anchors), variant_count, slug, base[:60],
415	    )
416	    return queries
417

1 """Publication-date resolver — htmldate-style cascade over ALREADY-fetched content. 2 3 I-deepfix-001 FF5 (fix-wave, Part A prerequisite): the temporal-scope enforcement 4 predicate (``constraint_enforcement._row_pub_ym`` / ``_row_out_of_window``) can only 5 judge a source out-of-window when the source row carries a structured publication 6 date. Today the ONLY code that stamps a date is the OpenAlex authority enrich, so 7 every crawled web page / institutional PDF / general-web source arrives UNDATED and 8 fail-opens straight into the grounding set even under a HARD user-stated date bound 9 (e.g. "based on academic research published before June 2023"). 10 11 This module gives the enforcement predicate the dates it lacks by resolving a 12 publication date from content the pipeline ALREADY fetched — ZERO new network. It is a 13 pure function: same inputs → same output, no I/O, no env reads, no global state. The 14 env gate (``PG_RESOLVE_PUBDATE_FROM_HTML``) and the wiring into ``live_retriever`` / 15 ``constraint_enforcement`` are a SEPARATE wave (FF6); this wave ships the pure resolver 16 plus its unit test only. (FF6 also threads the OpenAlex ``cand.metadata`` — whose 17 ``year`` the authority-enrich path discards — through the new ``search_metadata`` 18 argument, so the leaked 2026 ``openalex.org`` row is dated at zero cost.) 19 20 Cascade precedence (htmldate — JOSS 10.21105/joss.02439; Parse.ly / Webz.io metadata 21 guidance confirm JSON-LD as the most reliable signal, meta tags + as fallbacks). 22 Structured signals (publisher HTML metadata AND the search-result API metadata) rank 23 ahead of the weaker URL-slug / PDF-creation heuristics: 24 25 1. JSON-LD ``datePublished`` (then ``dateCreated``) inside ', 73 re.DOTALL | re.IGNORECASE, 74 ) 75 76 # JSON-LD date keys, searched as raw JSON string values so nesting (@graph arrays, nested 77 # objects) and block concatenation are handled uniformly without a strict JSON parse. 78 _JSONLD_DATE_KEYS = ("datePublished", "dateCreated") 79 80 # 1	"""Qualitative present-vs-absent clinical-safety conflict detector (I-meta-002-q1d #944).
2	
3	The numeric `contradiction_detector` only sees disagreements that carry a number. The most
4	patient-dangerous disagreements — contraindication PRESENT vs ABSENT, drug-interaction warning
5	present vs absent, eligibility/exclusion — carry NO number and are structurally invisible to it
6	(the lethal-error class per `feedback_qualitative_negation_escapes_regex_2026_05_26`). This module
7	adds a parallel, precision-first qualitative assertion-status conflict path, surfaced into the SAME
8	`contradictions.json` + report.
9	
10	Method: NegEx (Chapman 2001) / ConText (Harkema-Chapman 2009) rule-cue assertion-status — no LLM by
11	default (NO SPEND). Cue precedence per span (Codex brief-gate iter-2 P1.a):
12	    permissive_allow -> statistical_null -> uncertainty -> real_negation(net XOR) -> antonym/affirm -> hedge
13	so a deontic "may be co-administered" (ABSENT, definite) wins over the epistemic hedge "may"
14	(INDETERMINATE) and the mandatory DDI antonym recall case hard-fires.
15	
16	Two-pass detection (Codex brief-gate iter-1 P1.2 + iter-2 P1.b):
17	- PASS A (hard conflict): group by the FULL key (subject, concept_type, object_slot, condition_scope);
18	  flag only same-key clusters with >=2 DISTINCT sources holding DIFFERING DEFINITE polarity
19	  (PRESENT vs ABSENT). INDETERMINATE / STATISTICAL_NULL cannot anchor a hard conflict.
20	- PASS B (review candidate): group by the COARSE key (subject, concept_type) only; every definite-
21	  disagreeing distinct-source pair NOT already a hard conflict — because object_slot/condition_scope
22	  differ or are missing/broad, or a definite opposes an INDETERMINATE/STATISTICAL_NULL — is emitted as
23	  a `review_flag` (severity "review"), NEVER dropped. Only BOTH object_slots resolved to clearly-
24	  different specific entities is a determinable non-conflict (no flag).
25	
26	Fail-safe = escalate-to-review, never silent: a missed contradiction is the lethal error.
27	"High precision" means do NOT fire on a DETERMINABLE non-conflict, NOT go quiet when unsure.
28	"""

157	_SENTENCE_SPLIT_RE = re.compile(r"(?<=[.;!?])\s+")

165	def _split_clauses(text: str, term_terms: list[str]) -> list[str]:
166	    """Split a sentence into clauses on commas/semicolons AND termination terms (NegEx conjunctions),
167	    so high-precedence status cues (permissive/statistical/uncertainty/hedge) are scoped to the clause
168	    that holds the concept — NOT leaked across clauses (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1.3). ALSO splits on
169	    coordinating `and`/`or` so same-concept coordinated assertions ('safe in pregnancy AND
170	    contraindicated in lactation') become SEPARATE assertions each bound to its own condition (Codex
171	    diff-gate iter-4 P1); a clause that loses its drug inherits the sentence subject. Over-splitting a
172	    compound condition ('renal AND hepatic impairment') only UNDER-captures (the trailing fragment has
173	    no concept cue) — it never manufactures a false conflict. Original case preserved for the snippet."""
174	    pattern = r"[;,]|\b(?:and|or|" + "|".join(re.escape(t) for t in term_terms) + r")\b"
175	    return [p.strip() for p in re.split(pattern, text or "", flags=re.IGNORECASE) if p and p.strip()]

178	def _find_longest_cue(sentence_lc: str, cues: list[str]) -> Optional[tuple[str, int]]:
179	    """Return (cue, position) of the LONGEST matching cue in the sentence, else None.
180	    Longest-first so 'no contraindication' (absent) beats 'contraindication' (present)."""
181	    best: Optional[tuple[str, int]] = None
182	    for cue in cues:
183	        pos = sentence_lc.find(cue)
184	        if pos >= 0 and (best is None or len(cue) > len(best[0])):
185	            best = (cue, pos)
186	    return best

196	def _pre_scope(sentence_lc: str, concept_pos: int, term_terms: list[str], token_cap: int) -> str:
197	    """Bounded pre-negation region: up to token_cap words BEFORE the concept term, stopping at the
198	    nearest termination term (NegEx directional, precision-tightened window)."""
199	    pre = sentence_lc[:concept_pos]
200	    # truncate at the last termination term before the concept
201	    cut = 0
202	    for tt in term_terms:
203	        idx = pre.rfind(f" {tt} ")
204	        if idx >= 0:
205	            cut = max(cut, idx + len(tt) + 2)
206	    pre = pre[cut:]
207	    words = _WORD_RE.findall(pre)
208	    return " ".join(words[-token_cap:])

211	def _net_negation_flip(pre_region: str, concept_cue: str, real_neg: list[str]) -> bool:
212	    """Count real-negation cues in the bounded pre-region + a leading 'non-' on the concept; return
213	    True if the NET polarity flips (odd count) — collapses double negation (Codex iter-1 P1.6 net XOR).
214	    Cues are matched LONGEST-FIRST and each match is MASKED so an overlapping short cue cannot
215	    re-count inside it (Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1.b: 'no evidence of' must NOT also count 'no')."""
216	    region = " " + pre_region + " "
217	    count = 0
218	    for cue in sorted(real_neg, key=len, reverse=True):
219	        if " " in cue:
220	            pat = re.compile(re.escape(cue))
221	        else:
222	            pat = re.compile(rf"(?<![A-Za-z]){re.escape(cue)}(?![A-Za-z])")
223	        region, n = pat.subn(lambda m: " " * (m.end() - m.start()), region)
224	        count += n
225	    if concept_cue.startswith("non-") or " non-" in (" " + pre_region):
226	        count += 1
227	    return count % 2 == 1

230	def _local_window(text_lc: str, concept_pos: int, cue_len: int, token_window: int,
231	                  term_terms: list[str]) -> str:
232	    """A bounded window around the matched concept cue, CLIPPED at the nearest coordinating boundary
233	    (comma/semicolon/'and'/'or'/termination term) on EACH side, then capped to `token_window` words.
234	    High-precedence status cues are checked HERE (not clause-wide) so a permissive/statistical/hedge
235	    cue that belongs to a DIFFERENT coordinated concept cannot leak onto this one — in EITHER order
236	    (Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1.a + iter-3 P1: '... and may be co-administered' must not flip a
237	    preceding contraindication)."""
238	    boundary = re.compile(r"[;,]|\b(?:and|or|" + "|".join(re.escape(t) for t in term_terms) + r")\b",
239	                          re.IGNORECASE)
240	    start = 0
241	    for m in boundary.finditer(text_lc[:concept_pos]):
242	        start = m.end()
243	    cue_end = concept_pos + cue_len
244	    nxt = boundary.search(text_lc, cue_end)
245	    end = nxt.start() if nxt else len(text_lc)
246	    seg, cpos = text_lc[start:end], concept_pos - start
247	    pre_words = _WORD_RE.findall(seg[:cpos])[-token_window:]
248	    post_words = _WORD_RE.findall(seg[cpos + cue_len:])[:token_window]
249	    return " ".join(pre_words) + " " + seg[cpos:cpos + cue_len] + " " + " ".join(post_words)
250

252	def _classify_status(clause_lc: str, concept_cue: str, concept_pos: int, base: str,
253	                     lex: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
254	    """Resolve assertion status with the fixed cue precedence, evaluating the high-precedence cue
255	    classes in a BOUNDED, coordinator-clipped WINDOW around the matched concept (not clause-wide)."""
256	    window = _local_window(clause_lc, concept_pos, len(concept_cue),
257	                           int(lex["scope_token_cap"]), lex["termination_terms"])
258	    # 1. permissive/deontic permission -> ABSENT (definite), wins over hedge.
259	    if _phrase_present(window, lex["permissive_allow"]):
260	        return ABSENT
261	    # 2. statistical null (the lethal-FP class) -> STATISTICAL_NULL.
262	    if _phrase_present(window, lex["statistical_null"]):
263	        return STATISTICAL_NULL
264	    # 3. epistemic uncertainty -> INDETERMINATE.
265	    if _phrase_present(window, lex["uncertainty"]):
266	        return INDETERMINATE
267	    # 4. real-negation net XOR over the bounded pre-scope, applied to the base antonym polarity.
268	    pre = _pre_scope(clause_lc, concept_pos, lex["termination_terms"], int(lex["scope_token_cap"]))
269	    status = base
270	    if _net_negation_flip(pre, concept_cue, lex["real_negation"]):
271	        status = ABSENT if base == PRESENT else PRESENT
272	    # 5. epistemic hedge (permissive already ruled out at step 1) -> INDETERMINATE.
273	    if _phrase_present(window, lex["hedge"]):
274	        return INDETERMINATE
275	    return status
