310	# Severity/normality qualifiers that DISCRIMINATE a condition scope. Adjacent to the organ/condition
311	# cue, they distinguish "renal impairment" / "severe renal" from "normal renal function" so condition-
312	# stratified statements do NOT collapse to the same key. Concept-polarity words (contraindicated/safe/
313	# avoid) are NEVER included — only the population/organ qualifier survives.
314	_PRE_QUALIFIERS = frozenset({"normal", "severe", "mild", "moderate", "preserved", "chronic", "acute",
315	                             "advanced", "significant", "end-stage", "endstage", "decompensated"})
316	_POST_QUALIFIERS = frozenset({"impairment", "insufficiency", "failure", "dysfunction", "disease"})
317	
318	
319	def _extract_condition_scope(sentence_lc: str, lex: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
320	    """Return a DISCRIMINATING condition-scope phrase = the organ/condition cue word plus an adjacent
321	    severity/normality qualifier ONLY (never the concept-polarity verb). 'renal impairment' and
322	    'normal renal function' stay distinct (the red-team condition-stratified false-positive), while
323	    identical conditions ('pregnancy' both sides) collapse to the same key (a real conflict)."""
324	    tokens = [re.sub(r"[^a-z\-]", "", w) for w in sentence_lc.split()]
325	    for i, tok in enumerate(tokens):
326	        if not tok:
327	            continue
328	        if any(cue in tok for cue in lex["condition_scope_cues"]):
329	            parts = [tok]
330	            if i > 0 and tokens[i - 1] in _PRE_QUALIFIERS:
331	                parts.insert(0, tokens[i - 1])
332	            if i + 1 < len(tokens) and tokens[i + 1] in _POST_QUALIFIERS:
333	                parts.append(tokens[i + 1])
334	            return " ".join(parts)[:48]
335	    return ""

338	# Population-NEGATION cues that flip a condition_scope from the (default) WITH-population
339	# polarity to WITHOUT-population. A cue counts ONLY when it sits INSIDE the population
340	# phrase (between a population introducer and the condition cue) — see the bounded
341	# back-scan in _extract_condition_polarity.
342	_POPULATION_NEGATION_CUES = frozenset({
343	    "without", "not", "no", "non", "absent", "absence", "free", "lacking", "lack",
344	    "lacks", "devoid", "sans", "minus", "negative",
345	})
346	# HARD clause boundaries: a coordinating/subordinating conjunction starts a NEW clause —
347	# nothing before it is part of THIS population phrase. Relative pronouns (who/that/which)
348	# are deliberately NOT here: they are part of the population description ("patients WHO HAVE
349	# renal", "those WITH renal"), so the scan stays transparent across them.
350	_POPULATION_CLAUSE_BOUNDARIES = frozenset({
351	    "and", "or", "but", "while", "whereas", "because", "since", "however",
352	    "though", "although", "nevertheless",
353	})
354	# A RELATIVE-CLAUSE marker inside the population description ('patients WHO HAVE renal') means
355	# the population carries an embedded restriction the token scan cannot fully parse. With no
356	# resolved negation/exclusion the polarity is therefore UNCERTAIN ⇒ fail-closed to a
357	# SINGLETON (never a guessed 'with'). Over-fragmentation is SAFE; a guessed 'with' on a
358	# hidden exclusion is the lethal over-merge (Codex Slice-B iter-4 P0).
359	_RELATIVE_CLAUSE_MARKERS = frozenset({
360	    "who", "whom", "whose", "that", "which", "where", "wherein", "whereby",
361	})
362	# UNAMBIGUOUS exclusion operators that INVERT the population ("excluding/except/other than/
363	# rather than/apart from those with renal" ⇒ the population WITHOUT renal). Deliberately
364	# conservative: a FALSE-positive flip of an affirmative "with X" to "without X" would ITSELF
365	# be a lethal over-merge (with a genuine "without X"), so only operators that reliably denote
366	# exclusion are listed; ambiguous ones ('besides'/'outside'/'exclusive') are NOT.
367	_EXCLUSION_SINGLE = frozenset({
368	    "excluding", "except", "exclude", "excludes", "excluded",
369	    "omitting", "omit", "omits", "omitted",
370	})
371	_EXCLUSION_BIGRAMS = frozenset({("other", "than"), ("rather", "than"), ("apart", "from")})
372	# POST-cue passive restriction verbs whose population-restriction sense is COMMON but not
373	# unambiguous (they have benign uses: "drug WITHDRAWN from market"). When one appears AFTER
374	# the condition cue in the population clause it MIGHT restrict the population, so it FAILS
375	# CLOSED to a singleton (never a guessed 'with') rather than risk a false 'without' (a false
376	# 'without' would itself over-merge with a genuine without-population — the lethal direction).
377	_POST_CUE_RESTRICTION_CUES = frozenset({
378	    "removed", "dropped", "withdrawn", "barred", "disqualified", "ineligible",
379	    "precluded", "prohibited", "censored",
380	})
381	# Exclusion-META nouns ("renal impairment AS AN EXCLUSION CRITERION" — eligibility
382	# meta-language, not a clean population statement). Presence anywhere in the clause ⇒
383	# FAIL CLOSED to a singleton (Codex Slice-B iter-7 P0). Bare "criterion/criteria" is NOT
384	# here (it is usually inclusion language); only the explicit exclusion noun.
385	_EXCLUSION_META_NOUNS = frozenset({"exclusion", "exclusions"})
386	# Population SEVERITY / STAGE / THRESHOLD modifier WORDS. `_extract_condition_scope` drops
387	# these (it keeps only the organ cue + a severity adjective), so "STAGE 3 renal impairment"
388	# and "STAGE 4 renal impairment" — or "eGFR BELOW 30" and "eGFR ABOVE 60" — both collapse to
389	# the same condition_scope and would over-merge DIFFERENT severity populations. Their presence
390	# ⇒ FAIL CLOSED to a singleton (Codex Slice-B final-close P0). Digits in the clause are also
391	# caught directly (covers bare "stage 3" / "eGFR 30" numeric thresholds).
392	_POPULATION_MODIFIER_CUES = frozenset({
393	    "stage", "stages", "grade", "grades", "class", "classes",
394	    "below", "above", "under", "over", "exceeding", "less", "greater",
395	    "fewer", "between", "threshold",
396	})
397	# Sentinel: a population is present but its polarity cannot be confidently resolved.
398	# build_merge_key (via _ambiguous_polarity) treats it as UNKNOWN ⇒ forces a singleton.
399	POLARITY_AMBIGUOUS = "ambiguous"
400	
401	
402	# All population-altering tokens. If ANY appears in the population clause, the polarity is
403	# NOT a provably-clean affirmative ⇒ fail closed (Codex Slice-B iter-2..iter-8 + final-close
404	# proved token heuristics cannot safely RESOLVE with/without/broadened/severity-stratified
405	# across many standard phrasings).
406	_POLARITY_COMPLEXITY_TOKENS = (
407	    _POPULATION_NEGATION_CUES | _EXCLUSION_SINGLE | _EXCLUSION_META_NOUNS
408	    | _RELATIVE_CLAUSE_MARKERS | _POST_CUE_RESTRICTION_CUES | _POPULATION_MODIFIER_CUES
409	)

412	def _extract_condition_polarity(sentence_lc: str, lex: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
413	    """Polarity of the population/condition qualifier (Wave-3 I-arch-001 #1245 P0).
414	
415	    MAXIMALLY FAIL-CLOSED (operator decision 2026-06-13 after Codex Slice-B iters 2..8 found
416	    9 standard English phrasings of an excluded/without/broadened population that token
417	    heuristics cannot safely RESOLVE). The only LETHAL error is reading a non-affirmative
418	    population as the mergeable affirmative 'with', so we never try to resolve 'without' —
419	    we only ever emit the mergeable 'with' for a PROVABLY-clean affirmative, and a SINGLETON
420	    sentinel for everything else.
421	
422	    Returns:
423	      * 'with' — a PROVABLY-clean affirmative population: a BARE condition cue (no hyphen, so
424	        no compound adjective like 'renal-sparing'/'non-renal') whose surrounding clause
425	        contains NO population-altering token (negation / exclusion operator or noun /
426	        relative pronoun / restriction verb / exclusion bigram). The ONLY mergeable verdict.
427	      * ``POLARITY_AMBIGUOUS`` — a population cue is present but is NOT a provably-clean
428	        affirmative (any complexity token, or a hyphenated/compound cue) ⇒ a SINGLETON
429	        (never merges, with anything — incl. another non-affirmative). This is what makes the
430	        merge key have NO over-merge tail: a without/excluded/broadened population can never
431	        share a key with the affirmative (or with each other) because it always singletons.
432	      * '' — NO population cue (an unstratified claim).
433	
434	    Cost (accepted per operator): without/excluded/broadened population claims do NOT
435	    consolidate (each a singleton); over-fragmentation is SAFE, over-merge is lethal. A
436	    richer LLM polarity classifier is the tracked follow-up. Tokenisation mirrors
437	    `_extract_condition_scope`. Dormant: read ONLY by `build_merge_key` under
438	    PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN; never serialized.
439	    """
440	    tokens = [re.sub(r"[^a-z\-]", "", w) for w in sentence_lc.split()]
441	    for i, tok in enumerate(tokens):
442	        if not tok:
443	            continue
444	        if any(cue in tok for cue in lex["condition_scope_cues"]):
445	            # a hyphenated / compound-adjective or prefixed cue token ('renal-sparing',
446	            # 'non-renal', 'renal-free') is NOT a bare affirmative condition ⇒ FAIL CLOSED.
447	            if "-" in tok:
448	                return POLARITY_AMBIGUOUS
449	            # delimit the population CLAUSE around the cue (both directions, to a hard clause
450	            # boundary) and fail closed on ANY population-altering token anywhere in it.
451	            lo = 0
452	            for j in range(i - 1, -1, -1):
453	                if tokens[j] in _POPULATION_CLAUSE_BOUNDARIES:
454	                    lo = j + 1
455	                    break
456	            hi = len(tokens)
457	            for j in range(i + 1, len(tokens)):
458	                if tokens[j] in _POPULATION_CLAUSE_BOUNDARIES:
459	                    hi = j
460	                    break
461	            clause = tokens[lo:hi]
462	            if any(t in _POLARITY_COMPLEXITY_TOKENS for t in clause):
463	                return POLARITY_AMBIGUOUS
464	            for k in range(len(clause) - 1):
465	                if (clause[k], clause[k + 1]) in _EXCLUSION_BIGRAMS:
466	                    return POLARITY_AMBIGUOUS
467	            # a DIGIT anywhere in the population clause (stage/grade number, eGFR threshold,
468	            # "type 2", a frequency) ⇒ the population is severity/threshold-stratified and the
469	            # bare condition_scope cannot capture it ⇒ FAIL CLOSED (Codex Slice-B final-close).
470	            if any(ch.isdigit() for w in sentence_lc.split()[lo:hi] for ch in w):
471	                return POLARITY_AMBIGUOUS
472	            return "with"
473	    return ""

476	def _classify_cue_bucket(cset: dict[str, Any], bucket_field: str, cue: str) -> str:
477	    """Return the ontology bucket name a matched PRESENT cue falls into, else '' (UNKNOWN).
478	
479	    `bucket_field` is the per-concept lexicon sub-map name (`causal_strength_cues` for ae_causation,
480	    `warning_severity_cues` for warning). The matched `cue` is the exact lexicon string, so an exact
481	    `cue in bucket_list` membership test is sufficient and deterministic. A concept_type that owns no
482	    such sub-map, or a cue not partitioned into any bucket, yields '' (UNKNOWN) — design §4.4 Wave 3.
483	    """
484	    buckets = cset.get(bucket_field)
485	    if not isinstance(buckets, dict):
486	        return ''
487	    for bucket_name, bucket_cues in buckets.items():
488	        if cue in (bucket_cues or []):
489	            return str(bucket_name)
490	    return ''

493	def extract_qualitative_assertions(
494	    evidence: list[dict[str, Any]], domain: str | None = None,
495	) -> list[QualitativeAssertion]:
496	    """Extract qualitative clinical-safety assertions from evidence rows. One row may yield several
497	    (one per concept occurrence per sentence). `no_assertion` pseudo cues skip the span entirely.
498	
499	    B9 domain-generalization (the spine): this detector's cue lexicon
500	    (`config/clinical_safety/qualitative_conflict_lexicon.yaml`) is CLINICAL —
501	    contraindication / drug-interaction / adverse-event present-vs-absent cues.
502	    Running it on a NON-clinical corpus produced the 2,738 junk
503	    contradiction baskets on the labor-displacement report (the clinical cue
504	    set fired on every topic). The `domain` param is now READ and gated through
505	    the deterministic `is_clinical_domain` backbone: on a non-clinical run the
506	    clinical lexicon does NOT apply, so we return no qualitative assertions and
507	    the (numeric/NLI) detectors + the span-grounding faithfulness gate remain
508	    the backstops. Clinical runs (`domain="clinical"`, or a blank domain over a
509	    clinically-signalled corpus) keep the FULL clinical behavior byte-identical.
510	    """
511	    from src.polaris_graph.domain.domain_signal import is_clinical_domain
512	    if not is_clinical_domain(domain, evidence):
513	        # Non-clinical corpus: the clinical present-vs-absent cue lexicon does
514	        # not apply. Skip clinical extraction entirely (no junk clinical
515	        # baskets); non-clinical claim consolidation + faithfulness run on the
516	        # numeric/atomic path. Never raises, never holds.
517	        return []
518	    lex = _load_lexicon()
519	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.contradiction_detector import _normalize_subject
520	    out: list[QualitativeAssertion] = []
521	    for ev in evidence or []:
522	        quote = ev.get("direct_quote") or ev.get("statement") or ""
523	        if not quote:
524	            continue
525	        evid = str(ev.get("evidence_id", ""))
526	        url = ev.get("source_url", "") or ev.get("url", "")
527	        tier = ev.get("tier", "") or ev.get("source_tier", "")
528	        for sentence in _split_sentences(quote):
529	            # the drug usually appears once at the head of the sentence; a clause that names no drug
530	            # inherits the sentence subject (so 'X is contraindicated ... but safe in ...' keeps X).
531	            # B13 (I-arch-011): ``general_fallback=True`` makes the SENTENCE subject domain-general —
532	            # a device / procedure / population entity (e.g. "dbs") is extracted when NO drug name is
533	            # present, instead of "". Drug names still win. Without this, a non-drug clinical corpus
534	            # (Parkinson / DBS safety) resolved EVERY qualitative subject to "" so unrelated safety
535	            # flags collapsed into one ("", concept) bucket — diluting real T1 contradictions into
536	            # noise. Advisory surface only (labels, never drops/holds); faithfulness gates untouched.
537	            sentence_subject = _normalize_subject(
538	                sentence, fallback="", general_fallback=True)
539	            for clause_raw in _split_clauses(sentence, lex["termination_terms"]):
540	                clause = clause_raw.lower()
541	                # true pseudo / no-assertion -> the concept is not asserted; skip this clause.
542	                if _phrase_present(clause, lex["no_assertion"]):
543	                    continue
544	                for concept_type, cset in lex["concept_types"].items():
545	                    present_hit = _find_longest_cue(clause, cset.get("present_cues", []))
546	                    absent_hit = _find_longest_cue(clause, cset.get("absent_cues", []))
547	                    hit, base = None, None
548	                    # prefer the longest matching cue across both polarities
549	                    if present_hit and absent_hit:
550	                        if len(absent_hit[0]) >= len(present_hit[0]):
551	                            hit, base = absent_hit, ABSENT
552	                        else:
553	                            hit, base = present_hit, PRESENT
554	                    elif present_hit:
555	                        hit, base = present_hit, PRESENT
556	                    elif absent_hit:
557	                        hit, base = absent_hit, ABSENT
558	                    if hit is None:
559	                        continue
560	                    cue, pos = hit
561	                    status = _classify_status(clause, cue, pos, base, lex)
562	                    # B13 (I-arch-011): clause-level subject stays DRUG-ONLY so a clause that
563	                    # names a drug binds to it, and a clause that loses its drug falls through to
564	                    # ``sentence_subject``. The domain-general fallback is applied ONCE at the
565	                    # SENTENCE level (above): on a non-drug corpus the sentence subject carries the
566	                    # device/procedure entity which every clause inherits. Applying the general
567	                    # fallback HERE too would let a per-clause filler noun ("constipation",
568	                    # "contraindicated") override the real sentence subject and break inheritance.
569	                    subject = _normalize_subject(clause, fallback="") or sentence_subject
570	                    owner = lex["object_slot_owner"].get(concept_type, "object_slot")
571	                    object_slot, condition_scope = "", ""
572	                    cscope = _extract_condition_scope(clause, lex)
573	                    # POPULATION polarity of the same condition cue (Wave-3 #1245 P0).
574	                    # Aligns with cscope: non-empty cscope <-> polarity in {with, without};
575	                    # empty cscope <-> ''. Dormant: read ONLY by build_merge_key under the flag.
576	                    condition_polarity = _extract_condition_polarity(clause, lex)
577	                    if owner == "condition_scope":
578	                        condition_scope = cscope
579	                    else:
580	                        object_slot = _extract_object_slot(
581	                            clause_raw, clause, concept_type, pos, cue, subject
582	                        )
583	                        condition_scope = cscope
584	                    # Intra-slot ontology discriminators (Wave 3 §4.4): classify the matched cue ONLY
585	                    # on a PRESENT hit (an ABSENT cue is not partitioned into the present buckets).
586	                    # '' (UNKNOWN) for every other concept_type and on every absent hit. Dormant until
587	                    # claim_graph.build_merge_key reads it under PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN.
588	                    causal_strength, warning_severity = '', ''
589	                    if base == PRESENT:
590	                        if concept_type == "ae_causation":
591	                            causal_strength = _classify_cue_bucket(
592	                                cset, "causal_strength_cues", cue)
593	                        elif concept_type == "warning":
594	                            warning_severity = _classify_cue_bucket(
595	                                cset, "warning_severity_cues", cue)
596	                    out.append(QualitativeAssertion(
597	                        evidence_id=evid, subject=subject, concept_type=concept_type,
598	                        object_slot=object_slot, condition_scope=condition_scope,
599	                        assertion_status=status, cue=cue, context_snippet=clause_raw[:200],
600	                        source_url=url, source_tier=tier,
601	                        causal_strength=causal_strength, warning_severity=warning_severity,
602	                        condition_polarity=condition_polarity,
603	                    ))
604	    return out

611	_NCT_RE = re.compile(r"\bNCT\d{8}\b", re.IGNORECASE)
612	_DOI_RE = re.compile(r"10\.\d{4,9}/[-._;()/:A-Za-z0-9]+", re.IGNORECASE)
613	_PMID_RE = re.compile(r"/pubmed/(\d+)|[?&]pmid=(\d+)|\bPMID[:\s]+(\d+)", re.IGNORECASE)
614

616	def _normalize_source_id(url: str) -> str:
617	    """Normalize a source to a stable identity (NCT / DOI / PMID / host+path), so the same source
618	    self-quoted in two sections counts as ONE source and cannot raise a cross-source conflict."""
619	    u = (url or "").strip().lower()
620	    if not u:
621	        return ""
622	    m = _NCT_RE.search(u)
623	    if m:
624	        return "nct:" + m.group(0).lower()
625	    m = _DOI_RE.search(u)
626	    if m:
627	        return "doi:" + m.group(0)
628	    m = _PMID_RE.search(u)
629	    if m:
630	        return "pmid:" + next(g for g in m.groups() if g)
631	    u = re.split(r"[?#]", u)[0].rstrip("/")
632	    return u

659	def _owner_value(a: QualitativeAssertion, owner_of: dict[str, str]) -> str:
660	    """The canonical discriminating slot value for this assertion's concept_type (Codex iter-2 P2.b)."""
661	    return a.object_slot if owner_of.get(a.concept_type, "object_slot") == "object_slot" else a.condition_scope

664	def _objects_disjoint(o1: str, o2: str) -> bool:
665	    """Two resolved object slots are 'determinably different' ONLY if both are non-empty and NEITHER
666	    contains the other — so 'an increased risk of pancreatitis' and 'pancreatitis' are NOT treated as
667	    different (same outcome, looser extraction), but 'nausea' and 'pancreatitis' ARE. An empty slot is
668	    unresolved → not determinably different → routes to review (never a silent drop)."""
669	    if not o1 or not o2:
670	        return False
671	    return o1 != o2 and o1 not in o2 and o2 not in o1

674	def detect_qualitative_conflicts(
675	    assertions: list[QualitativeAssertion],
676	) -> list[QualitativeConflictRecord]:
677	    """Two-pass: exact-key hard conflicts (Pass A) + coarse-key review flags (Pass B)."""
678	    owner_of = _load_lexicon()["object_slot_owner"]
679	    records: list[QualitativeConflictRecord] = []
680	    full: dict[tuple[str, str, str, str], list[QualitativeAssertion]] = {}
681	    coarse: dict[tuple[str, str], list[QualitativeAssertion]] = {}
682	    for a in assertions:
683	        if a.subject:
684	            full.setdefault((a.subject, a.concept_type, a.object_slot, a.condition_scope), []).append(a)
685	        coarse.setdefault((a.subject, a.concept_type), []).append(a)
686	
687	    # ── PASS A: hard conflicts on the FULL key — ONLY when the owner slot is RESOLVED ────────────
688	    # (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1.1: an unresolved/empty owner key must NOT hard-fire; it routes to
689	    # Pass B review.) The hard-conflict identity is keyed by the FULL key + the exact source pair
690	    # (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1.2), so a hard conflict at one scope does NOT suppress review flags
691	    # for a DIFFERENT object_slot/condition_scope between the same sources.
692	    hard_keys: set[tuple] = set()  # (subject, concept_type, object_slot, condition_scope, src_a, src_b)
693	    for (subject, concept_type, object_slot, condition_scope), group in full.items():
694	        owner_val = object_slot if owner_of.get(concept_type, "object_slot") == "object_slot" else condition_scope
695	        if not owner_val:
696	            continue  # unresolved owner -> defer to Pass B review, never a hard conflict
697	        present = [a for a in group if a.assertion_status == PRESENT]
698	        absent = [a for a in group if a.assertion_status == ABSENT]
699	        if not present or not absent:
700	            continue
701	        src_present, src_absent = _distinct_sources(present), _distinct_sources(absent)
702	        if not src_present or not src_absent:
703	            continue  # need resolvable sources on both sides
704	        if not any(sp != sa for sp in src_present for sa in src_absent):
705	            continue  # all present/absent from the same single source -> not cross-source
706	        records.append(QualitativeConflictRecord(
707	            predicate=_predicate_label(concept_type, object_slot, condition_scope),
708	            claims=[_claim_dict(a) for a in present + absent], subject=subject, severity="high",
709	            conflict_reason=(
710	                f"{concept_type} asserted PRESENT and ABSENT across "
711	                f"{len(src_present | src_absent)} distinct sources at the same "
712	                f"{owner_of.get(concept_type, 'object')}='{owner_val}'."
713	            ),
714	        ))
715	        for sp in present:
716	            for sa in absent:
717	                pair = tuple(sorted((_normalize_source_id(sp.source_url),
718	                                     _normalize_source_id(sa.source_url))))
719	                hard_keys.add((subject, concept_type, object_slot, condition_scope, pair[0], pair[1]))
720	
721	    # ── PASS B: review flags on the COARSE key (Codex iter-1 P1.2 + iter-2 P1.b/P2.a) ───────────
722	    for (subject, concept_type), group in coarse.items():
723	        definite = [a for a in group if a.assertion_status in _DEFINITE]
724	        soft = [a for a in group if a.assertion_status not in _DEFINITE]
725	        for i in range(len(definite)):
726	            for j in range(i + 1, len(definite)):
727	                ai, aj = definite[i], definite[j]
728	                if ai.assertion_status == aj.assertion_status:
729	                    continue
730	                sid_i, sid_j = _normalize_source_id(ai.source_url), _normalize_source_id(aj.source_url)
731	                if not sid_i or not sid_j or sid_i == sid_j:
732	                    continue  # same/unresolved source: not a cross-source disagreement
733	                # Suppress ONLY the EXACT pair already emitted as a hard conflict (same full key +
734	                # same source pair) — a hard conflict at a DIFFERENT scope/object does not suppress.
735	                if ai.object_slot == aj.object_slot and ai.condition_scope == aj.condition_scope:
736	                    pair = tuple(sorted((sid_i, sid_j)))
737	                    if (subject, concept_type, ai.object_slot, ai.condition_scope, pair[0], pair[1]) in hard_keys:
738	                        continue
739	                # determinable non-conflict ONLY if both object_slots resolved + clearly different
740	                if _objects_disjoint(ai.object_slot, aj.object_slot):
741	                    continue
742	                records.append(_review_record(
743	                    subject, concept_type, [ai, aj],
744	                    "object_slot or condition_scope differs/missing — review (not auto-fired)",
745	                ))
746	        # definite opposed by an INDETERMINATE / STATISTICAL_NULL across distinct sources -> review
747	        for d in definite:
748	            for s in soft:
749	                sid_d, sid_s = _normalize_source_id(d.source_url), _normalize_source_id(s.source_url)
750	                if not sid_d or not sid_s or sid_d == sid_s:
751	                    continue
752	                # determinable-different outcomes are unrelated -> no review noise (Codex diff-gate
753	                # iter-4 P2; same containment-aware guard as the definite-vs-definite path).
754	                if _objects_disjoint(d.object_slot, s.object_slot):
755	                    continue
756	                records.append(_review_record(
757	                    subject, concept_type, [d, s],
758	                    f"definite {d.assertion_status} vs {s.assertion_status} across sources — review",
759	                ))
760	
761	    rank = {"high": 0, "medium": 1, "review": 2}
762	    records.sort(key=lambda r: (rank.get(r.severity, 3), r.predicate))
763	    return records

766	def _review_record(subject: str, concept_type: str, pair: list[QualitativeAssertion],
767	                   reason: str) -> QualitativeConflictRecord:
768	    objs = [a.object_slot for a in pair if a.object_slot]
769	    scopes = [a.condition_scope for a in pair if a.condition_scope]
770	    qual = (objs or scopes or [""])[0]
771	    return QualitativeConflictRecord(
772	        predicate=_predicate_label(concept_type, objs[0] if objs else "", scopes[0] if scopes else ""),
773	        claims=[_claim_dict(a) for a in pair], subject=subject, severity="review",
774	        conflict_reason=reason,
775	        recommended_action="Indeterminate qualitative disagreement — route to human review; do not treat as resolved.",
776	    )

1	"""Phase C — REAL enforcement of quality + topicality as CITABLE-ELIGIBILITY.
2	
3	This module is the executable core the review (Sol §4 "Make ``high_quality`` real",
4	"Make topicality real"; GATE_REVIEW_VERDICT Phase C item 4) demands. It turns two
5	previously-decorative contract dimensions into deterministic, domain-neutral,
6	POST-FETCH eligibility verdicts over metadata that live retrieval ALREADY fetched:
7	
8	  * ``scope.source_quality`` (e.g. hard "only high-quality / peer-reviewed") ->
9	    a domain-neutral quality profile scored over ``tier`` / ``is_peer_reviewed`` /
10	    ``openalex_venue`` / retraction / degraded-shell flags / OJS-mill host
11	    heuristics. "journal-shaped" != peer-reviewed; a conference proceeding is not a
12	    journal unless the contract allows it. Unknown metadata under a HARD
13	    "only high-quality" fails CLOSED for citable eligibility (disclosed), never
14	    silently relaxed. A SOFT preference reorders only (emits a weight, no drop).
15	
16	  * topicality -> the fetched body (``statement`` + ``direct_quote``) is scored
17	    against the CLEAN objective + owning research thread (NOT the instruction-laden
18	    prompt). Confirmed off-topic (below floor) is quarantined from the citable
19	    menu; uncertain stays and is down-ranked/marked.
20	
21	CRITICAL — this is entirely UPSTREAM of the frozen verifier. It decides which
22	evidence rows are ELIGIBLE to enter the citable menu (``evidence_for_gen``) BEFORE
23	``strict_verify`` ever enumerates the pool. It NEVER changes HOW a claim is
24	verified or cited. ``provenance_generator.py`` / ``strict_verify`` stay 0-diff. A
25	row failing a HARD predicate is REMOVED from the citable menu here but KEPT in the
26	corpus + disclosure (§-1.3 disclose-don't-delete), exactly mirroring the existing
27	scope-hard grounding mask.
28	
29	Everything is pure data: no network, no LLM, no I/O. The whole module no-ops when
30	its inputs carry no hard quality/topicality predicate (byte-identical). It is only
31	reached under ``PG_GATE`` at the citable-menu seam.
32	"""

178	def _has_doi_or_journal_credential(row: Any, url: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
179	    """FIX 5(b): POSITIVE-evidence second-chance signal for an otherwise-UNKNOWN row.
180	
181	    True iff the deterministic document-genre CLASSIFIER resolves the row to a
182	    peer-reviewed journal/review article
183	    (``is_peer_reviewed_journal_article(classify_document_type(...)[0]) == True``).
184	    A bare DOI is NOT sufficient on its own: the DOI is an INPUT to the classifier
185	    (it helps genre resolution) but a positive T1-scholarly verdict comes ONLY from the
186	    classifier's peer-reviewed-journal judgement. Preprint (arXiv 10.48550), dataset
187	    (Zenodo 10.5281), working-paper (NBER 10.3386, SSRN 10.2139), and bare/content-shell
188	    DOIs classify to a NON-journal genre and therefore return ``(False, ...)`` here.
189	
190	    Reuses the SAME predicate the scope-facet classifier already trusts — single source of
191	    truth, no duplicate journal logic. Pure / offline / fail-open: any classifier fault yields
192	    ``(False, ...)`` so a scoring error never RESCUES a row (it can only leave it UNKNOWN).
193	
194	    This is consulted ONLY after every FAIL path has already returned, so it can never
195	    re-admit a retracted / predatory / non-peer-reviewed / low-tier source."""
196	    # genre: the peer-reviewed-journal predicate the scope classifier trusts. The DOI feeds
197	    # the classifier as one input; it is NOT a standalone credential (a bare/preprint/dataset
198	    # DOI resolves to a non-journal genre -> not-T1 -> the row stays UNKNOWN).
199	    try:
200	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.document_type_classifier import (
201	            classify_document_type,
202	            is_peer_reviewed_journal_article,
203	        )
204	        dt, _basis = classify_document_type(
205	            openalex_publication_type=str(_row_get(row, "openalex_publication_type", "") or ""),
206	            openalex_source_type=str(_row_get(row, "openalex_source_type", "") or ""),
207	            openalex_is_peer_reviewed=_row_get(row, "openalex_is_peer_reviewed", None),
208	            source_class=str(_row_get(row, "source_class", "") or ""),
209	            url=url,
210	            title=str(_row_get(row, "title", "") or ""),
211	            doi=str(_row_get(row, "doi", "") or ""),
212	        )
213	        if is_peer_reviewed_journal_article(dt):
214	            return True, f"peer-reviewed journal article ({dt.value})"
215	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open: no rescue on error
216	        pass
217	    return False, ""

221	# GENERALIZED Fix 5(b): signal -> (tier, kind) REGISTRY (journal => PASS is ONE
222	# T1 instance, not the mechanism). Consulted where the journal "4.5 second-chance"
223	# used to sit — AFTER every FAIL return, BEFORE the UNKNOWN fail-closed. The table
224	# is DATA (kind tokens), never control-flow literals: the SHAPE (signal->tier->∈allowed)
225	# is the deliverable; adding a kind later must be a row here, not a branch.
226	#
227	#   T1 = authoritative-universal (validated peer-reviewed scholarly OR official
228	#        gov / primary / statute / authenticated filing) -> PASS UNCONDITIONALLY
229	#        for any contract. "journal => PASS" is the T1-scholarly row, nothing more.
230	#   T2 = reputable newswire / authenticated IR / established analyst (author+method)
231	#        -> PASS iff its kind ∈ allowed_kinds and ∉ excluded_kinds.
232	#   T3 = issuer-primary (press release / corporate) -> PASS iff kind ∈ allowed and ∉ excluded.
233	#   unrated / no positive signal -> return None; the existing UNKNOWN path is unchanged.
234	#
235	# DOI-ALONE IS NOT T1: the T1-scholarly predicate REUSES ``_has_doi_or_journal_credential``,
236	# which is CLASSIFIER-CONFIRMED — it returns True only when the genre classifier resolves the
237	# row to a peer-reviewed journal/review article (article/review + journal source-type or a
238	# known peer-reviewed registrant/host). A bare, preprint (arXiv), dataset (Zenodo),
239	# working-paper (NBER/SSRN), predatory, or content-shell DOI classifies to a non-journal
240	# genre and does NOT PASS — the DOI is only an INPUT to the classifier, never a credential.
241	_TIER_T1 = "T1"
242	_TIER_T2 = "T2"
243	_TIER_T3 = "T3"

246	# conservative host-suffix set — a match is a strong POSITIVE authority signal.
247	_GOV_PRIMARY_HOST_SUFFIXES: tuple[str, ...] = (
248	    ".gov", ".gov.uk", ".gouv.fr", ".gc.ca", ".gov.au", ".govt.nz", ".gov.in",
249	    ".europa.eu", ".un.org", ".who.int", ".imf.org", ".worldbank.org", ".oecd.org",

272	def _positive_signal_tier(row: Any, url: str) -> "tuple[str, str] | None":
273	    """Map a row's POSITIVE credentials to ``(tier, kind)`` or ``None``.
274	
275	    First match wins with a recorded basis (the caller stringifies the kind). Pure /
276	    offline / fail-open: any fault yields ``None`` (never a rescue on error). This is
277	    consulted ONLY after every FAIL return, so it can never re-admit a retracted /
278	    predatory / non-peer-reviewed / low-tier source (INV-3 evidence-positive only).
279	
280	    Over-engineering guard (§3.2): ≤4 predicates over already-fetched fields, no
281	    credibility ontology. T2/T3 host predicates (reputable newsroom / issuer-primary)
282	    are a labelled TODO — the SHAPE ships now; a new kind is a row, not a branch.
283	    """
284	    # T1-scholarly: the SAME predicate the scope classifier already trusts, CLASSIFIER-
285	    # CONFIRMED. NOT "DOI alone" — a bare/preprint/dataset DOI classifies to a non-journal
286	    # genre and does NOT earn T1 (it stays UNKNOWN); only a positive peer-reviewed-journal
287	    # verdict promotes (M8).
288	    try:
289	        ok, _basis = _has_doi_or_journal_credential(row, url)
290	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open: a fault never rescues a row
291	        ok = False
292	    if ok:
293	        return (_TIER_T1, "peer_reviewed_journal")
294	    # T1-government / primary authority (DOI-independent, universal).
295	    if _official_gov_host(url):
296	        return (_TIER_T1, "government")
297	    # TODO(v2, data-not-branch): T2 reputable-newsroom (author+method) -> ("T2","news")
298	    # and T3 issuer-primary -> ("T3","press_release"/"corporate"), gated on kind∈allowed.
299	    # Deferred per §3.2 until the host predicates are cheaply available from the
300	    # existing facet/tier classifier — adding each is ONE row here, never a new branch.
301	    return None

305	# GENERALIZED Fix 5(d): row -> normalized source-KIND token (for kind-match
306	# ordering + adequacy). REUSES the existing document-genre classifier — DATA, not
307	# a new ontology. Maps a ``DocumentType`` genre to the free-text kind vocabulary
308	# the contract's ``allowed_source_kinds`` uses ("journal" / "news" / "government"
309	# / "press release" / ...). Pure / offline / fail-open (UNKNOWN on any fault).
310	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
311	
312	# DocumentType.value -> canonical kind token. Extend as DATA; never a control literal.
313	_DOCTYPE_TO_KIND: dict[str, str] = {
314	    "JOURNAL_ARTICLE": "journal",
315	    "REVIEW_ARTICLE": "journal",
316	    "PREPRINT": "preprint",
317	    "CONFERENCE_PAPER": "conference",
318	    "WORKING_PAPER": "working_paper",
319	    "BOOK": "book",
320	    "REPORT": "report",
321	    "NEWS": "news",
322	    "PRESS_RELEASE": "press_release",
323	    "BLOG_COMMENTARY": "blog",
324	    "ENCYCLOPEDIA": "encyclopedia",
325	    "DATASET": "dataset",
326	    "UGC": "ugc",
327	    "PREDATORY_OA_JOURNAL": "predatory",
328	    "UNKNOWN": "",
329	}
330	
331	# Normalize a free-text allowed/excluded kind token (contract vocabulary) to the
332	# canonical set above so "peer-reviewed journals" and "journal articles" both match
333	# the "journal" genre. DATA table; the anti-hardcode grep excludes this module's
334	# registries. Keys are substrings tested against the lowercased contract token.
335	_KIND_SYNONYM_SUBSTR: tuple[tuple[str, str], ...] = (
336	    ("journal", "journal"), ("peer", "journal"), ("scholarly", "journal"),
337	    ("academic", "journal"), ("news", "news"), ("wire", "news"),
338	    ("press release", "press_release"), ("press-release", "press_release"),
339	    ("gov", "government"), ("government", "government"), ("official", "government"),
340	    ("statute", "government"), ("regulat", "government"),
341	    ("blog", "blog"), ("report", "report"), ("white paper", "report"),
342	    ("whitepaper", "report"), ("book", "book"), ("preprint", "preprint"),
343	    ("dataset", "dataset"), ("conference", "conference"), ("proceeding", "conference"),
344	)
345	
346	
347	def normalize_kind_token(token: str) -> str:
348	    """Canonicalize a free-text contract source-kind token to the genre vocabulary."""
349	    t = str(token or "").strip().lower()
350	    if not t:
351	        return ""
352	    for sub, canon in _KIND_SYNONYM_SUBSTR:
353	        if sub in t:
354	            return canon
355	    return t  # unknown token passes through verbatim (still matchable exactly)
356	
357	
358	def normalize_kinds(tokens: "Any") -> frozenset[str]:
359	    """Canonicalize an iterable of contract kind tokens (empty => empty frozenset)."""
360	    out: set[str] = set()
361	    for tok in (tokens or []):
362	        canon = normalize_kind_token(str(tok))
363	        if canon:
364	            out.add(canon)
365	    return frozenset(out)

374	    of a row, NEVER a DOI/journal shortcut."""

394	def score_source_quality(
395	    row: Any,
396	    *,
397	    allowed_kinds: frozenset[str] = frozenset(),
398	    excluded_kinds: frozenset[str] = frozenset(),
399	) -> tuple[str, float, str]:
400	    """Deterministic, domain-neutral quality verdict for one fetched row.
401	
402	    Returns ``(verdict, weight, basis)``:
403	      * ``verdict`` in {PASS, FAIL, UNKNOWN} for a HARD "only high-quality" predicate.
404	        PASS = positively-evidenced high quality; FAIL = positively-evidenced low
405	        quality (retracted / predatory host / low tier / non-peer-reviewed shell);
406	        UNKNOWN = no usable quality metadata (fail-CLOSED at the hard seam).
407	      * ``weight`` in (0, 1] — the SOFT-preference demote (never 0; order-not-drop).
408	      * ``basis`` — the rule + evidence that fired (for the receipt).
409	
410	    "journal-shaped" is NOT peer-reviewed: an ``is_peer_reviewed`` flag that is
411	    explicitly False (fetched, not merely absent) is a FAIL even if the venue looks
412	    journal-like. A retraction is always a FAIL. Absent metadata is UNKNOWN.
413	
414	    ``allowed_kinds`` / ``excluded_kinds`` (both default EMPTY => byte-identical to
415	    every existing caller) carry the contract's normalized source-kind policy so the
416	    GENERALIZED signal->tier resolver can PASS a T2/T3 credential iff its kind is
417	    contract-allowed (a T1 credential PASSes unconditionally). NO LLM here.
418	    """
419	    # 1) retraction — always disqualifying, highest precedence.
420	    if bool(_row_get(row, "is_retracted", False)):
421	        return FAIL, 0.05, "retracted (is_retracted=True)"
422	
423	    url = _row_url(row)
424	    # 2) predatory / OJS-mill host — journal-shaped but not peer-reviewed.
425	    if _predatory_host(url):
426	        return FAIL, 0.1, f"predatory/OJS-mill host heuristic matched ({url})"
427	
428	    tier = str(_row_get(row, "tier", "") or "").strip().upper()
429	    pr = _row_get(row, "is_peer_reviewed", None)  # None = not fetched (absent)
430	
431	    # 3) explicit peer-review evidence.
432	    if pr is True and tier in _HIGH_QUALITY_TIERS:
433	        return PASS, 1.0, f"peer_reviewed=True AND tier={tier} (high-quality band)"
434	    if pr is False:
435	        # journal-SHAPED but explicitly NOT peer-reviewed -> not high quality.
436	        return FAIL, 0.2, f"is_peer_reviewed=False (journal-shaped != peer-reviewed); tier={tier or 'UNKNOWN'}"
437	
438	    # 4) tier-only evidence (peer-review flag absent).
439	    if tier in _HIGH_QUALITY_TIERS:
440	        return PASS, 1.0, f"tier={tier} (high-quality band; peer_reviewed flag absent)"
441	    if tier in _LOW_QUALITY_TIERS:
442	        return FAIL, 0.3, f"tier={tier} (low-quality band: industry/news/blog/stub)"
443	
444	    # 4.5) GENERALIZED Fix 5(b): DETERMINISTIC signal->(tier,kind) RESOLVER before the
445	    # UNKNOWN fail-closed. EVERY FAIL path (retraction / predatory host / is_peer_reviewed=False /
446	    # low tier) has already returned above, and so has every positive PASS — so a row reaching
447	    # here has NO negative evidence. If a positive credential resolves to a tier, that is POSITIVE
448	    # evidence of quality — PASS rather than fail-close it out (the 41%-UNKNOWN over-mask).
449	    # EVIDENCE-POSITIVE ONLY (INV-3): this block contains ONLY ``return PASS``; it can NEVER
450	    # re-admit a FAIL row (they returned above), only rescue UNKNOWN rows with a positive signal.
451	    #   T1 (authoritative-universal: peer-reviewed scholarly OR gov/primary) -> PASS for ANY contract.
452	    #   T2/T3 -> PASS iff the credential's kind ∈ allowed_kinds and ∉ excluded_kinds (contract wants it).
453	    # "journal => PASS" is JUST the T1-scholarly row. DOI-alone is NOT T1 (the predicate rejects preprints).
454	    _sig = _positive_signal_tier(row, url)
455	    if _sig is not None:
456	        _sig_tier, _sig_kind = _sig
457	        if _sig_tier == _TIER_T1:
458	            return PASS, 1.0, f"signal->tier resolver: {_sig_tier} kind={_sig_kind} (authoritative-universal, evidence-positive)"
459	        if _sig_kind in allowed_kinds and _sig_kind not in excluded_kinds:
460	            return PASS, 1.0, f"signal->tier resolver: {_sig_tier} kind={_sig_kind} ∈ allowed (contract wants this kind, evidence-positive)"
461	        # T2/T3 whose kind is not contract-allowed falls through to the existing UNKNOWN path.
462	
463	    # 5) content-shell degradation is a quality-of-content negative but NOT a hard
464	    #    quality FAIL on its own — it is UNKNOWN (fail-closed under hard) with a demote.
465	    if (bool(_row_get(row, "content_starved", False))
466	            or bool(_row_get(row, "landing_page", False))
467	            or bool(_row_get(row, "citation_metadata_shell", False))):
468	        return UNKNOWN, 0.4, "content shell/starved (no quality metadata; fail-closed under hard)"
469	
470	    # 6) no usable quality metadata at all -> UNKNOWN (fail-CLOSED under a hard
471	    #    "only high-quality"; a soft preference keeps it at neutral weight).
472	    return UNKNOWN, 0.6, f"no quality metadata (tier={tier or 'UNKNOWN'}, peer_reviewed absent) — fail-closed under hard"
473

480	def build_quality_eligibility(
481	    policy: Any,
482	    evidence_rows: "list[Any] | None",
483	    *,
484	    quality_term_id: str = "scope.source_quality",
485	) -> EligibilityPlan:
486	    """Apply the contract's quality predicate to a billed candidate set.
487	
488	    ``policy`` is a :class:`RetrievalPolicy` (duck-typed: reads ``quality_profile``,
489	    ``predicate_force['quality_profile']``, ``contract_hash``). When no quality
490	    profile is set, returns an EMPTY plan (no-op, byte-identical).
491	
492	    HARD "only high-quality": a row scoring FAIL or UNKNOWN is added to
493	    ``eligibility_excluded_ids`` (fail-closed on UNKNOWN — never silently relaxed)
494	    and carries a FAIL/UNKNOWN receipt; a PASS row carries a pass receipt. SOFT
495	    preference: NO exclusion — only a demote weight in ``url_to_quality_weight`` and
496	    a receipt, so ranking prefers high quality without starving the menu.
497	    """
498	    plan = EligibilityPlan()
499	    quality_profile = getattr(policy, "quality_profile", None)
500	    if not _is_high_quality_request(quality_profile):
501	        return plan  # no hard/soft quality predicate -> byte-identical no-op
502	
503	    force = (getattr(policy, "predicate_force", {}) or {}).get("quality_profile", "soft")
504	    is_hard = str(force).strip().lower() == "hard"
505	    contract_hash = str(getattr(policy, "contract_hash", "") or "")
506	
507	    # GENERALIZED Fix 5(b): thread the contract's normalized source-kind policy so the
508	    # signal->tier resolver can PASS a contract-allowed T2/T3 credential (T1 is unconditional).
509	    # Empty (no source-kind clause) => byte-identical to the pre-generalization behavior.
510	    _allowed_kinds = normalize_kinds(getattr(policy, "allowed_source_kinds", None))
511	    _excluded_kinds = normalize_kinds(getattr(policy, "excluded_source_kinds", None))
512	
513	    for row in list(evidence_rows or []):
514	        url = _row_url(row)
515	        if not url:
516	            continue
517	        verdict, weight, basis = score_source_quality(
518	            row, allowed_kinds=_allowed_kinds, excluded_kinds=_excluded_kinds,
519	        )
520	        plan.receipts.append(SourceReceipt(
521	            contract_hash=contract_hash,
522	            term_id=quality_term_id,
523	            source_id=url,
524	            stage=STAGE_QUALITY,
525	            verdict=verdict,
526	            basis=basis,
527	        ))
528	        if is_hard:
529	            # HARD: FAIL and UNKNOWN are both out of the citable menu (fail-closed).
530	            if verdict in (FAIL, UNKNOWN):
531	                plan.eligibility_excluded_ids.add(url)
532	                plan.excluded_records.append({
533	                    "source_id": url, "stage": STAGE_QUALITY,
534	                    "verdict": verdict, "basis": basis, "force": "hard",
535	                })
536	        else:
537	            # SOFT: order-not-drop. A FAIL/UNKNOWN demotes; never excluded.
538	            if weight < 1.0:
539	                plan.url_to_quality_weight[url] = weight
540	    return plan
541

543	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
544	# GENERALIZED Fix 5 — source-KIND eligibility + corpus adequacy + acquisition
545	# receipt (§3.5). Replaces the RETIRED ``journal_only_filter.py`` hard mask: a hard
546	# kind restriction NEVER masks a frozen corpus — it arms a real nonmatching-mask
547	# ONLY behind corpus-adequacy AND a matching acquisition receipt; otherwise it
548	# degrades to a visible PREFERENCE (reorder) plus a disclosure line. This is the
549	# C2-safe replacement for the fail-closed corpus filter.
550	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
551	
552	# Default in-scope-KIND adequacy floor (distinct usable in-scope rows). A hard kind
553	# restriction may arm a mask only when the corpus is provably adequate at/above this.
554	DEFAULT_SOURCE_KIND_MIN_ADEQUATE = 25
555	
556	
557	def corpus_kind_adequacy(
558	    rows: "list[Any] | None",
559	    allowed_kinds: frozenset[str],
560	    *,
561	    min_rows: int = DEFAULT_SOURCE_KIND_MIN_ADEQUATE,
562	) -> tuple[bool, int]:
563	    """Count DISTINCT usable in-scope-KIND rows and test the adequacy floor.
564	
565	    Adequacy counts the KIND of a row (``classified_kind``), NEVER a DOI/journal
566	    shortcut (§3 3/3). A row counts iff its kind ∈ ``allowed_kinds`` AND it does not
567	    score a quality FAIL (a predatory/retracted in-scope row is not usable evidence).
568	    Returns ``(adequate, n)`` where ``adequate = n >= min_rows``. Empty allowed => not
569	    adequate at 0 (the caller never arms a mask without an allowed set)."""
570	    if not allowed_kinds:
571	        return (False, 0)
572	    seen: set[str] = set()
573	    for r in list(rows or []):
574	        if classified_kind(r) not in allowed_kinds:
575	            continue
576	        if score_source_quality(r, allowed_kinds=allowed_kinds)[0] == FAIL:
577	            continue
578	        url = _row_url(r)
579	        if url:
580	            seen.add(url)
581	    n = len(seen)
582	    return (n >= min_rows, n)
583	
584	
585	def _acquisition_receipt_matches(policy: Any, acquisition_receipt: "Any") -> bool:
586	    """True iff the corpus was fetched UNDER this contract's kind lanes (Codex receipt).
587	
588	    The strongest available C2 guard against the 997->131 frozen-corpus replay: a
589	    hard kind mask arms only when the acquisition receipt's ``contract_hash`` matches
590	    the policy's ``contract_hash``. A frozen/replayed unscoped corpus (no receipt, or
591	    a mismatched hash) therefore NEVER arms the mask — it degrades to prefer+disclose.
592	    Lightweight: a single hash equality on the already-stamped receipt."""
593	    want = str(getattr(policy, "contract_hash", "") or "").strip()
594	    if not want:
595	        return False
596	    got = ""
597	    if isinstance(acquisition_receipt, Mapping):
598	        got = str(acquisition_receipt.get("contract_hash") or "").strip()
599	    else:
600	        got = str(getattr(acquisition_receipt, "contract_hash", "") or "").strip()
601	    return bool(got) and got == want
602	
603	
604	@dataclass
605	class SourceKindPlan:
606	    """Outcome of source-KIND eligibility over one billed candidate set.
607	
608	    ``armed`` True iff a HARD nonmatching-mask fired (adequate AND receipt-matched):
609	    ``eligibility_excluded_ids`` then masks out-of-scope-kind rows from the citable
610	    menu. When NOT armed, ``eligibility_excluded_ids`` is EMPTY and the caller applies
611	    only the kind-match REORDER (prefer) plus ``disclosure`` (the degrade line)."""
612	
613	    armed: bool = False
614	    eligibility_excluded_ids: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)
615	    receipts: list[SourceReceipt] = field(default_factory=list)
616	    excluded_records: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
617	    disclosure: str = ""
618	    in_scope_count: int = 0
619	
620	
621	def build_source_kind_eligibility(
622	    policy: Any,
623	    rows: "list[Any] | None",
624	    acquisition_receipt: "Any" = None,
625	    *,
626	    min_rows: int = DEFAULT_SOURCE_KIND_MIN_ADEQUATE,
627	    hard_enabled: bool = False,
628	    source_kind_term_id: str = "scope.source_types",
629	) -> SourceKindPlan:
630	    """Source-KIND eligibility (C2-safe replacement for the retired journal-only mask).
631	
632	    ORDER OF PRECEDENCE (§3.5):
633	      1. EXCLUSIONS ALWAYS WIN FIRST — every excluded-kind row is masked, monotonically;
634	         a PASS/weight can NEVER re-include an excluded kind. Exclusions are never
635	         adequacy-checked and never receipt-gated.
636	      2. HARD allowed_source_kinds arms a nonmatching-mask ONLY when ALL of:
637	         ``hard_enabled`` (PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_HARD) AND the term force is hard AND
638	         the corpus is adequate (``corpus_kind_adequacy`` >= ``min_rows``) AND the
639	         acquisition receipt matches. Otherwise it DOWNGRADES to prefer (no exclusion)
640	         and records an Assumption + SourceReceipt basis + a disclosure line.
641	      3. Empty allowed AND empty excluded => EMPTY plan (byte-identical no-op).
642	
643	    Counts in-scope-KIND rows, never DOI/journal rows. All upstream of the frozen verifier."""
644	    plan = SourceKindPlan()
645	    allowed = normalize_kinds(getattr(policy, "allowed_source_kinds", None))
646	    excluded = normalize_kinds(getattr(policy, "excluded_source_kinds", None))
647	    contract_hash = str(getattr(policy, "contract_hash", "") or "")
648	    row_list = list(rows or [])
649	    if not allowed and not excluded:
650	        return plan  # no source-kind clause -> byte-identical no-op
651	
652	    # (1) EXCLUSIONS ALWAYS WIN — monotonic union, never re-included, never gated.
653	    for r in row_list:
654	        url = _row_url(r)
655	        if not url:
656	            continue
657	        k = classified_kind(r)
658	        if k and k in excluded:
659	            plan.eligibility_excluded_ids.add(url)
660	            plan.excluded_records.append({
661	                "source_id": url, "stage": STAGE_QUALITY,
662	                "verdict": FAIL, "basis": f"source-kind exclusion: kind={k} ∈ excluded (always wins)",
663	                "force": "hard",
664	            })
665	            plan.receipts.append(SourceReceipt(
666	                contract_hash=contract_hash, term_id="scope.excluded_source_kinds",
667	                source_id=url, stage=STAGE_QUALITY, verdict=FAIL,
668	                basis=f"excluded source-kind {k} masked (exclusion always wins)",
669	            ))
670	
671	    if not allowed:
672	        return plan  # exclusion-only contract: mask done, no allow-mask to arm
673	
674	    # (2) HARD allowed-kind nonmatching-mask — arms ONLY behind adequacy + receipt.
675	    force = (getattr(policy, "predicate_force", {}) or {}).get("allowed_source_kinds", "soft")
676	    term_is_hard = str(force).strip().lower() == "hard"
677	    adequate, n = corpus_kind_adequacy(row_list, allowed, min_rows=min_rows)
678	    plan.in_scope_count = n
679	    receipt_ok = _acquisition_receipt_matches(policy, acquisition_receipt)
680	    arm_hard = bool(hard_enabled and term_is_hard and adequate and receipt_ok)
681	
682	    if arm_hard:
683	        plan.armed = True
684	        for r in row_list:
685	            url = _row_url(r)
686	            if not url or url in plan.eligibility_excluded_ids:
687	                continue
688	            k = classified_kind(r)
689	            if k not in allowed:
690	                plan.eligibility_excluded_ids.add(url)
691	                plan.excluded_records.append({
692	                    "source_id": url, "stage": STAGE_QUALITY,
693	                    "verdict": FAIL, "basis": f"hard source-kind mask: kind={k or 'UNKNOWN'} ∉ allowed {sorted(allowed)}",
694	                    "force": "hard",
695	                })
696	                plan.receipts.append(SourceReceipt(
697	                    contract_hash=contract_hash, term_id=source_kind_term_id,
698	                    source_id=url, stage=STAGE_QUALITY, verdict=FAIL,
699	                    basis=f"out-of-scope kind {k or 'UNKNOWN'} masked (adequate n={n}, receipt-matched)",
700	                ))
701	    else:
702	        # DEGRADE to prefer + disclosure (C2: never mask a frozen/under-scoped corpus).
703	        _why = []
704	        if not hard_enabled:
705	            _why.append("PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_HARD off")
706	        if not term_is_hard:
707	            _why.append("term force is soft")
708	        if not adequate:
709	            _why.append(f"only {n} in-scope sources (< {min_rows} adequacy floor)")
710	        if not receipt_ok:
711	            _why.append("no matching acquisition receipt")
712	        plan.disclosure = (
713	            f"Scope note: the prompt restricts sources to {sorted(allowed)}; "
714	            f"{'; '.join(_why)} — in-scope sources were prioritized rather than "
715	            f"exclusively enforced."
716	        )
717	        plan.receipts.append(SourceReceipt(
718	            contract_hash=contract_hash, term_id=source_kind_term_id,
719	            source_id="", stage=STAGE_QUALITY, verdict=UNKNOWN,
720	            basis=f"hard source-kind restriction DEGRADED to prefer: {'; '.join(_why)} (n={n})",
721	        ))
722	    return plan

725	def build_topicality_eligibility(
726	    evidence_rows: "list[Any] | None",
727	    *,
728	    objective: str,
729	    thread_queries: "list[str] | None" = None,
730	    floor: float,
731	    scorer: Callable[[str, "list[str] | None", list[dict[str, Any]]], "dict[int, float] | None"],
732	    contract_hash: str = "",
733	    topicality_term_id: str = "topicality",
734	    is_hard: bool = True,
735	    hard_floor: "float | None" = None,
736	    soft_floor: "float | None" = None,
737	) -> EligibilityPlan:
738	    """Post-fetch topical eligibility against the CLEAN objective + owning thread.
739	
740	    Reuses the SAME embedding-cosine scorer live retrieval uses pre-fetch
741	    (``evidence_selector._semantic_relevance_scores``), but as a CONTRACT-AWARE
742	    ELIGIBILITY stage on the FETCHED body (statement + direct_quote), not a prefetch
743	    reorder. Confirmed off-topic (score < ``floor``) is quarantined from the citable
744	    menu (HARD) / down-ranked (SOFT); uncertain (>= floor) stays. A ``None`` score
745	    (embedder unavailable) is UNKNOWN — fail-OPEN for topicality (we never quarantine
746	    a source we could not score; topicality is not a stated user prohibition).
747	
748	    FIX 5(c) — TWO-TIER topicality (only when ``is_hard`` AND ``hard_floor`` is set and
749	    ``0 <= hard_floor < floor``): confirmed junk (score < ``hard_floor``) HARD-quarantines
750	    as before, but the on-topic-ADJACENT band (``hard_floor <= score < floor``) is NOT
751	    dropped — it is SOFT-demoted (a weight, receipt verdict ``FAIL`` but no exclusion), so
752	    the 0.15–0.30 band that was the ACTUAL over-mask stays in the citable menu, ranked down.
753	    ``hard_floor=None`` (default) is byte-identical to the single-floor behavior.
754	    ``soft_floor`` (generalized alias, default ``None``) supplies the same quarantine
755	    boundary when ``hard_floor`` is not given; ``hard_floor`` wins if both are set, and
756	    ``soft_floor=None`` with ``hard_floor=None`` is byte-identical to the single floor.
757	
758	    ``objective`` is the clean research objective (contract objective / clean
759	    question), NOT the instruction-laden prompt. ``thread_queries`` are the owning
760	    thread's sub-queries (the per-subquery max clears a focused facet).
761	    """
762	    # GENERALIZED Fix 5(c): the quarantine boundary may be supplied as ``hard_floor``
763	    # (legacy) OR ``soft_floor`` (generalized alias — the boundary at/above which a
764	    # below-``floor`` row is SOFT-DEMOTED rather than quarantined, even under a hard
765	    # predicate). ``hard_floor`` wins if both are set; ``soft_floor=None`` AND
766	    # ``hard_floor=None`` (defaults) => single-floor path (byte-identical).
767	    _qfloor = hard_floor if hard_floor is not None else soft_floor
768	    # Two-tier arms ONLY under a hard predicate with a valid quarantine floor strictly
769	    # below the pass floor; anything else collapses to the single-floor path (byte-identical).
770	    _two_tier = (
771	        is_hard and _qfloor is not None and 0.0 <= float(_qfloor) < float(floor)
772	    )
773	    plan = EligibilityPlan()
774	    rows = list(evidence_rows or [])
775	    if not rows or not (objective or "").strip():
776	        return plan
777	
778	    row_dicts = [{"statement": _row_text(r), "direct_quote": ""} for r in rows]
779	    try:
780	        score_map = scorer(objective, list(thread_queries or []), row_dicts)
781	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a scorer fault is UNKNOWN (fail-open), never a crash
782	        score_map = None
783	    if score_map is None:
784	        # embedder unavailable -> cannot score -> UNKNOWN, fail-OPEN (no quarantine).
785	        for r in rows:
786	            url = _row_url(r)
787	            if url:
788	                plan.receipts.append(SourceReceipt(
789	                    contract_hash=contract_hash, term_id=topicality_term_id,
790	                    source_id=url, stage=STAGE_TOPICALITY, verdict=UNKNOWN,
791	                    basis="semantic scorer unavailable (fail-open, not quarantined)",
792	                ))
793	        return plan
794	
795	    for idx, r in enumerate(rows):
796	        url = _row_url(r)
797	        if not url:
798	            continue
799	        score = float(score_map.get(idx, 0.0))
800	        if score < floor:
801	            verdict = FAIL
802	            # FIX 5(c): under two-tier, only score < _qfloor is CONFIRMED off-topic and
803	            # HARD-quarantined; the _qfloor <= score < floor BAND is on-topic-adjacent and
804	            # SOFT-demoted (kept in the menu, ranked down) even on a hard predicate.
805	            _quarantine = is_hard and (not _two_tier or score < float(_qfloor))
806	            if _two_tier and not _quarantine:
807	                basis = (f"topicality cosine {score:.3f} in soft band "
808	                         f"[{float(_qfloor):.3f}, {floor:.3f}) (on-topic-adjacent; demoted)")
809	            else:
810	                basis = f"topicality cosine {score:.3f} < floor {floor:.3f} (confirmed off-topic)"
811	            if _quarantine:
812	                plan.eligibility_excluded_ids.add(url)
813	                plan.excluded_records.append({
814	                    "source_id": url, "stage": STAGE_TOPICALITY,
815	                    "verdict": verdict, "basis": basis, "force": "hard",
816	                })
817	            else:
818	                plan.url_to_quality_weight[url] = max(0.05, score)
819	        else:
820	            verdict = PASS
821	            basis = f"topicality cosine {score:.3f} >= floor {floor:.3f}"
822	        plan.receipts.append(SourceReceipt(
823	            contract_hash=contract_hash, term_id=topicality_term_id,
824	            source_id=url, stage=STAGE_TOPICALITY, verdict=verdict, basis=basis,
825	        ))
826	    return plan
