1	"""Pure, no-network question decomposition for the live retrieval path (I-meta-002-q1d #951 q1d-a).
2	
3	The 5 golden benchmark questions are 40-70-word multi-clause paragraphs; four ship with NO
4	hand-authored `amplified` query list, so the whole paragraph was fired as ~one keyword query and
5	each sub-topic was under-retrieved (the Codex-verified S0 depth gap). This module decomposes the
6	question into focused sub-queries deterministically — NO network, NO LLM, pure string operations —
7	which the sweep prepends to the amplified-query list before `run_live_retrieval`.
8	
9	Splitting is deliberately CONSERVATIVE (Codex brief-gate iter-1): split only on sentence terminators,
10	semicolons, explicit enumerators, and top-level conjunction/connective boundaries — NEVER on bare
11	commas. Conjunction / `versus` splits require BOTH sides to carry >= MIN_SPLIT_CONTENT_WORDS content
12	words, so protected compounds ("safety and efficacy", "type 2 diabetes", "non-small cell lung cancer")
13	are preserved BY CONSTRUCTION.
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import os
19	import re
20	
21	# Content-word tokenization (stopword-filtered, 3+ chars) — mirrors the lexical notion used by the
22	# fetch-time rerank; pure, no model.
23	_DECOMP_STOPWORDS = frozenset({
24	    "the", "a", "an", "and", "or", "but", "is", "are", "was", "were", "of", "in", "on", "at",
25	    "to", "for", "with", "by", "from", "as", "that", "this", "these", "those", "it", "its",
26	    "be", "been", "what", "which", "who", "how", "why", "when", "where", "we", "our", "their",
27	    "between", "into", "about", "than", "such", "does", "do", "can", "may", "any", "also",
28	})
29	
30	# Minimum content words for a sub-query to be KEPT (drop noise fragments).
31	MIN_FRAGMENT_CONTENT_WORDS = 3
32	# Minimum content words on EACH side of a conjunction/connective for the split to be allowed
33	# (protects "safety and efficacy", "type 2 diabetes", etc. — each side has < 4 content words).
34	MIN_SPLIT_CONTENT_WORDS = 4
35	# Hard cap on emitted sub-queries (keeps the per-question query fan-out bounded; PR1's total
36	# fetch_cap + rerank keep the corpus bounded regardless).
37	# I-cap-001 (#1059) Part A: un-throttled to Tier-A SOTA (operator-approved 6->15) + made env-overridable
38	# (LAW VI). The default IS full capability (no-downgrade directive) — a run with no env set fans out to 15.
39	DEFAULT_MAX_SUBQUERIES = int(os.getenv("PG_MAX_SUBQUERIES", "15"))
40	
41	# Top-level connective boundaries we MAY split on (only when both sides are query-like).
42	# "vs." is normalized to "vs" up front so it never reaches the terminator regex as a period.
43	_CONNECTIVES = (" as well as ", " versus ", " vs ", " and ")
44	# Protected "X and Y" clinical compounds: even when both surrounding fragments carry >=4 content
45	# words, an `and` flanked by one of these (last-word-of-left, first-word-of-right) pairs is a single
46	# concept, NOT a clause boundary (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1). Lowercased word pairs.
47	_PROTECTED_AND_PAIRS = frozenset({
48	    ("safety", "efficacy"), ("efficacy", "safety"),
49	    ("signs", "symptoms"), ("symptoms", "signs"),
50	    ("risks", "benefits"), ("benefits", "risks"),
51	    ("morbidity", "mortality"), ("mortality", "morbidity"),
52	    ("diagnosis", "treatment"), ("incidence", "prevalence"),
53	    ("sensitivity", "specificity"), ("safety", "tolerability"),
54	})
55	# Additive connectives where a protected-compound guard applies ("safety and efficacy"). Comparators
56	# (" versus ", " vs ") are NOT additive — "safety vs efficacy" is a valid split — so the guard skips them.
57	_ADDITIVE_CONNECTIVES = (" and ", " as well as ")
58	# Abbreviations whose internal period(s) must NOT be treated as sentence terminators. "vs." is
59	# handled separately (normalized to "vs"). Periods here are masked (CASE-INSENSITIVELY, preserving
60	# the original casing) before the terminator split.
61	_ABBREVIATIONS = ("e.g.", "i.e.", "etc.", "et al.", "fig.", "no.", "approx.", "ca.")
62	_ABBREVIATION_RE = re.compile("|".join(re.escape(a) for a in _ABBREVIATIONS), re.IGNORECASE)
63	_PERIOD_MASK = "\x00"
64	# Sentence/clause terminators we always split on. Enumerators like "1.", "2)", "(a)" are handled
65	# by the terminator + enumerator regex below.
66	_TERMINATOR_RE = re.compile(r"(?<=[.;?])\s+|\s*\|\s*")
67	# Strip a leading enumerator marker ("1.", "2)", "(a)", "- ", "• ") from a clause.
68	_ENUMERATOR_RE = re.compile(r"^\s*(?:\(?[0-9a-zA-Z]\)|[0-9]+[.)]|[-•])\s+")
69	
70	
71	def _content_tokens(text: str) -> list[str]:
72	    toks = re.findall(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z\-]{2,}", (text or "").lower())
73	    return [t for t in toks if t not in _DECOMP_STOPWORDS]
74	
75	
76	def _normalize(clause: str) -> str:
77	    clause = _ENUMERATOR_RE.sub("", clause or "")
78	    return re.sub(r"\s+", " ", clause).strip().strip(",;:")
79	
80	
81	def _flanking_words(left: str, right: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
82	    """The last word of `left` and the first word of `right`, lowercased + punctuation-stripped."""
83	    lw = left.split()[-1].lower().strip(",;:.()") if left.split() else ""
84	    rw = right.split()[0].lower().strip(",;:.()") if right.split() else ""
85	    return lw, rw
86	
87	
88	def _split_on_connectives(clause: str) -> list[str]:
89	    """Split a clause on the FIRST top-level connective where both sides have
90	    >= MIN_SPLIT_CONTENT_WORDS content words AND the connective is not flanked by a protected
91	    clinical compound (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1); recurse on each side. Otherwise return [clause].
92	    Bare commas / weak coordinators are never split points."""
93	    lower = clause.lower()
94	    for conn in _CONNECTIVES:
95	        idx = lower.find(conn)
96	        while idx != -1:
97	            left = clause[:idx].strip()
98	            right = clause[idx + len(conn):].strip()
99	            protected = (
100	                conn in _ADDITIVE_CONNECTIVES
101	                and _flanking_words(left, right) in _PROTECTED_AND_PAIRS
102	            )
103	            if (len(_content_tokens(left)) >= MIN_SPLIT_CONTENT_WORDS
104	                    and len(_content_tokens(right)) >= MIN_SPLIT_CONTENT_WORDS
105	                    and not protected):
106	                return _split_on_connectives(left) + _split_on_connectives(right)
107	            idx = lower.find(conn, idx + len(conn))
108	    return [clause]
109	
110	
111	def decompose_question(question: str, *, max_subqueries: int = DEFAULT_MAX_SUBQUERIES) -> list[str]:
112	    """Decompose a multi-clause question into focused sub-query strings. Pure / no-network / no-LLM.
113	
114	    Returns [] for a short single-clause question (the caller then falls back to today's behavior —
115	    the full question is always seeded separately by `run_live_retrieval`). Sub-queries are normalized,
116	    fragment-filtered (>= MIN_FRAGMENT_CONTENT_WORDS content words), de-duplicated case-insensitively,
117	    and capped at `max_subqueries`, preserving leading-clause-first order.
118	    """
119	    if not question or not question.strip():
120	        return []
121	    # Stage 0: abbreviation protection (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1). Normalize "vs." -> "vs" so the
122	    # >=4-per-side connective guard handles comparators (never the period terminator), and MASK the
123	    # internal periods of other abbreviations so they are not mistaken for sentence boundaries.
124	    work = re.sub(r"\bvs\.", "vs", question, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
125	    # Case-insensitive masking that PRESERVES the matched text's casing (so "E.g."/"Fig."/"No." at a
126	    # sentence start are protected too — Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1).
127	    work = _ABBREVIATION_RE.sub(lambda m: m.group(0).replace(".", _PERIOD_MASK), work)
128	    # Stage 1: split on sentence terminators / enumerators / pipes.
129	    primary = [c for c in _TERMINATOR_RE.split(work) if c and c.strip()]
130	    # Stage 2: split each clause on safe top-level connectives.
131	    clauses: list[str] = []
132	    for clause in primary:
133	        clauses.extend(_split_on_connectives(clause))
134	    # Stage 2b (I-deepfix-001 B3 decomposer leg, #1347): drop DIRECTIVE / meta-
135	    # instruction clauses BEFORE they can become live search sub-queries. This is
136	    # the EARLIEST defense-in-depth point — an injected do-not-view / output-shape
137	    # / prohibition-block sentence in the raw question splits cleanly here and
138	    # otherwise passes the >=3-content-word floor straight into the amplified set
139	    # (verified on fresh2: decompose_question(raw_q) emitted 7 directive leaks
140	    # incl. the Salari do-not-view URL block). Pure, no network — reuses the SAME
141	    # `strip_directive_clauses` predicate the retrieval-side scope validator runs,
142	    # so the two legs share one classifier. Gated by PG_QUERY_DIRECTIVE_SCREEN
143	    # (default ON, faithfulness-neutral); OFF reverts to byte-identical behavior.
144	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.scope_query_validator import (  # noqa: PLC0415
145	        directive_screen_enabled,
146	        strip_directive_clauses,
147	    )
148	    if directive_screen_enabled():
149	        clauses, _directive_dropped = strip_directive_clauses(clauses)
150	        if _directive_dropped:
151	            import logging  # noqa: PLC0415
152	            logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.query_decomposer").info(
153	                "[query_decomposer] B3 directive-screen dropped %d directive "
154	                "sub-clause(s) before search: %r",
155	                len(_directive_dropped), _directive_dropped[:5],
156	            )
157	
158	    # Stage 2c (I-deepfix-001 B2 deny-list leg, #1346): drop any generated sub-query that
159	    # carries an operator-PROHIBITED reference fragment (a blocked title / DOI / PII named in
160	    # the question's do-not-view appendix) so the blocked work is never QUERIED. This is the
161	    # ONE legitimate hard drop (§-1.3) — an explicit prohibition, not a relevance/credibility
162	    # call. Complements B3 (which strips directive-shaped clauses): B2 catches a blocked
163	    # DOI/PII/title fragment even when it rides inside an otherwise-normal-looking sub-query.
164	    # Pure / no network; reuses the SAME registry the fetch + selection seams use. Empty
165	    # registry (no appendix, or PG_BLOCKED_REFERENCE_DENYLIST=0) => byte-identical no-op.
166	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.blocked_reference_registry import (  # noqa: PLC0415
167	        build_blocked_registry,
168	    )
169	    _blocked_registry = build_blocked_registry(question)
170	    if not _blocked_registry.is_empty:
171	        _kept_clauses: list[str] = []
172	        _blocked_dropped: list[str] = []
173	        for _clause in clauses:
174	            _hit, _reason = _blocked_registry.is_blocked(url=_clause, title=_clause)
175	            if _hit:
176	                _blocked_dropped.append(_clause)
177	            else:
178	                _kept_clauses.append(_clause)
179	        if _blocked_dropped:
180	            clauses = _kept_clauses
181	            import logging  # noqa: PLC0415
182	            logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.query_decomposer").info(
183	                "[query_decomposer] B2 blocked-reference deny-list dropped %d "
184	                "prohibited sub-query(ies) before search: %r",
185	                len(_blocked_dropped), _blocked_dropped[:5],
186	            )
187	
188	    # Stage 3: unmask abbreviation periods, normalize, fragment-filter, dedup (case-insensitive), cap.
189	    out: list[str] = []
190	    seen: set[str] = set()
191	    for clause in clauses:
192	        norm = _normalize(clause.replace(_PERIOD_MASK, "."))
193	        if len(_content_tokens(norm)) < MIN_FRAGMENT_CONTENT_WORDS:
194	            continue
195	        key = norm.lower()
196	        if key in seen:
197	            continue
198	        seen.add(key)
199	        out.append(norm)
200	        if len(out) >= max_subqueries:
201	            break
202	    # A single clause out of a single-clause question adds nothing → fall back to [].
203	    if len(out) <= 1:
204	        return []
205	    return out
206	
207	
208	def distill_keywords(question: str, *, max_terms: int = 8) -> str:
209	    """FX-18 (#1122): distill an NL question to a SHORT space-joined keyword phrase for a KEYWORD
210	    index (Semantic Scholar bulk returned ~0 for the 40-70-word NL golden questions). Content tokens
211	    only (stopword-filtered, 3+ chars — reuses `_content_tokens`), de-duplicated in first-seen order,
212	    capped at `max_terms`. Pure / no-network / no-LLM. Returns '' when the question has no content
213	    tokens so the caller falls back to the full query (never sends an empty search).
214	    """
215	    out: list[str] = []
216	    seen: set[str] = set()
217	    for tok in _content_tokens(question):
218	        if tok in seen:
219	            continue
220	        seen.add(tok)
221	        out.append(tok)
222	        if len(out) >= max_terms:
223	            break
224	    return " ".join(out)
225	
226	
227	def build_amplified_query_list(
228	    *,
229	    hand_authored: list[str],
230	    decomposed: list[str],
231	    regulatory: list[str],
232	    trial: list[str],
233	) -> list[str]:
234	    """Build the effective amplified-query list with a deterministic prepend order
235	    (hand_authored, decomposed, regulatory, trial) and case-insensitive dedup. Pure — the sweep
236	    wiring test asserts prepend order / dedup / cap behavior without invoking live retrieval.
237	
238	    The anchor full question is NEVER added here — `run_live_retrieval` seeds it separately, so the
239	    decomposed sub-queries augment (not replace) the anchor.
240	    """
241	    out: list[str] = []
242	    seen: set[str] = set()
243	    for group in (hand_authored, decomposed, regulatory, trial):
244	        for q in group or []:
245	            norm = (q or "").strip()
246	            if not norm:
247	                continue
248	            key = norm.lower()
249	            if key in seen:
250	                continue
251	            seen.add(key)
252	            out.append(norm)
253	    return out
254

1	"""Qwen3-Reranker causal-LM yes/no-logit relevance scorer (I-bug-reranker-noise, #1312).
2	
3	WHY THIS EXISTS. The recency-completion reranker WINNER is ``Qwen/Qwen3-Reranker-4B``
4	(I-recency-001 #1296). It was *scored* in the bake-off via the model authors' canonical
5	causal-LM method — append a fixed ``Judge whether ... "yes" or "no"`` template, run one
6	forward pass, and read ``softmax(logit["yes"], logit["no"])[1]`` as P(relevant). Production,
7	however, loaded the SAME model id through ``sentence_transformers.CrossEncoder``. A Qwen3
8	reranker is an ``AutoModelForCausalLM``, NOT a sequence-classification head; ``CrossEncoder``
9	attaches a FRESH, RANDOMLY-INITIALIZED classification head to it. That head was never trained,
10	so ``encoder.predict()`` returns ~0.5 NOISE — the reranker winner was, in effect, NOT running
11	in production (the reorder it produced was random). The bake-off file documents this exact
12	trap (``scripts/dr_benchmark/retrieval_bakeoff/reranker/run_bakeoff.py`` lines 27-29, 130):
13	"loading a Qwen reranker via CrossEncoder mints a random score head -> ~0.5 noise; the
14	canonical method scores P('yes')".
15	
16	WHAT THIS MODULE IS. A tiny, lazy, GPU-first scorer that reproduces the bake-off's proven
17	``_build_qwen_causal_fn`` method as production code (the bake-off lives under ``scripts/`` and
18	must NOT be imported by ``src/``). It exposes one callable:
19	
20	    score_query_document_relevance(query, documents, *, model_id, device=None) -> list[float]
21	
22	returning P("yes") in [0, 1] per document, in INPUT ORDER (a higher score = more relevant).
23	
24	INVARIANTS PRESERVED BY THE CALLER (``evidence_selector._maybe_rerank_selection``):
25	  * torch / transformers are imported LAZILY *inside* this module's functions, and this module
26	    is itself imported lazily by the caller only on the reranker-ON path — so the default-OFF
27	    selection path pays ZERO import / model cost.
28	  * Any load/scoring failure is the CALLER's concern: it falls back LOUDLY to the original
29	    (tier-balanced) order. This module simply raises on failure; it never returns a silent stub.
30	  * Model id stays a ``.env`` knob (``PG_RERANKER_MODEL`` -> ``CrossEncoderConfig.from_env``);
31	    this module is id-agnostic and works for 0.6B / 4B / 8B identically (same architecture, same
32	    template) — which also makes the fix CPU-fire-testable on the 0.6B while production serves
33	    the 4B on GPU.
34	"""
35	
36	from __future__ import annotations
37	
38	import logging
39	import os
40	from typing import Optional, Sequence
41	
42	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.qwen_reranker_scorer")
43	
44	
45	class RerankerLoadError(RuntimeError):
46	    """I-deepfix-001 P1-1 (#1344): raised ONLY when the W7 reranker STRUCTURALLY
47	    fails to load — the lazy torch/transformers import, ``from_pretrained``, the
48	    ``.to(device)`` placement, or the yes/no token-id resolution. The caller
49	    (``evidence_selector._maybe_rerank_selection``) treats this as the structural
50	    W7-dark signal the winner-firing gate hard-gates on. A failure of the per-document
51	    FORWARD PASS (a transient encode/OOM exception) is NOT wrapped in this type — it
52	    surfaces as a plain exception so the caller can treat it as a transient (the model
53	    DID load) and NOT flip the sticky structural signal. This mirrors the embedder's
54	    structural-vs-transient split (``evidence_selector._get_semantic_embedder``)."""
55	
56	# The fixed scoring template from the Qwen3-Reranker model card (verbatim with the bake-off's
57	# proven ``_build_qwen_causal_fn``). The model answers "yes"/"no"; we read the next-token logits
58	# of those two tokens and softmax them -> P("yes") = relevance.
59	_SYSTEM_PREFIX = (
60	    "<|im_start|>system\nJudge whether the Document meets the requirements based on the Query "
61	    'and the Instruct provided. Note that the answer can only be "yes" or "no".<|im_end|>\n'
62	    "<|im_start|>user\n"
63	)
64	_ASSISTANT_SUFFIX = "<|im_end|>\n<|im_start|>assistant\n<think>\n\n</think>\n\n"
65	_INSTRUCT = "Given a web search query, retrieve relevant passages that answer the query"
66	
67	# Per-document input truncation length (tokens). A CAP, not a target — long docs are truncated
68	# so the single forward pass stays bounded. Matches the bake-off's max_length.
69	_MAX_INPUT_TOKENS = 4096
70	
71	
72	def _resolve_device(device: Optional[str]) -> str:
73	    """GPU-first: ``cuda`` when available, else ``cpu``. Honors an explicit override.
74	
75	    Precedence: an explicit ``device`` arg wins (the production W7 path pre-resolves
76	    in ``evidence_selector._maybe_rerank_selection`` so env > cfg.device is decided
77	    THERE, before the call). This ``PG_RERANKER_DEVICE`` fallback is defense for
78	    DIRECT callers that pass ``device=None`` (and the offline test surface) — it is a
79	    LAUNCH-ENV read, not a slate force-ON value, so it does not affect SLATE-PURITY /
80	    NO-LOSER gating (those gate winner force-ON *values*, not device reads)."""
81	    if device:
82	        return device
83	    env_device = os.getenv("PG_RERANKER_DEVICE", "").strip()
84	    if env_device:
85	        return env_device
86	    import torch  # lazy
87	
88	    return "cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"
89	
90	
91	def score_query_document_relevance(
92	    query: str,
93	    documents: Sequence[str],
94	    *,
95	    model_id: str,
96	    device: Optional[str] = None,
97	) -> list[float]:
98	    """Score each document's relevance to ``query`` as P("yes") in [0, 1], in input order.
99	
100	    Loads ``model_id`` as an ``AutoModelForCausalLM`` (the CORRECT class for a Qwen3 reranker)
101	    and reads the next-token "yes"/"no" logits — the canonical, bake-off-proven method. A real
102	    relevance signal: a clearly-relevant document scores markedly HIGHER than junk (unlike the
103	    ~0.5 noise the random CrossEncoder head produced).
104	
105	    Raises on any load/scoring failure (the caller falls back loudly to the original order).
106	    torch + transformers are imported here, lazily, so the reranker-OFF path never touches them.
107	    """
108	    docs = list(documents)
109	    if not docs:
110	        return []
111	
112	    # I-deepfix-001 P1-1 (#1344): the STRUCTURAL LOAD region. A failure HERE (import /
113	    # from_pretrained / device placement / token-id resolution) is a structural W7-dark
114	    # signal -> re-raise as RerankerLoadError so the caller marks the gate dark. A
115	    # later FORWARD-PASS failure (below, in _score_one) is a transient and is NOT
116	    # wrapped, so it does not flip the sticky structural signal.
117	    try:
118	        import torch  # lazy — nothing here loads on the reranker-OFF path
119	        from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer
120	
121	        resolved_device = _resolve_device(device)
122	        logger.info(
123	            "[qwen-reranker] loading %s on %s (causal-LM yes/no-logit scoring; NOT CrossEncoder)",
124	            model_id, resolved_device,
125	        )
126	        tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_id, padding_side="left")
127	        model = (
128	            AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(model_id, dtype="auto")
129	            .to(resolved_device)
130	            .eval()
131	        )
132	        token_id_no = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids("no")
133	        token_id_yes = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids("yes")
134	    except Exception as load_exc:  # structural import / load / device / token failure
135	        raise RerankerLoadError(
136	            f"W7 reranker structural load failed for {model_id!r}: {load_exc}"
137	        ) from load_exc
138	
139	    def _score_one(document: str) -> float:
140	        text = (
141	            _SYSTEM_PREFIX
142	            + f"<Instruct>: {_INSTRUCT}\n<Query>: {query or ''}\n<Document>: {document}"
143	            + _ASSISTANT_SUFFIX
144	        )
145	        inputs = tokenizer(
146	            text,
147	            return_tensors="pt",
148	            truncation=True,
149	            max_length=_MAX_INPUT_TOKENS,
150	        ).to(resolved_device)
151	        with torch.no_grad():
152	            last_token_logits = model(**inputs).logits[0, -1]
153	        yes_no_pair = torch.stack(
154	            [last_token_logits[token_id_no], last_token_logits[token_id_yes]]
155	        ).float()
156	        return float(torch.softmax(yes_no_pair, dim=0)[1])
157	
158	    return [_score_one(d) for d in docs]
159

1	"""M-28 Fix #1 (2026-04-20): authoritative-source query expansion.
2	
3	Generalizable abstraction: scope templates carry an optional list of
4	host-shaped strings under `regulatory_anchors`. When present, the
5	expander emits one extra amplified query per anchor of the form
6	`{base_question} site:{anchor}`. When absent / empty / malformed,
7	the expander returns an empty list. The template is the ONLY place
8	the host list lives; this module has no baked-in host knowledge and
9	no baked-in query-domain knowledge. It treats anchors as opaque
10	host-shaped tokens.
11	
12	Design invariants:
13	  - Zero host names, zero domain names, zero jurisdictional terms
14	    anywhere in this module. All such content lives in YAML templates
15	    and in test fixtures. A repository-level test
16	    (`test_m28_regulatory_expander_no_hardcoded_hosts`) asserts this
17	    at CI time so future edits cannot slip in a hard-code by accident.
18	  - Template-driven: the caller chooses the anchors per domain by
19	    writing them into the template. Every domain supported by the
20	    scope_templates/ directory uses the same expander code.
21	  - Backwards-compatible: missing list / empty list / malformed
22	    entries all return an empty list, so the expander is safe to call
23	    unconditionally.
24	
25	Design:
26	  - Caller (sweep orchestrator) loads the scope template once and
27	    calls `expand_regulatory_queries(question, template)` to get the
28	    extra queries, merges them into the amplified list.
29	  - The live retriever does not know about the anchor concept; the
30	    extra queries travel through as regular amplified queries and go
31	    through the same scope-validator de-drift + Serper/S2 fanout.
32	"""
33	from __future__ import annotations
34	
35	import logging
36	import os
37	from typing import Any
38	
39	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.regulatory_expander")
40	
41	
42	# PG_SWEEP_MAX_REGULATORY_ANCHORS: soft upper bound on how many anchor
43	# queries this function will emit from a single template. A template
44	# with more anchors still loads fine; the expander just truncates
45	# emission to the cap. Set to 0 to disable the cap.
46	#
47	# M-43 (2026-04-22): raised default 10 -> 12 after a prior retrieval
48	# sweep silently truncated the final anchor in an 11-entry template,
49	# dropping downstream bibliography coverage to zero for that
50	# jurisdiction. 12 fits the current template with one future-addition
51	# headroom. Extra retrieval cost per run: ~2 queries, negligible.
52	_DEFAULT_MAX_ANCHORS = 12
53	
54	
55	def _max_anchors() -> int:
56	    """Read the max-anchor cap from env each call (so a test can monkey-
57	    patch without importing a module-level constant)."""
58	    raw = os.getenv("PG_SWEEP_MAX_REGULATORY_ANCHORS")
59	    if raw is None:
60	        return _DEFAULT_MAX_ANCHORS
61	    try:
62	        val = int(raw)
63	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
64	        return _DEFAULT_MAX_ANCHORS
65	    return max(0, val)
66	
67	
68	def _extract_anchors(template: dict[str, Any] | None) -> list[str]:
69	    """Pull the `regulatory_anchors` list from a loaded scope template.
70	
71	    Returns an empty list for missing / malformed / empty configuration.
72	    Trims whitespace, skips non-string entries and empties.
73	    """
74	    if not isinstance(template, dict):
75	        return []
76	    raw = template.get("regulatory_anchors")
77	    if not isinstance(raw, list):
78	        return []
79	    anchors: list[str] = []
80	    for entry in raw:
81	        if not isinstance(entry, str):
82	            continue
83	        stripped = entry.strip().lower()
84	        # Reject entries that look like full URLs — the `site:` search
85	        # operator expects a host. A full URL with a path would produce
86	        # an invalid query. Keep only host-shaped tokens. Also reject
87	        # entries containing whitespace.
88	        if not stripped or "/" in stripped or " " in stripped:
89	            continue
90	        anchors.append(stripped)
91	    # Deduplicate while preserving declared order.
92	    seen: set[str] = set()
93	    unique: list[str] = []
94	    for a in anchors:
95	        if a not in seen:
96	            seen.add(a)
97	            unique.append(a)
98	    return unique
99	
100	
101	def expand_regulatory_queries(
102	    question: str,
103	    template: dict[str, Any] | None,
104	) -> list[str]:
105	    """Return `site:{anchor}` queries derived from the scope template.
106	
107	    Args:
108	        question: the user's research question (or any base query text).
109	        template: the loaded scope template dict (or None if none set).
110	
111	    Returns:
112	        A list of `{question} site:{anchor}` strings, one per configured
113	        anchor (up to `PG_SWEEP_MAX_REGULATORY_ANCHORS` entries). Empty
114	        list when the template has no `regulatory_anchors` key, an
115	        empty list, or when the question is empty/whitespace.
116	
117	    This function is pure and deterministic — no network, no state.
118	    """
119	    base = (question or "").strip()
120	    if not base:
121	        return []
122	    anchors = _extract_anchors(template)
123	    if not anchors:
124	        return []
125	    cap = _max_anchors()
126	    if cap > 0 and len(anchors) > cap:
127	        logger.info(
128	            "[regulatory_expander] template has %d anchors; capped to %d "
129	            "via PG_SWEEP_MAX_REGULATORY_ANCHORS",
130	            len(anchors), cap,
131	        )
132	        anchors = anchors[:cap]
133	    queries = [f"{base} site:{anchor}" for anchor in anchors]
134	    logger.info(
135	        "[regulatory_expander] emitted %d anchor queries for base=%r",
136	        len(queries), base[:60],
137	    )
138	    return queries
139

1	"""I-complete-004 (#1190): targeted required-entity retrieval lane.
2	
3	PURPOSE
4	=======
5	Some clinical must-cover S0 safety entities (contraindications, dosing limits,
6	boxed warnings, regulatory status) live on drug-label / guideline pages
7	(DailyMed, accessdata.fda.gov, EMA, NICE, Health Canada, NIH ODS) that the
8	generic Serper/S2 research corpus rarely surfaces. When that content is absent
9	from the cited corpus, the report cannot make verified contraindication/dosing
10	claims and the must-cover slots gap-disclose — the recurring cause of
11	`four_role_held` on clinical questions (#1188 + canary diagnosis).
12	
13	This lane is an ENV-GATED, additive retry. For each must-cover entity that is
14	STILL unsatisfied after the normal V30 frame fetch, it:
15	
16	1. Builds TARGETED safety queries (intervention anchor x entity safety term)
17	   biased to an authoritative clinical-authority domain set UNION the entity's
18	   OWN ``url_pattern`` host, and fires them through ``search_fn`` (Serper) to
19	   DISCOVER candidate authoritative URLs.
20	2. FETCHES the discovered candidate URLs through the EXISTING live-retrieval
21	   pipeline (``retrieval_fn`` = ``run_live_retrieval`` with ``seed_urls`` +
22	   ``seed_only=True`` — the same AccessBypass/Zyte chokepoint, no Serper/S2
23	   fan-out), producing canonical evidence rows that carry their REAL fetched
24	   URLs.
25	3. RETURNS those evidence rows so the caller MERGES them into the corpus
26	   (``evidence_for_gen``) — exactly like the saturation gap-round merge.
27	
28	FAITHFULNESS (§-1.1 — LETHAL otherwise)
29	=======================================
30	The discovered rows are injected as ORDINARY corpus evidence carrying their REAL
31	fetched URLs — they are NEVER keyed to an entity_id and NEVER relabeled with an
32	entity's canonical ``url_pattern`` (the citation-faithfulness lie). All
33	downstream gating is UNCHANGED: the generator can fill a must-cover slot ONLY if
34	the new evidence passes the SAME strict_verify, and the 4-role coverage gate
35	(`_entity_canonical_match`, operator-locked, NOT touched here) still requires
36	EXACT canonical-identifier equality. An entity with no verifiable authoritative
37	content stays the gap-disclosure it already was — no fabrication, no forced
38	coverage.
39	
40	HONEST SCOPE
41	============
42	Because the 4-role coverage gate for url-pattern regulatory entities requires
43	``record.url == entity.url_pattern`` EXACTLY, injecting an ALTERNATE authoritative
44	URL CANNOT flip that specific entity's coverage. What this lane does is get the
45	authoritative SAFETY CONTENT into the corpus so the generator can write VERIFIED
46	contraindication / dosing claims (directly addressing #1190's "absent from the
47	cited research corpus"). It flips 4-role coverage only for DOI/PMID-keyed
48	entities, or when the entity's exact ``url_pattern`` is itself among the fetched
49	URLs — not for the url-pattern regulatory entity class as a rule.
50	
51	PURITY / TESTABILITY
52	====================
53	``run_required_entity_lane`` takes the search and retrieval callables by
54	DEPENDENCY INJECTION (``search_fn`` / ``retrieval_fn``). Production wires the
55	real Serper search and ``run_live_retrieval``; tests pass deterministic stubs.
56	NO network in this module. The caller owns the env gate and the corpus merge.
57	"""

198	def _bounded_anchor(research_question: str) -> str:
199	    """Whitespace-collapsed research question clipped to the query-anchor char
200	    ceiling (mirrors :func:`_intervention_anchor`'s ``[:120]`` fallback).
201	
202	    Bounding the anchor length keeps the built ``<anchor> <entity>`` query inside
203	    Serper's 2048-char ``q`` limit so a long question no longer 400s the coverage
204	    lane. It bounds a QUERY STRING length only — no source is dropped (§-1.3).
205	    A ``0`` ceiling (explicit operator override) disables the anchor clip.
206	    """
207	    collapsed = " ".join((research_question or "").split()).strip()
208	    limit = _query_anchor_max_chars()
209	    return collapsed[:limit] if limit > 0 else collapsed
210

212	def _bounded_gap_query(anchor: str, entity: str) -> str:
213	    """Build ``<anchor> <entity>`` and hard-clip to the Serper query ceiling.
214	
215	    The anchor is already length-bounded by :func:`_bounded_anchor`; this final
216	    clip is the belt-and-suspenders guarantee that NO built query can exceed the
217	    Serper ``q`` limit even for a pathologically long entity term. A ``0``
218	    ceiling (explicit operator override) disables the final clip.
219	    """
220	    query = f"{anchor} {entity}".strip() if anchor else (entity or "").strip()
221	    limit = _serper_query_max_chars()
222	    if limit > 0 and len(query) > limit:
223	        query = query[:limit].rstrip()
224	    return query

228	    """The authoritative clinical-authority domain set (LAW VI: env-overridable).
229

231	    are dropped. Empty/absent -> the operator-approved default set.

240	def entity_url_host(url_pattern: Optional[str]) -> Optional[str]:
241	    """Return the bare host of an entity's ``url_pattern`` (no scheme/port/path).
242	
243	    Used to UNION the entity's own canonical host onto the per-entity domain
244	    bias so url-only-canonical entities are reachable. Returns None when the
245	    pattern is empty or carries no host.
246	    """
247	    if not url_pattern or not isinstance(url_pattern, str):
248	        return None
249	    try:
250	        host = urlsplit(url_pattern.strip()).netloc
251	    except ValueError:
252	        return None
253	    if not host:
254	        return None
255	    # Strip any userinfo / port; keep the bare registrable host.
256	    host = host.split("@")[-1].split(":")[0].strip().lower()
257	    return host or None

260	def frame_row_is_unsatisfied(row: FrameRow, *, min_span_chars: Optional[int] = None) -> bool:
261	    """An entity is UNSATISFIED iff its FrameRow has no verifiable span.
262	
263	    Mirrors the gap-routing test in contract_section_runner.py:370-386:
264	    FRAME_GAP_UNRECOVERABLE, OR METADATA_ONLY with a direct_quote shorter than
265	    the minimum verifiable span. Any other class (OPEN_ACCESS / ABSTRACT_ONLY /
266	    HUMAN_CURATED / METADATA_ONLY-with-real-quote) is already satisfied.
267	    """
268	    floor = _min_verifiable_span_chars() if min_span_chars is None else min_span_chars
269	    if row.provenance_class == ProvenanceClass.FRAME_GAP_UNRECOVERABLE:
270	        return True
271	    if row.provenance_class == ProvenanceClass.METADATA_ONLY:
272	        return len((row.direct_quote or "").strip()) < floor
273	    return False

276	def _intervention_anchor(
277	    *,
278	    entity_meta: Optional[Any],
279	    scope_overrides: Optional[Mapping[str, Any]],
280	    research_question: str,
281	) -> str:
282	    """Pick the cleanest anchor for the targeted query (deterministic order).
283	
284	    Priority: the contract entity's ``label_name`` (cleanest, e.g. "Mounjaro");
285	    then ``scope_overrides['intervention']``; then a trimmed prefix of the
286	    research question. Never empty (the question is always present).
287	    """
288	    label_name = getattr(entity_meta, "label_name", None) if entity_meta is not None else None
289	    if isinstance(label_name, str) and label_name.strip():
290	        return label_name.strip()
291	    if scope_overrides:
292	        intervention = scope_overrides.get("intervention")
293	        if isinstance(intervention, str) and intervention.strip():
294	            return intervention.strip()
295	    # Fallback: first chunk of the research question (bounded for query sanity).
296	    return (research_question or "").strip()[:120]

299	def build_targeted_queries(
300	    *,
301	    entity: Mapping[str, Any],
302	    intervention_anchor: str,
303	    max_queries: Optional[int] = None,
304	) -> tuple[str, ...]:
305	    """Build the targeted safety queries for one unsatisfied entity.
306	
307	    ``<intervention_anchor> <safety term>`` per the entity's ``s0_category``
308	    (falling back to generic safety terms). Deterministic order, de-duplicated,
309	    capped at ``max_queries`` (env-bounded). The authoritative-domain ``site:``
310	    bias is applied by the search backend via the ``domains=`` argument, NOT
311	    baked into the query string here.
312	    """
313	    cap = (
314	        _bounded_int_env(_MAX_QUERIES_PER_ENTITY_ENV, _DEFAULT_MAX_QUERIES_PER_ENTITY)
315	        if max_queries is None
316	        else max(0, max_queries)
317	    )
318	    s0_category = entity.get("s0_category")
319	    terms = _S0_CATEGORY_QUERY_TERMS.get(s0_category or "", _GENERIC_SAFETY_TERMS)
320	    anchor = (intervention_anchor or "").strip()
321	    queries: list[str] = []
322	    seen: set[str] = set()
323	    for term in terms:
324	        query = f"{anchor} {term}".strip() if anchor else term.strip()
325	        key = query.lower()
326	        if query and key not in seen:
327	            seen.add(key)
328	            queries.append(query)
329	        if len(queries) >= cap:
330	            break
331	    return tuple(queries)

335	# Max targeted gap queries the general still-missing-entity detector emits per run
336	# (compute-safety bound; each query still flows the unchanged weight-and-consolidate
337	# path + frozen verify — §-1.3, never a breadth target).
338	_MISSING_ENTITY_MAX_QUERIES_ENV = "PG_MISSING_ENTITY_GAP_MAX_QUERIES"
339	_DEFAULT_MISSING_ENTITY_MAX_QUERIES = 12

342	def entity_covered_in_corpus(entity_term: str, corpus_texts: Sequence[str]) -> bool:
343	    """True iff ``entity_term`` appears (case-insensitive substring) in ANY of the
344	    corpus texts (titles / quotes / statements the caller supplies).
345	
346	    This is a DETECT-only signal for the R3 gap-detect: a required entity whose
347	    name never appears in the gathered corpus is a candidate for a targeted
348	    corrective retrieval. It is deliberately lenient (substring, case-folded) so a
349	    present-but-differently-cased mention still counts as covered — the detector
350	    must never over-fire a corrective round for an entity the corpus already has.
351	    """
352	    needle = " ".join((entity_term or "").split()).strip().lower()
353	    if not needle:
354	        return True  # empty term is vacuously "covered" — never fire a query for it
355	    for text in corpus_texts or ():
356	        if needle in (text or "").lower():
357	            return True
358	    return False
359	
360	
361	def missing_entity_gap_queries(
362	    *,
363	    required_entities: Sequence[str],
364	    corpus_texts: Sequence[str],
365	    research_question: str,
366	    max_queries: Optional[int] = None,
367	) -> list[str]:
368	    """R3 (#1344): derive targeted corrective queries for STILL-MISSING required
369	    entities — field-agnostic (no clinical-safety literal).
370	
371	    DRB-II 2nd-order recall rubrics need a chained fact: A -> find entity -> fact
372	    B. The up-front decomposition fans out once and the single-pass CRAG loop can
373	    stop before the chain closes, so entities the task requires but the corpus
374	    never surfaced stay uncovered. This detector compares the REQUIRED entities
375	    against what the corpus actually mentions (:func:`entity_covered_in_corpus`)
376	    and emits ONE research-question-anchored targeted query per STILL-MISSING
377	    entity. Each query flows the SAME weight-and-consolidate retrieval + the
378	    UNCHANGED frozen verify — this only decides WHAT to search next, never drops
379	    a source or relaxes a gate (§-1.3).
380	
381	    Anchoring to the research question keeps the corrective query on-subject (the
382	    same drift fix FS-Researcher's scope-anchor applies) so a bare entity name
383	    does not generalise into its broad field.
384	
385	    Args:
386	        required_entities: entity names/labels the task requires covered.
387	        corpus_texts: the text the corpus currently carries (titles, direct
388	            quotes, statements) used to decide coverage.
389	        research_question: the retrieval anchor prepended to each gap query.
390	        max_queries: override for `PG_MISSING_ENTITY_GAP_MAX_QUERIES` (tests).
391	
392	    Returns:
393	        An ordered, de-duplicated, capped list of targeted gap queries — one per
394	        still-missing entity. Empty iff every required entity is already covered
395	        (the caller then fires no corrective round).
396	    """
397	    cap = (
398	        _bounded_int_env(
399	            _MISSING_ENTITY_MAX_QUERIES_ENV, _DEFAULT_MISSING_ENTITY_MAX_QUERIES
400	        )
401	        if max_queries is None
402	        else max(0, max_queries)
403	    )
404	    anchor = _bounded_anchor(research_question)
405	    queries: list[str] = []
406	    seen: set[str] = set()
407	    for raw_entity in required_entities or ():
408	        entity = " ".join((raw_entity or "").split()).strip()
409	        if not entity:
410	            continue
411	        if entity_covered_in_corpus(entity, corpus_texts):
412	            continue
413	        query = _bounded_gap_query(anchor, entity)
414	        key = query.lower()
415	        if key in seen:
416	            continue
417	        seen.add(key)
418	        queries.append(query)
419	        if len(queries) >= cap:
420	            break
421	    return queries
422

424	def _domains_for_entity(entity: Mapping[str, Any]) -> list[str]:
425	    """Authoritative domains UNION the entity's own ``url_pattern`` host."""
426	    domains = list(required_entity_domains())
427	    host = entity_url_host(entity.get("url_pattern"))
428	    if host and host not in domains:
429	        domains.append(host)
430	    return domains

433	def _result_url(hit: Any) -> str:
434	    """Extract a candidate URL from a single Serper result row (or raw string)."""
435	    if isinstance(hit, str):
436	        return hit.strip()
437	    if isinstance(hit, Mapping):
438	        for key in ("url", "link"):
439	            value = hit.get(key)
440	            if isinstance(value, str) and value.strip():
441	                return value.strip()
442	    return ""

445	def run_required_entity_lane(
446	    *,
447	    frame_rows: Sequence[FrameRow],
448	    entity_meta_by_id: Mapping[str, Any],
449	    entity_cfg_by_id: Mapping[str, Mapping[str, Any]],
450	    research_question: str,
451	    scope_overrides: Optional[Mapping[str, Any]],
452	    search_fn: Callable[..., Any],
453	    retrieval_fn: Callable[..., Any],
454	) -> RequiredEntityLaneResult:
455	    """Run the targeted required-entity lane (ENV-GATED by the CALLER).
456	
457	    For each UNSATISFIED FrameRow:
458	      1. Build + fire targeted authoritative-domain-biased search queries via
459	         ``search_fn(query, domains=, max_results=)`` and COLLECT the candidate
460	         URLs (capped per-entity and total-entities).
461	      2. FETCH the collected candidate URLs through ``retrieval_fn`` (the live
462	         retriever) with ``seed_urls`` + ``seed_only=True`` — same Zyte
463	         chokepoint, no Serper/S2 fan-out — producing canonical evidence rows
464	         carrying their REAL fetched URLs.
465	
466	    Returns the NEWLY fetched evidence rows for the CALLER to merge into the
467	    corpus (dedup + evidence_id renumber, like the saturation gap-round). The
468	    lane NEVER mutates ``frame_rows`` and NEVER keys a row to an entity_id, so a
469	    fetched row can never be relabeled as the entity (provenance honesty).
470	
471	    DEPENDENCY INJECTION: ``search_fn`` and ``retrieval_fn`` are injected so this
472	    is pure / offline-testable. The caller owns the env gate (``lane_enabled()``).
473	    """
474	    max_entities = _bounded_int_env(_MAX_ENTITIES_ENV, _DEFAULT_MAX_ENTITIES)
475	    max_results = _bounded_int_env(
476	        _MAX_RESULTS_PER_QUERY_ENV, _DEFAULT_MAX_RESULTS_PER_QUERY
477	    )
478	    max_seed_urls = _bounded_int_env(
479	        _MAX_SEED_URLS_PER_ENTITY_ENV, _DEFAULT_MAX_SEED_URLS_PER_ENTITY
480	    )
481	    floor = _min_verifiable_span_chars()
482	
483	    attempted: list[str] = []
484	    queries_by_entity: dict[str, tuple[str, ...]] = {}
485	    seed_urls: list[str] = []
486	    seen_seed: set[str] = set()
487	
488	    for row in frame_rows:
489	        if len(attempted) >= max_entities:
490	            break
491	        if not frame_row_is_unsatisfied(row, min_span_chars=floor):
492	            continue
493	        entity_id = row.entity_id
494	        entity_cfg = entity_cfg_by_id.get(entity_id)
495	        if entity_cfg is None:
496	            # No config to anchor the targeted query on — cannot act; skip.
497	            continue
498	        attempted.append(entity_id)
499	
500	        anchor = _intervention_anchor(
501	            entity_meta=entity_meta_by_id.get(entity_id),
502	            scope_overrides=scope_overrides,
503	            research_question=research_question,
504	        )
505	        queries = build_targeted_queries(
506	            entity=entity_cfg, intervention_anchor=anchor
507	        )
508	        queries_by_entity[entity_id] = queries
509	
510	        # (1) Targeted authoritative-domain-biased search -> DISCOVER candidate
511	        # URLs (consume the search output). Errors are non-fatal: a failed query
512	        # simply yields no candidates for that entity.
513	        domains = _domains_for_entity(entity_cfg)
514	        entity_urls: list[str] = []
515	        for query in queries:
516	            try:
517	                hits = search_fn(query, domains=domains, max_results=max_results)
518	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — discovery is best-effort
519	                logger.warning(
520	                    "required_entity_lane: search failed for entity=%s query=%r",
521	                    entity_id,
522	                    query,
523	                )
524	                continue
525	            for hit in hits or []:
526	                url = _result_url(hit)
527	                if not url or url in seen_seed:
528	                    continue
529	                seen_seed.add(url)
530	                entity_urls.append(url)
531	                if len(entity_urls) >= max_seed_urls:
532	                    break
533	            if len(entity_urls) >= max_seed_urls:
534	                break
535	        seed_urls.extend(entity_urls)
536	
537	    if not seed_urls:
538	        return RequiredEntityLaneResult(
539	            evidence_rows=[],
540	            attempted_entity_ids=tuple(attempted),
541	            queries_by_entity=queries_by_entity,
542	            seed_urls=(),
543	        )
544	
545	    # (2) FETCH the discovered URLs through the existing live retriever, seed-only
546	    # (no Serper/S2 fan-out), so each candidate is fetched + classified through
547	    # the SAME chokepoint and emerges as a canonical evidence row with its REAL
548	    # url. A retrieval failure leaves the corpus unchanged (best-effort).
549	    try:
550	        result = retrieval_fn(
551	            research_question=research_question,
552	            amplified_queries=[],
553	            seed_urls=list(seed_urls),
554	            seed_only=True,
555	            seed_source=SEED_SOURCE_LABEL,
556	            seed_query_origin=SEED_QUERY_ORIGIN,
557	            anchor_seed=False,
558	        )
559	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a failed fetch leaves the corpus as-is
560	        logger.warning(
561	            "required_entity_lane: live-retrieval fetch raised for %d seed urls; "
562	            "leaving corpus unchanged",
563	            len(seed_urls),
564	        )
565	        return RequiredEntityLaneResult(
566	            evidence_rows=[],
567	            attempted_entity_ids=tuple(attempted),
568	            queries_by_entity=queries_by_entity,
569	            seed_urls=tuple(seed_urls),
570	        )
571	
572	    evidence_rows = list(getattr(result, "evidence_rows", []) or [])
573	    return RequiredEntityLaneResult(
574	        evidence_rows=evidence_rows,
575	        attempted_entity_ids=tuple(attempted),
576	        queries_by_entity=queries_by_entity,

581	# ============================================================================
582	# COVERAGE LEVER L5 — question/facet-derived required-entity retrieval lane
583	# ============================================================================
584	# I-deepfix-001 (#1344) DRB-II COVERAGE lever L5.
585	#
586	# The R3 gap-detect (:func:`missing_entity_gap_queries`) already turns a KNOWN
587	# list of required entities into targeted corrective queries. L5 closes the
588	# remaining gap: it DERIVES that required-entity set from the QUESTION and the
589	# FACET tree (the R1 :mod:`expert_facet_planner` output) — deterministically and
590	# OFFLINE — so the lane runs even when no external rubric hands us the list. It
591	# then targets ONLY the derived entities the corpus does not yet mention and
592	# fetches candidate sources for them through the SAME injected live-retrieval
593	# chokepoint the clinical lane uses (seed-only, no Serper/S2 fan-out).
594	#
595	# FAITHFULNESS (§-1.1 / operator BINDING — LETHAL otherwise)
596	# ----------------------------------------------------------
597	# Identical contract to the clinical lane: fetched rows are ORDINARY corpus
598	# evidence carrying their REAL fetched URLs; they are NEVER keyed to an entity
599	# and NEVER relabeled. strict_verify / NLI entailment / 4-role D8 / provenance /
600	# span-grounding are UNCHANGED. A derived required entity for which the lane
601	# surfaces NO evidence STAYS exactly the gap disclosure it already was — it is
602	# recorded in ``gap_entities`` and NOTHING is injected for it (no fabrication, no
603	# forced coverage). Deriving an entity only decides WHAT to search next; it can
604	# never credit coverage on its own (§-1.3 WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE, never
605	# FILTER / FORCE). The per-entity + per-run bounds are compute-safety CEILINGS
606	# billed by actual use — never a breadth target / cap / thinner / floor.
607	#
608	# DEFAULT-ON kill-switch (LAW VI): ``PG_COVERAGE_L5_REQUIRED_ENTITY`` defaults
609	# ON; set it to 0/false/off for a byte-identical no-op.
610	# ============================================================================
611	
612	# --- default-ON kill-switch (LAW VI) ------------------------------------------
613	_COVERAGE_L5_ENABLED_ENV = "PG_COVERAGE_L5_REQUIRED_ENTITY"
614	
615	# --- compute-safety CEILING on derived entities (NOT a breadth target; §-1.3) -
616	_COVERAGE_L5_MAX_ENTITIES_ENV = "PG_COVERAGE_L5_MAX_ENTITIES"
617	_DEFAULT_COVERAGE_L5_MAX_ENTITIES = 24
618	
619	# Honest source/origin tags so merged L5 rows are attributed to THIS lane (never
620	# mislabeled as clinical-lane seeds or primary-trial seeds).
621	COVERAGE_L5_SEED_SOURCE_LABEL = "required_entity_coverage_l5"
622	COVERAGE_L5_SEED_QUERY_ORIGIN = "required_entity_coverage_l5_targeted_search"
623	
624	# Question words / articles / conjunctions / pronouns / common lead verbs that
625	# must NOT seed (or continue) a proper-noun run — a sentence-initial capital or a
626	# Title-Cased function word is not a named entity. Compared case-folded, so a
627	# capitalized "The"/"In" inside a Title-Case facet name still breaks the run.
628	_ENTITY_STOPWORDS: frozenset = frozenset({
629	    "the", "a", "an", "and", "or", "but", "of", "for", "to", "in", "on", "at",
630	    "by", "with", "from", "as", "how", "what", "why", "when", "where", "which",
631	    "who", "whom", "whose", "does", "do", "did", "is", "are", "was", "were",
632	    "will", "would", "can", "could", "should", "shall", "may", "might", "must",
633	    "has", "have", "had", "this", "that", "these", "those", "it", "its", "their",
634	    "our", "your", "his", "her", "into", "about", "over", "under", "between",
635	})
636	# Lowercase name-internal connectives allowed to BRIDGE a proper-noun run, but
637	# ONLY when the next token is itself a capitalized non-stopword ("Bank of
638	# England", "University of Toronto"). "and"/"or" are deliberately EXCLUDED so a
639	# list ("Ozempic and Mounjaro") splits into distinct entities instead of merging.
640	_ENTITY_CONNECTIVES: frozenset = frozenset({
641	    "of", "de", "von", "van", "der", "di", "da", "del", "du", "la", "le",
642	    "dos", "das", "ter",
643	})
644	
645	# One word-ish token: starts alnum, keeps internal &./'- (so "GLP-1", "U.S.",
646	# "S&P" survive as single tokens). Surrounding punctuation/quotes are dropped.
647	_ENTITY_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9&./'\-]*")
648	# Explicitly quoted spans (straight + curly) are trusted entity phrases verbatim.
649	_STRAIGHT_QUOTE_RE = re.compile(r'"([^"\n]{2,80})"')
650	_CURLY_QUOTE_RE = re.compile("[“]([^”\n]{2,80})[”]")
651	
652	# Fetch-width bounds are SHARED with the clinical lane (same lane family, same
653	# chokepoint): PG_REQUIRED_ENTITY_MAX_RESULTS_PER_QUERY / _MAX_SEED_URLS_PER_ENTITY.
654

656	def coverage_l5_enabled() -> bool:
657	    """True unless the L5 kill-switch is explicitly disabled (DEFAULT-ON, LAW VI).
658	
659	    ``PG_COVERAGE_L5_REQUIRED_ENTITY`` defaults ON; only an explicit
660	    ``0``/``false``/``no``/``off`` (case-insensitive) disables the lane, after
661	    which it is a byte-identical no-op. Blank/unset => ON.
662	    """
663	    return (os.getenv(_COVERAGE_L5_ENABLED_ENV, "1") or "1").strip().lower() not in (
664	        "0",
665	        "false",
666	        "no",
667	        "off",
668	    )
669	
670	
671	def _coverage_l5_max_entities() -> int:
672	    return _bounded_int_env(
673	        _COVERAGE_L5_MAX_ENTITIES_ENV, _DEFAULT_COVERAGE_L5_MAX_ENTITIES
674	    )
675

677	def _entity_tokens(text: str) -> list[str]:
678	    return _ENTITY_TOKEN_RE.findall(text or "")
679	
680	
681	def _quoted_spans(text: str) -> list[str]:
682	    """Return explicitly quoted spans (straight + curly). Trusted verbatim."""
683	    out: list[str] = []
684	    out.extend(_STRAIGHT_QUOTE_RE.findall(text or ""))
685	    out.extend(_CURLY_QUOTE_RE.findall(text or ""))
686	    return out
687	
688	
689	def _proper_noun_phrases(text: str, *, max_run_tokens: int = 6) -> list[str]:
690	    """Deterministic proper-noun phrase extraction (offline, no LLM).
691	
692	    A "run" is a maximal sequence of capitalized non-stopword tokens, bridged
693	    only by a lowercase name-internal connective that is immediately followed by
694	    another capitalized non-stopword token. Sentence-initial function words and
695	    Title-Cased articles break the run (they are stopwords, compared case-folded)
696	    so a question like "How does AI ..." yields "AI", not "How". Over-inclusion is
697	    harmless here — an extra derived entity only fires ONE bounded targeted query
698	    and can never credit coverage (verify is unchanged); under-merging (splitting
699	    "Ozempic and Mounjaro") is preferred over wrongly welding two distinct names.
700	    """
701	    toks = _entity_tokens(text)
702	    n = len(toks)
703	    phrases: list[str] = []
704	    i = 0
705	    while i < n:
706	        tok = toks[i]
707	        low = tok.lower()
708	        is_head = (
709	            tok[:1].isupper()
710	            and low not in _ENTITY_STOPWORDS
711	            and any(c.isalpha() for c in tok)
712	        )
713	        if not is_head:
714	            i += 1
715	            continue
716	        run = [tok]
717	        j = i + 1
718	        while j < n and len(run) < max_run_tokens:
719	            t = toks[j]
720	            tl = t.lower()
721	            if (
722	                t[:1].isupper()
723	                and tl not in _ENTITY_STOPWORDS
724	                and any(c.isalpha() for c in t)
725	            ):
726	                run.append(t)
727	                j += 1
728	                continue
729	            # connective bridge: lowercase name-internal word flanked by a
730	            # following capitalized non-stopword token (peek ahead).
731	            if (
732	                tl in _ENTITY_CONNECTIVES
733	                and (j + 1) < n
734	                and toks[j + 1][:1].isupper()
735	                and toks[j + 1].lower() not in _ENTITY_STOPWORDS
736	            ):
737	                run.append(t)
738	                j += 1
739	                continue
740	            break
741	        # Trim any trailing connective (defensive — the lookahead prevents it).
742	        while run and run[-1].lower() in _ENTITY_CONNECTIVES:
743	            run.pop()
744	        if run and any(len(r) >= 2 and any(c.isalpha() for c in r) for r in run):
745	            phrases.append(" ".join(run))
746	        i = j if j > i else i + 1
747	    return phrases
748

750	def _facet_text(facet: Any) -> str:
751	    """Extract the display text of one facet (Mapping / Facet-like / str)."""
752	    if isinstance(facet, Mapping):
753	        for key in ("name", "facet", "title", "label", "text"):
754	            value = facet.get(key)
755	            if isinstance(value, str) and value.strip():
756	                return value
757	        return ""
758	    name = getattr(facet, "name", None)
759	    if isinstance(name, str) and name.strip():
760	        return name
761	    if isinstance(facet, str):
762	        return facet
763	    return ""

766	def _explicit_facet_entities(facet: Any) -> list[str]:
767	    """Curated entity names carried on a facet Mapping (trusted verbatim)."""
768	    if not isinstance(facet, Mapping):
769	        return []
770	    out: list[str] = []
771	    for key in ("entities", "required_entities"):
772	        value = facet.get(key)
773	        if not isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
774	            continue
775	        for item in value:
776	            if isinstance(item, str):
777	                out.append(item)
778	            elif isinstance(item, Mapping):
779	                name = item.get("name") or item.get("label") or item.get("id")
780	                if isinstance(name, str):
781	                    out.append(name)
782	    return out

785	def extract_required_entities(
786	    research_question: str,
787	    facets: Optional[Sequence[Any]] = None,
788	    *,
789	    max_entities: Optional[int] = None,
790	) -> tuple[str, ...]:
791	    """Derive the required-entity set from the QUESTION and the FACET tree.
792	
793	    DETERMINISTIC / OFFLINE — no network, no LLM. The set is the union of:
794	
795	    1. Curated entity names carried explicitly on a facet Mapping
796	       (``entities`` / ``required_entities``) — trusted verbatim, added first.
797	    2. Proper-noun phrases + quoted spans in the research question.
798	    3. Proper-noun phrases + quoted spans in each facet's display text.
799	
800	    De-duplicated case-insensitively (first-appearance order preserved) and
801	    capped at ``max_entities`` (``PG_COVERAGE_L5_MAX_ENTITIES``, a compute-safety
802	    CEILING — §-1.3, never a breadth target). ``facets`` accepts a heterogeneous
803	    sequence of strings, :class:`~...expert_facet_planner.Facet` objects, or
804	    Mappings; unknown shapes are skipped (fail-safe).
805	    """
806	    cap = _coverage_l5_max_entities() if max_entities is None else max(0, max_entities)
807	    ordered: list[str] = []
808	    seen: set[str] = set()
809	
810	    def _add(term: Any) -> None:
811	        if not isinstance(term, str):
812	            return
813	        cleaned = " ".join(term.split()).strip().strip('"“”').strip()
814	        if len(cleaned) < 2:
815	            return
816	        key = cleaned.lower()
817	        if key in seen:
818	            return
819	        seen.add(key)
820	        ordered.append(cleaned)
821	
822	    # 1. explicit curated entity lists (highest confidence, added first)
823	    for facet in facets or ():
824	        for name in _explicit_facet_entities(facet):
825	            _add(name)
826	
827	    # 2. proper nouns + quoted spans from the research question
828	    for phrase in _proper_noun_phrases(research_question or ""):
829	        _add(phrase)
830	    for span in _quoted_spans(research_question or ""):
831	        _add(span)
832	
833	    # 3. proper nouns + quoted spans from each facet's display text
834	    for facet in facets or ():
835	        text = _facet_text(facet)
836	        if not text:
837	            continue
838	        for phrase in _proper_noun_phrases(text):
839	            _add(phrase)
840	        for span in _quoted_spans(text):
841	            _add(span)
842	
843	    return tuple(ordered[:cap])
844

846	@dataclass
847	class CoverageL5Result:
848	    """Outcome of one L5 lane invocation (for the manifest / audit trail).
849	
850	    ``evidence_rows`` are the NEWLY fetched corpus rows (real fetched URLs) the
851	    caller merges into ``evidence_for_gen`` (same dedup + evidence_id renumber
852	    the saturation gap-round + clinical lane use). ``gap_entities`` are the
853	    derived required entities for which the lane surfaced NO candidate source —
854	    they STAY gap disclosures (nothing injected, no fabrication). All counts are
855	    deterministic and the lane NEVER mutates its inputs.
856	    """
857	
858	    evidence_rows: list = field(default_factory=list)
859	    derived_entities: tuple = ()
860	    missing_entities: tuple = ()
861	    queries_by_entity: Mapping = field(default_factory=dict)
862	    gap_entities: tuple = ()
863	    seed_urls: tuple = ()
864	
865	
866	def run_l5_required_entity_coverage(
867	    *,
868	    research_question: str,
869	    facets: Optional[Sequence[Any]],
870	    corpus_texts: Sequence[str],
871	    search_fn: Callable[..., Any],
872	    retrieval_fn: Callable[..., Any],
873	    domains: Optional[Sequence[str]] = None,
874	) -> CoverageL5Result:
875	    """Run the COVERAGE lever L5 lane (DEFAULT-ON, self-gated).
876	
877	    Pipeline:
878	      1. DERIVE the required-entity set from ``research_question`` + ``facets``
879	         (:func:`extract_required_entities`).
880	      2. Keep ONLY the derived entities the corpus does not already mention
881	         (:func:`entity_covered_in_corpus`) — the lenient substring coverage
882	         signal that never over-fires for a present entity.
883	      3. For each still-missing entity, fire ONE research-question-anchored
884	         targeted ``search_fn`` query and COLLECT candidate URLs (bounded). An
885	         entity that surfaces ZERO candidates is recorded in ``gap_entities`` and
886	         STAYS a gap disclosure — nothing is injected for it.
887	      4. FETCH the collected URLs through ``retrieval_fn`` seed-only (same Zyte
888	         chokepoint, no Serper/S2 fan-out) and RETURN the fetched rows for the
889	         caller to merge. Rows carry their REAL fetched URLs and are NEVER keyed
890	         to an entity nor relabeled (provenance honesty — the operator-locked
891	         exact-equality coverage gate cannot be tricked).
892	
893	    ``domains`` defaults to an EMPTY bias (field-agnostic — L5 spans economics,
894	    technology, policy, ... so it must NOT bias to a clinical-authority set). The
895	    caller may pass a bias set. DEPENDENCY-INJECTED ``search_fn`` / ``retrieval_fn``
896	    keep this pure / offline-testable. Kill-switch OFF => empty no-op result.
897	    """
898	    if not coverage_l5_enabled():
899	        return CoverageL5Result()
900	
901	    derived = extract_required_entities(research_question, facets)
902	    if not derived:
903	        return CoverageL5Result(derived_entities=derived)
904	
905	    missing = tuple(
906	        entity
907	        for entity in derived
908	        if not entity_covered_in_corpus(entity, corpus_texts)
909	    )
910	    if not missing:
911	        return CoverageL5Result(derived_entities=derived, missing_entities=())
912	
913	    max_results = _bounded_int_env(
914	        _MAX_RESULTS_PER_QUERY_ENV, _DEFAULT_MAX_RESULTS_PER_QUERY
915	    )
916	    max_seed_urls = _bounded_int_env(
917	        _MAX_SEED_URLS_PER_ENTITY_ENV, _DEFAULT_MAX_SEED_URLS_PER_ENTITY
918	    )
919	    bias = list(domains or [])
920	    anchor = _bounded_anchor(research_question)
921	
922	    queries_by_entity: dict[str, str] = {}
923	    seed_urls: list[str] = []
924	    seen_seed: set[str] = set()
925	    gap_entities: list[str] = []
926	
927	    for entity in missing:
928	        query = _bounded_gap_query(anchor, entity)
929	        queries_by_entity[entity] = query
930	        try:
931	            hits = search_fn(query, domains=bias, max_results=max_results)
932	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — discovery is best-effort
933	            logger.warning("coverage_l5: search failed for entity=%r", entity)
934	            hits = None
935	        entity_urls: list[str] = []
936	        for hit in hits or []:
937	            url = _result_url(hit)
938	            if not url or url in seen_seed:
939	                continue
940	            seen_seed.add(url)
941	            entity_urls.append(url)
942	            if len(entity_urls) >= max_seed_urls:
943	                break
944	        if not entity_urls:
945	            # No candidate authoritative source surfaced -> the entity STAYS a
946	            # gap disclosure (never fabricated, never forced-covered).
947	            gap_entities.append(entity)
948	        seed_urls.extend(entity_urls)
949	
950	    if not seed_urls:
951	        return CoverageL5Result(
952	            evidence_rows=[],
953	            derived_entities=derived,
954	            missing_entities=missing,
955	            queries_by_entity=queries_by_entity,
956	            gap_entities=tuple(gap_entities),
957	            seed_urls=(),
958	        )
959	
960	    try:
961	        result = retrieval_fn(
962	            research_question=research_question,
963	            amplified_queries=[],
964	            seed_urls=list(seed_urls),
965	            seed_only=True,
966	            seed_source=COVERAGE_L5_SEED_SOURCE_LABEL,
967	            seed_query_origin=COVERAGE_L5_SEED_QUERY_ORIGIN,
968	            anchor_seed=False,
969	        )
970	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a failed fetch leaves the corpus as-is
971	        logger.warning(
972	            "coverage_l5: live-retrieval fetch raised for %d seed urls; "
973	            "leaving corpus unchanged",
974	            len(seed_urls),
975	        )
976	        return CoverageL5Result(
977	            evidence_rows=[],
978	            derived_entities=derived,
979	            missing_entities=missing,
980	            queries_by_entity=queries_by_entity,
981	            gap_entities=tuple(gap_entities),
982	            seed_urls=tuple(seed_urls),
983	        )
984	
985	    evidence_rows = list(getattr(result, "evidence_rows", []) or [])
986	    return CoverageL5Result(
987	        evidence_rows=evidence_rows,
988	        derived_entities=derived,
989	        missing_entities=missing,
990	        queries_by_entity=queries_by_entity,
991	        gap_entities=tuple(gap_entities),
992	        seed_urls=tuple(seed_urls),
993	    )

1	"""I-arch-007 ITEM 6 (#1264) — A15 resume FETCH-SHELL re-fetch (over-drop input fix).
2	
3	THE PROBLEM (the Q90 ``abort_excessive_gap`` 96% over-drop): a resume reloads the frozen
4	``corpus_snapshot`` with NO re-retrieval, so the V30 contract's REQUIRED T1 anchor rows (e.g.
5	SAE J3016, ALI product-liability, NTSB guidelines, UNECE ALKS, PACER docket) that were fetched as
6	EMPTY SHELLS on the crashed run are reloaded UNTOUCHED. The run log shows
7	``A15 refresh: N reloaded rows fetch-degraded (failed/shell/content-starved) ... detection-only``.
8	An empty cited span means the generator's claims about that anchor have nothing to ground against,
9	so ``strict_verify`` CORRECTLY drops them — that cascades into an over-drop abort.
10	
11	THE FIX — GET THE CONTENT, NEVER RELAX THE GATE: turn the A15 *detection-only* flag into an actual
12	RE-FETCH of the shell / failed / content-starved rows, routing paywalled/blocked anchors through the
13	existing AccessBypass cascade (which already includes the Zyte fallback when ``ZYTE_API_KEY`` is set).
14	When a re-fetch yields a usable (>= the refetch contract's 100-char) provenance quote that is NOT
15	itself content-starved, the row's ``direct_quote`` grounding is REPOPULATED and the degraded flags are
16	cleared, so ``strict_verify`` now has a REAL span to verify. A row that is STILL a shell after the
17	re-fetch stays flagged (disclosed), never fabricated.
18	
19	FAITHFULNESS-SAFE BY CONSTRUCTION: this module touches the INPUT only — it repopulates a row's fetched
20	span with freshly-fetched real content. It moves NO strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 / span-grounding /
21	section-floor / sentinel threshold. The re-fetched span flows through the UNCHANGED ``strict_verify``
22	exactly like any other fetched span; a row that cannot be re-grounded is left degraded (the gate then
23	honestly drops it). The master flag defaults OFF => no re-fetch => byte-identical resume.
24	"""
25	
26	from __future__ import annotations
27	
28	import dataclasses
29	import os
30	from typing import Any, Callable, Sequence
31	
32	# LAW VI: env-overridable, default OFF (unset => no re-fetch => byte-identical legacy resume).
33	_ENV_RESUME_REFETCH = "PG_RESUME_REFETCH_DEGRADED"
34	
35	# The grounding field strict_verify reads (``_build_provenance_quote`` writes it at
36	# live_retriever.py:4361). A successful re-fetch repopulates THIS field so the gate has a real span.
37	_GROUNDING_FIELD = "direct_quote"
38	
39	# The degraded-state flags the A15 detector set on the reloaded row. A successful re-fetch clears
40	# them so downstream telemetry/disclosure reflects the row is no longer a shell.
41	_DEGRADED_FLAGS = (

68	def refetch_degraded_resume_rows(
69	    degraded_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
70	    *,
71	    refetch_fn: Callable[[str], tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]],
72	    is_content_starved_fn: Callable[[str], bool],
73	    recovered_error_class_fn: Callable[[str], str] | None = None,
74	    log: Callable[[str], None] | None = None,
75	) -> dict[str, Any]:
76	    """Re-fetch each fetch-degraded resume row in place; repopulate its grounding span on success.
77	
78	    For every row in ``degraded_rows`` this calls ``refetch_fn(url)`` (the live-retriever
79	    ``refetch_for_extraction_with_diagnostics`` cascade — including the Zyte fallback). When the
80	    re-fetch returns a non-empty quote that is NOT content-starved AND is NOT a registry/error/block
81	    page (per ``recovered_error_class_fn`` — the SAME ``live_retriever._recovered_content_error_class``
82	    screen the live forced-Zyte adoption path uses), the row's ``direct_quote`` is REPOPULATED with the
83	    fresh span and the degraded flags are cleared (the row recovered). When the re-fetch yields nothing
84	    usable (still a shell, no URL, an error/registry/block page, or the cascade failed), the row is left
85	    flagged — it stays disclosed and the UNCHANGED ``strict_verify`` will honestly drop it (never a
86	    fabricated span). Mutates the row dicts in place; returns a per-call telemetry summary.
87	
88	    ``recovered_error_class_fn`` (optional injection, default None == legacy length-only path): a
89	    ``text -> class-token`` classifier (empty token == real content, ADOPT it). The production caller
90	    wires ``live_retriever._recovered_content_error_class`` so an 821-char doi.org "DOI Not Found"
91	    registry page (non-starved, real English prose) is NOT adopted as grounding and does NOT clear the
92	    degraded flags — closing the Codex P1 where a non-starved error page's flags were cleared, letting
93	    ``is_row_genuinely_recovered`` accept it and ``propagate_recovered_spans_to_frame_rows`` relabel a
94	    HOLLOW FrameRow to OPEN_ACCESS. §-1.3: refusing to adopt a fetch FAILURE as grounding is
95	    faithfulness-STRENGTHENING, not a source DROP — the row stays disclosed and flows through the
96	    UNCHANGED strict_verify.
97	
98	    Faithfulness-neutral: only the INPUT span is refreshed with real fetched content — no gate moves.
99	    Fail-LOUD per row (each recovery / residual-shell / error-page is logged) and fail-open overall (a
100	    re-fetch exception for one row never aborts the resume; that row stays flagged).
101	    """
102	    _log = log or (lambda _m: None)
103	    _error_class_fn = recovered_error_class_fn or (lambda _t: "")
104	    recovered: list[str] = []
105	    still_shell: list[str] = []
106	    error_page: list[str] = []
107	    no_url: list[str] = []
108	    errors: list[str] = []
109	
110	    for row in degraded_rows:
111	        if not isinstance(row, dict):
112	            continue
113	        eid = str(row.get("evidence_id", "") or "")
114	        url = _row_url(row)
115	        if not url:
116	            no_url.append(eid)
117	            continue
118	        try:
119	            quote, _diag = refetch_fn(url)
120	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — one row's re-fetch must never abort the resume
121	            errors.append(eid)
122	            _log(
123	                f"[resume]      A15 re-fetch ERROR for ev={eid} url={url[:80]}: "
124	                f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc} — row left flagged (gate will drop if unground-able)"
125	            )
126	            continue
127	        # A usable re-fetch must clear the refetch contract (non-empty, >=100 chars by construction in
128	        # refetch_for_extraction_with_diagnostics), the content-starvation heuristic, AND the
129	        # registry/error/block-page screen — so neither a shell that merely cleared 100 chars NOR a
130	        # non-starved error/registry page (e.g. an 821-char doi.org "DOI Not Found") is treated as
131	        # recovered. The error screen runs on the SAME classifier the live forced-Zyte adoption path
132	        # uses, so the resume path can never adopt a fetch FAILURE the live path would reject.
133	        if quote and not is_content_starved_fn(quote):
134	            try:
135	                _err_class = _error_class_fn(quote) or ""
136	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-OPEN: a screen error never rejects a real body
137	                _err_class = ""
138	                _log(
139	                    f"[resume]      A15 re-fetch error-screen raised for ev={eid} "
140	                    f"(fail-open, adopting the recovered body): {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
141	                )
142	            if _err_class:
143	                error_page.append(eid)
144	                _log(
145	                    f"[resume]      A15 re-fetch ERROR-PAGE ev={eid} url={url[:80]} "
146	                    f"(re-fetch returned a {_err_class} page — non-starved but a fetch FAILURE, "
147	                    "NOT grounding; degraded flags KEPT, row left flagged + disclosed; strict_verify "
148	                    "will honestly drop any ungrounded claim, NO fabrication)"
149	                )
150	                continue
151	            row[_GROUNDING_FIELD] = quote
152	            for _flag in _DEGRADED_FLAGS:
153	                if _flag in row:
154	                    row[_flag] = False
155	            recovered.append(eid)
156	            _log(
157	                f"[resume]      A15 re-fetch RECOVERED ev={eid} url={url[:80]} "
158	                f"(repopulated {_GROUNDING_FIELD} with {len(quote)} chars; "
159	                "flows through UNCHANGED strict_verify)"
160	            )
161	        else:
162	            still_shell.append(eid)
163	            _log(
164	                f"[resume]      A15 re-fetch STILL-SHELL ev={eid} url={url[:80]} "
165	                "(no usable content after the AccessBypass+Zyte cascade — row left flagged + "
166	                "disclosed; strict_verify will honestly drop any ungrounded claim, NO fabrication)"
167	            )
168	
169	    return {
170	        "attempted": len(degraded_rows),
171	        "recovered": recovered,
172	        "still_shell": still_shell,
173	        "error_page": error_page,
174	        "no_url": no_url,
175	        "errors": errors,
176	    }

204	def is_row_genuinely_recovered(row: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
205	    """True iff a reloaded contract row is a GENUINE A15 recovery safe to propagate as a span.
206	
207	    A row qualifies only when it (a) carries a non-empty grounding span AND (b) has NO residual
208	    A15 degraded flag still set. ``refetch_degraded_resume_rows`` clears ``_DEGRADED_FLAGS``
209	    (``content_starved`` / ``fetch_failed`` / ``landing_page`` / ``resume_refresh_pending``) ONLY on
210	    real recovery; a still-shell / un-refetched row keeps them. Without this guard a still-degraded
211	    shell whose leftover span merely clears the length floor (a paywall/landing-page notice) would be
212	    harvested as a "recovered" span and relabel a HOLLOW FrameRow to OPEN_ACCESS — a shell rendered as
213	    a real anchor (the P6 caller defect). Excluding it is faithfulness-STRENGTHENING (no fake anchor),
214	    not a source DROP: the degraded row itself stays in ``evidence_for_gen`` untouched and still flows
215	    through the UNCHANGED strict_verify, which honestly drops any ungrounded claim.
216	    """
217	    if not isinstance(row, dict):
218	        return False
219	    if not str(row.get(_GROUNDING_FIELD) or "").strip():
220	        return False
221	    for _flag in _DEGRADED_FLAGS:
222	        if bool(row.get(_flag)):
223	            return False
224	    return True
