227	def recovered_spans_from_reloaded_rows(
228	    evidence_for_gen: Sequence[Any],
229	    *,
230	    recovered_error_class_fn: Callable[[str], str] | None = None,
231	    log: Callable[[str], None] | None = None,
232	) -> dict[str, str]:
233	    """Build the ``{v30_entity_id -> recovered direct_quote}`` propagation map for the P6 resume path.
234	
235	    Mirrors the run_honest_sweep V30 caller but ONLY admits GENUINELY-recovered contract rows
236	    (``is_row_genuinely_recovered`` — a non-empty span AND every A15 degraded flag cleared). A
237	    reloaded row that is still a degraded shell — even one whose leftover span cleared the length
238	    floor — is EXCLUDED so it can never relabel a hollow FrameRow to OPEN_ACCESS. Fail-LOUD: any
239	    excluded non-empty-but-degraded shell is logged (never silently swallowed). Faithfulness-neutral:
240	    the excluded row stays in the corpus and flows through the UNCHANGED strict_verify.
241	
242	    CONTENT RE-SCREEN (the P6 propagation-map Codex BLOCKER): is_row_genuinely_recovered gates on
243	    FLAG-STATE alone. But the resume degraded-detector (run_honest_sweep_r3.py, the
244	    flags+is_content_starved selector) only ever FLAGS a starved/failed/landing row, so an UNFLAGGED,
245	    non-starved fetch-FAILURE registry/error page (e.g. an 821-char doi.org "DOI Not Found" page
246	    reloaded straight from the snapshot) is NEVER flagged, NEVER re-fetched, and so the error-page
247	    screen inside refetch_degraded_resume_rows never runs on it. Such a row passes the flag-only guard
248	    and would relabel a HOLLOW FrameRow to OPEN_ACCESS (a fetch FAILURE rendered as a real anchor).
249	    recovered_error_class_fn (the production caller wires the SAME live-path screen,
250	    live_retriever._recovered_content_error_class, NO new detector) RE-SCREENS the candidate span
251	    CONTENT here at the propagation-map build point, REGARDLESS of flag-state; a non-empty class token
252	    (a registry / error-shell / block page) is REJECTED from the map. Per DNA §-1.3: refusing to
253	    propagate a fetch FAILURE as a filled anchor is faithfulness-STRENGTHENING, not a source DROP, the
254	    row still stays in evidence_for_gen as a disclosed gap and flows through the UNCHANGED
255	    strict_verify. Default None == legacy no-screen (byte-identical); FAIL-OPEN, a screen exception
256	    never rejects a real body (it is adopted, with a loud warning).
257	    """
258	    _log = log or (lambda _m: None)
259	    _error_class_fn = recovered_error_class_fn or (lambda _t: "")
260	    out: dict[str, str] = {}
261	    skipped_degraded: list[str] = []
262	    skipped_error_page: list[str] = []
263	    for row in evidence_for_gen:
264	        if not isinstance(row, dict) or not row.get("v30_frame_row"):
265	            continue
266	        eid = str(row.get("v30_entity_id") or row.get("evidence_id") or "")
267	        if not eid:
268	            continue
269	        if is_row_genuinely_recovered(row):
270	            span = row[_GROUNDING_FIELD]
271	            # Re-screen the CONTENT with the same live-path error-page screen, regardless of
272	            # flag-state, so an unflagged/never-re-fetched fetch-FAILURE page is rejected here.
273	            try:
274	                _err_class = _error_class_fn(str(span or "")) or ""
275	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 fail-OPEN: a screen error never rejects a real body
276	                _err_class = ""
277	                _log(
278	                    f"[resume]      A15 P6: propagation-map error-screen raised for entity={eid} "
279	                    f"(fail-open, adopting the span): {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
280	                )
281	            if _err_class:
282	                skipped_error_page.append(f"{eid}({_err_class})")
283	                continue
284	            out[eid] = span
285	        elif str(row.get(_GROUNDING_FIELD) or "").strip():
286	            # Non-empty span but a residual A15 degraded flag is still set => still a shell, NOT a
287	            # recovery. Do NOT propagate (would fake a hollow anchor into OPEN_ACCESS). Disclose loudly.
288	            skipped_degraded.append(eid)
289	    if skipped_error_page:
290	        _log(
291	            "[resume]      A15 P6: EXCLUDED "
292	            f"{len(skipped_error_page)} reloaded contract row(s) from span propagation whose span "
293	            "CONTENT is a fetch-FAILURE registry/error/block page (re-screened regardless of "
294	            f"flag-state): {skipped_error_page[:10]} — a fetch FAILURE is NOT a recovery, the hollow "
295	            "anchor stays a disclosed gap (NO fabrication; the row still flows through UNCHANGED "
296	            "strict_verify)"
297	        )
298	    if skipped_degraded:
299	        _log(
300	            "[resume]      A15 P6: EXCLUDED "
301	            f"{len(skipped_degraded)} still-degraded reloaded contract row(s) from span "
302	            f"propagation (non-empty shell span but a residual degraded flag is set): "
303	            f"{skipped_degraded[:10]} — a shell is NOT a recovery, the hollow anchor stays a "
304	            "disclosed gap (NO fabrication; the row still flows through UNCHANGED strict_verify)"
305	        )
306	    return out

309	def propagate_recovered_spans_to_frame_rows(
310	    frame_rows: Sequence[Any],
311	    *,
312	    recovered_span_by_entity: dict[str, str],
313	    min_span_chars: int | None = None,
314	    log: Callable[[str], None] | None = None,
315	) -> tuple[tuple[Any, ...], dict[str, Any]]:
316	    """Propagate A15-recovered spans into HOLLOW V30 contract FrameRows on ``--resume``.
317	
318	    For each FrameRow whose fresh ``fetch_compiled_frame`` result is HOLLOW — a
319	    ``FRAME_GAP_UNRECOVERABLE`` row, OR a row whose ``direct_quote`` is shorter than the verifiable-
320	    span floor (the SAME predicate the contract runner's ``_frame_row_has_usable_quote`` uses to route
321	    a slot to gap-disclosure) — this looks up a recovered span in ``recovered_span_by_entity`` (keyed
322	    by the FrameRow ``entity_id`` == the reloaded contract row's ``v30_entity_id``). When a usable
323	    recovered span (non-empty AND >= the floor) exists, the FrameRow is rebuilt (``dataclasses.replace``
324	    on the frozen row) with ``direct_quote`` = the recovered span, ``provenance_class`` lifted to
325	    ``OPEN_ACCESS`` (it now carries real fetched content), and ``quote_source="a15_resume_refetch"``.
326	
327	    HOLLOW-ONLY + NO-CLOBBER: a FrameRow that already carries a usable span is returned UNCHANGED, so
328	    a fresh re-fetch that DID recover is never overwritten by a stale snapshot span. A ``HUMAN_CURATED``
329	    row (a PERMANENT provenance marker) is never overwritten. A hollow row with NO matching recovered
330	    span is returned unchanged — it stays a disclosed gap and the UNCHANGED ``strict_verify`` honestly
331	    drops any ungrounded claim (NO fabrication).
332	
333	    FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL BY CONSTRUCTION: this only refreshes a row's INPUT span with real recovered
334	    fetched content (the exact content ``refetch_degraded_resume_rows`` already re-grounded and that
335	    flows through the UNCHANGED strict_verify via ``register_frame_rows_into_evidence_pool`` + slot-fill).
336	    It moves NO gate/threshold. Empty ``recovered_span_by_entity`` => every row unchanged => byte-identical.
337	
338	    Returns ``(new_frame_rows_tuple, telemetry)`` where ``telemetry["propagated"]`` is the list of
339	    entity_ids whose hollow FrameRow was filled.
340	    """
341	    from .frame_fetcher import ProvenanceClass  # noqa: PLC0415 — lazy: keep module-top deps minimal
342	
343	    _log = log or (lambda _m: None)
344	    floor = _min_verifiable_span_chars() if min_span_chars is None else int(min_span_chars)
345	
346	    propagated: list[str] = []
347	    out: list[Any] = []
348	    for row in frame_rows:
349	        eid = str(getattr(row, "entity_id", "") or "")
350	        prov = getattr(row, "provenance_class", None)
351	        quote = str(getattr(row, "direct_quote", "") or "").strip()
352	
353	        # A HUMAN_CURATED marker is permanent — never relabel/overwrite it.
354	        if prov == ProvenanceClass.HUMAN_CURATED:
355	            out.append(row)
356	            continue
357	
358	        is_hollow = (
359	            prov == ProvenanceClass.FRAME_GAP_UNRECOVERABLE
360	            or len(quote) < floor
361	        )
362	        recovered_raw = recovered_span_by_entity.get(eid)
363	        recovered = str(recovered_raw or "").strip()
364	
365	        if is_hollow and recovered and len(recovered) >= floor:
366	            new_row = dataclasses.replace(
367	                row,
368	                direct_quote=recovered_raw,
369	                provenance_class=ProvenanceClass.OPEN_ACCESS,
370	                quote_source="a15_resume_refetch",
371	            )
372	            out.append(new_row)
373	            propagated.append(eid)
374	            _log(
375	                f"[resume]      A15 P6: filled HOLLOW V30 contract frame row entity={eid} "
376	                f"with {len(recovered)} chars recovered on resume "
377	                "(flows through UNCHANGED strict_verify; no gate moved)"
378	            )
379	        else:
380	            out.append(row)
381	
382	    return tuple(out), {
383	        "attempted": len(frame_rows),
384	        "propagated": propagated,
385	    }

1	"""Multi-round saturation search — I-meta-005 Phase 4 (#988). PURE decision logic.
2	
3	Phase 4 closes gap #3 (search depth): the single-pass retrieval becomes a
4	GAP-TARGETED SATURATION LOOP. When the Phase-3 plan-sufficiency gate returns
5	EXPAND, this module decides whether to fire another retrieval round for ONLY the
6	under-covered sub-questions, re-gate, and stop on gap-closure OR
7	marginal-novelty < epsilon OR round/budget exhaustion -> a PARTIAL report (not a
8	blind abort).
9	
10	KEY DESIGN (brief §2.1 / §2.2):
11	- Everything here is PURE / no-network / spend-free. The live retrieval round and
12	  the generator are INJECTED as callables by the sweep runner, so this orchestrator
13	  constructs NO HTTP client and bills NO generator token. The build + smoke drive
14	  the loop with STUB callables (capture-only, controlled per-round evidence).
15	- `marginal_novelty`: the fraction of a new round's rows that are NOVEL by
16	  canonical URL (the EXISTING `run_diff` canonicalizer over `source_url`), with
17	  intra-round duplicates collapsed.
18	- `gap_sub_queries`: the sub-query TEXTS to fire next, covering BOTH shortfall
19	  modes (empty-facet AND total-shortfall). NEVER empty when a section is
20	  under-covered (else the loop would have no query to fire).
21	- `saturation_decision`: a PRIORITY LADDER over {CONTINUE, STOP_SUFFICIENT,
22	  STOP_NOVELTY, STOP_BUDGET}. Budget is checked BEFORE novelty.
23	
24	NO `if domain ==` / NO clinical literal on this path — the loop targets the
25	plan's under-covered sub-queries (field-agnostic), never a domain.
26	"""
27	from __future__ import annotations

37	# identifier query params so `?abstract_id=123` vs `?abstract_id=456` stay

50	def canonical_source_url(url: str) -> str:
51	    """PUBLIC wrapper over `run_diff._normalize_url` (brief §2.1).
52	
53	    Single dedup identity for the saturation novelty metric. Importing the
54	    private `_normalize_url` directly is undesirable across modules, so this is
55	    the public seam — it delegates verbatim (no reimplementation).

60	def _row_source_url(row: Any) -> str:
61	    """Extract the row's canonical URL field (brief §2.1).
62	
63	    Live rows carry `source_url` (NOT `url`), `live_retriever.py:2221`. Accepts
64	    dict rows (the live shape) and falls back to object attributes defensively.
65	    """

68	    return getattr(row, "source_url", "") or getattr(row, "url", "") or ""

70	
71	def marginal_novelty(
72	    prev_evidence_rows: list[Any],
73	    new_round_rows: list[Any],
74	) -> float:
75	    """Fraction of `new_round_rows` that are NOVEL vs `prev_evidence_rows`.
76	
77	    Single dedup identity = canonical URL via `canonical_source_url`
78	    (run_diff canonicalizer over `source_url`). A new row is NOVEL iff its
79	    canonical URL is not already present in `prev_evidence_rows`. Intra-round
80	    duplicates collapse too — two rows in the SAME new round with the same
81	    canonical URL count as ONE novel (brief §2.1).
82	
83	    Returns `len(novel) / max(1, len(new_round_rows))`.
84	    """
85	    prev_canon: set[str] = set()
86	    for row in prev_evidence_rows or []:
87	        canon = canonical_source_url(_row_source_url(row))
88	        if canon:
89	            prev_canon.add(canon)
90	
91	    seen_this_round: set[str] = set()
92	    novel = 0
93	    for row in new_round_rows or []:
94	        canon = canonical_source_url(_row_source_url(row))
95	        # A row with no usable URL cannot be deduped — count it as novel once
96	        # only if its (empty) canon hasn't been seen this round.
97	        if canon in prev_canon or canon in seen_this_round:
98	            continue
99	        seen_this_round.add(canon)
100	        novel += 1
101	    return novel / max(1, len(new_round_rows or []))
102	
103	
104	def gap_sub_queries(sufficiency_report: Any, plan: Any) -> list[str]:
105	    """The sub-query TEXTS to fire next for the under-covered sections (brief §2.1).
106	
107	    Covers BOTH shortfall modes the Phase-3 gate fails on (`plan_sufficiency_gate
108	    .py:308`): `covered_count < evidence_target` OR `empty_facets`:
109	      - under-covered section with `empty_facets` -> the sub-query texts at those
110	        empty facet indices;
111	      - under-covered section with `covered_count < evidence_target` but NO empty
112	        facets (total shortfall) -> ALL the section's mapped sub-query texts (fire
113	        the whole section to raise total coverage).
114	
115	    Deduped (order-preserving), field-agnostic, derived from the plan. NEVER
116	    empty when a section is under-covered — else the loop would have no query to
117	    fire (the exact gap Codex flagged).
118	    """
119	    sub_queries = list(getattr(plan, "sub_queries", []) or [])
120	    n_sub = len(sub_queries)
121	    per_unit = list(getattr(sufficiency_report, "per_unit", []) or [])
122	
123	    out: list[str] = []
124	    seen: set[str] = set()
125	
126	    def _add(idx: int) -> None:
127	        if 0 <= idx < n_sub:
128	            text = sub_queries[idx]
129	            key = " ".join(str(text or "").split()).strip().lower()
130	            if text and key and key not in seen:
131	                seen.add(key)
132	                out.append(text)
133	
134	    for unit in per_unit:
135	        if getattr(unit, "sufficient", True):
136	            continue
137	        empty_facets = list(getattr(unit, "empty_facets", []) or [])
138	        mapped = list(getattr(unit, "sub_query_indices", []) or [])
139	        if empty_facets:
140	            # Empty-facet mode: fire ONLY the under-covered facets' texts.
141	            for idx in empty_facets:
142	                _add(idx)
143	        else:
144	            # Total-shortfall mode (covered < target, no empty facet): fire the
145	            # WHOLE section's mapped facets to raise total coverage.
146	            for idx in mapped:
147	                _add(idx)
148	    return out
149	
150	
151	def saturation_decision(
152	    *,
153	    verdict: str,
154	    round_index: int,
155	    max_rounds: int,
156	    novelty: float,
157	    eps: float,
158	) -> str:
159	    """Decide the next saturation action (brief §2.1). PRIORITY LADDER — order
160	    matters; budget is checked BEFORE novelty.
161	
162	    Returns one of {CONTINUE, STOP_SUFFICIENT, STOP_NOVELTY, STOP_BUDGET}.
163	
164	    - verdict == proceed -> STOP_SUFFICIENT (gap closed).
165	    - verdict == abort -> STOP_BUDGET (the Phase-3 gate returns `abort` when
166	      `round_index >= max_rounds`; an explicit terminal, never unhandled).
167	    - verdict == expand AND round_index+1 >= max_rounds -> STOP_BUDGET (rounds
168	      exhausted).
169	    - verdict == expand AND round_index >= 1 AND novelty < eps -> STOP_NOVELTY
170	      (the last round added < eps novel rows — the curve flattened).
171	    - else (expand, rounds left, novelty >= eps) -> CONTINUE.
172	    """
173	    if verdict == "proceed":
174	        return STOP_SUFFICIENT
175	    # abort is a terminal verdict (rounds/budget exhausted at the gate).
176	    if verdict == "abort":
177	        return STOP_BUDGET
178	    # From here: verdict == expand (or any non-proceed/non-abort -> treat as
179	    # expand-style; budget-bounded). Budget BEFORE novelty.
180	    if round_index + 1 >= max_rounds:
181	        return STOP_BUDGET
182	    if round_index >= 1 and novelty < eps:
183	        return STOP_NOVELTY
184	    return CONTINUE
185	
186	
187	# ── budget accounting (PRE-SPEND, worst-case) ────────────────────────────────
188	
189	def per_query_discovery_cost(adapter_count: int) -> int:
190	    """Worst-case DISCOVERY calls per gap query (brief §2.2, iter-5 P1 #1).
191	
192	    core Serper + core S2 (`live_retriever.py:1790`,`:1806`) = 2 calls/query,
193	    PLUS the need-type dispatcher which calls EACH routed adapter inside
194	    `for q in queries` (`domain_backends.py:660`) = `adapter_count` calls PER gap
195	    query. (Fetch + OpenAlex are bounded by `fetch_cap`, not multiplied per gap
196	    query — out of this cap's scope.)
197	    """
198	    return 2 + max(0, int(adapter_count))
199	
200	
201	@dataclass
202	class BudgetPreflight:
203	    """Result of the PRE-SPEND budget preflight for one expansion round."""
204	
205	    allowed_queries: list[str]
206	    fired_cost: int            # worst-case discovery calls this round will spend
207	    truncated: bool            # True iff gap queries were truncated to fit
208	    exhausted: bool            # True iff remaining budget cannot fund any query
209	
210	
211	def preflight_round_budget(
212	    *,
213	    gap_queries: list[str],
214	    cumulative_discovery_calls: int,
215	    max_discovery_calls: int,
216	    cost_per_query: int,
217	) -> BudgetPreflight:
218	    """PRE-SPEND truncation so a round's WORST-CASE discovery spend CANNOT push
219	    `cumulative_discovery_calls` over `max_discovery_calls` (brief §2.2, iter-3
220	    P1; INVARIANT P4-14).
221	
222	    `remaining = MAX - cumulative`; if `remaining <= 0` -> exhausted (no query may
223	    fire). Else the round may fire at most `floor(remaining / cost_per_query)` gap
224	    queries — TRUNCATE `gap_queries` to that many.
225	    """
226	    cost = max(1, int(cost_per_query))
227	    remaining = int(max_discovery_calls) - int(cumulative_discovery_calls)
228	    if remaining <= 0:
229	        return BudgetPreflight(
230	            allowed_queries=[], fired_cost=0, truncated=bool(gap_queries),
231	            exhausted=True,
232	        )
233	    max_fire = remaining // cost
234	    if max_fire <= 0:
235	        # Remaining budget cannot fund even one gap query at worst-case cost.
236	        return BudgetPreflight(
237	            allowed_queries=[], fired_cost=0, truncated=bool(gap_queries),
238	            exhausted=True,
239	        )
240	    allowed = list(gap_queries[:max_fire])
241	    return BudgetPreflight(
242	        allowed_queries=allowed,
243	        fired_cost=len(allowed) * cost,
244	        truncated=len(allowed) < len(gap_queries),
245	        exhausted=False,
246	    )
247	
248	
249	# ── the injectable saturation orchestrator ───────────────────────────────────
250	
251	@dataclass
252	class RoundOutcome:
253	    """One saturation round's outputs from the injected `run_round_fn`.
254	
255	    The orchestrator stays client-free: the runner supplies a closure that does
256	    the actual retrieval -> select -> [V30/upload inject] -> gate; the smoke
257	    supplies a capture-only stub with controlled per-round evidence.
258	    """
259	
260	    # Cumulative RETRIEVED corpus rows (for the novelty metric; `source_url`).
261	    cumulative_retrieved_rows: list[Any]
262	    # The BILLED generator-visible set Phase 3 certifies (post-select+V30+upload).
263	    evidence_for_gen: list[Any]
264	    # The PlanSufficiencyReport for THIS round's billed set.
265	    sufficiency_report: Any
266	    # The RAW rows THIS round RETRIEVED (the novelty DENOMINATOR) -- INCLUDES
267	    # canonical-URL duplicates already present in the corpus, so the novelty
268	    # fraction can fall below 1.0 as later rounds re-fetch the same sources.
269	    # NOT the deduped additions: handing the deduped set here makes novelty
270	    # degenerate (always 1.0 unless a round adds exactly zero rows), so the
271	    # `novelty < eps` flatten stop would never fire below 1.0. Round 0 = all of
272	    # round 0 (no prior corpus, so every row is novel).
273	    new_round_rows: list[Any] = field(default_factory=list)
274	    # The cumulative RETRIEVED corpus snapshot taken BEFORE this round (the
275	    # novelty BASELINE a round's raw rows are compared against). Round 0 =
276	    # empty (no prior corpus). Carried explicitly so the loop never has to
277	    # reconstruct "prior corpus" by object-identity subtraction (which breaks
278	    # once `new_round_rows` holds raw rows not all present in the corpus).
279	    prev_corpus_rows: list[Any] = field(default_factory=list)
280	
281	
282	@dataclass
283	class SaturationResult:
284	    """Terminal state of the saturation loop."""
285	
286	    decision: str                 # STOP_SUFFICIENT | STOP_NOVELTY | STOP_BUDGET
287	    rounds_fired: int             # number of EXPANSION rounds fired (round 0 excl.)
288	    final_round: RoundOutcome
289	    cumulative_discovery_calls: int
290	    novelty_trajectory: list[float] = field(default_factory=list)
291	    truncated_any_round: bool = False
292	
293	
294	def run_saturation_loop(
295	    *,
296	    round0: RoundOutcome,
297	    run_round_fn: Callable[[list[str]], RoundOutcome],
298	    max_rounds: int,
299	    novelty_eps: float,
300	    max_discovery_calls: int,
301	    cost_per_query: int,
302	    plan: Any,
303	    log: Optional[Callable[[str], None]] = None,
304	) -> SaturationResult:
305	    """Drive the multi-round saturation loop (brief §2.2). PURE — constructs NO
306	    HTTP client; the live retrieval round is the INJECTED `run_round_fn`.
307	
308	    Args:
309	        round0: the already-executed round-0 outcome (today's single-pass
310	            retrieval -> select -> [V30/upload] -> gate). Round 0 is the
311	            un-truncatable baseline; its spend is NOT charged against the
312	            expansion-round budget (which starts at 0).
313	        run_round_fn: `(gap_queries) -> RoundOutcome`. The runner's closure fires
314	            a GAP-ONLY retrieval round (anchor-suppressed), merges with global
315	            evidence_id renumber, re-selects, re-injects V30/upload, re-gates.
316	        max_rounds: `PG_SATURATION_MAX_ROUNDS` — bounds total rounds (incl. 0).
317	        novelty_eps: `PG_SATURATION_NOVELTY_EPS` — epsilon for the flatten stop.
318	        max_discovery_calls: `PG_SATURATION_MAX_RETRIEVAL_CALLS` — the cumulative
319	            EXPANSION-round discovery-call cap (round 0 excluded).
320	        cost_per_query: worst-case `per_query_discovery_cost(adapter_count)`.
321	        plan: the pinned `ResearchPlan` (for `gap_sub_queries`).
322	        log: optional line logger.
323	
324	    Returns SaturationResult with the terminal decision + the final round.
325	    """
326	    _log = log or (lambda _m: None)
327	    round_index = 0
328	    current = round0
329	    cumulative_calls = 0            # EXPANSION-round discovery spend only.
330	    novelty_trajectory: list[float] = []
331	    truncated_any = False
332	
333	    while True:
334	        verdict = str(getattr(current.sufficiency_report, "verdict", "abort"))
335	        # Novelty of THE ROUND JUST RUN vs the cumulative corpus BEFORE it. For
336	        # round 0 there is no prior corpus, so novelty is definitionally 1.0 and
337	        # the round>=1 guard in `saturation_decision` ignores it anyway. For
338	        # expansion rounds the baseline is the round's OWN `prev_corpus_rows`
339	        # snapshot (the corpus as it stood BEFORE the round) and the denominator
340	        # is the RAW retrieved `new_round_rows` (incl. duplicates) -- so a round
341	        # that re-fetches mostly-seen sources reads a LOW novelty fraction and
342	        # the `< eps` flatten stop can actually fire. Reconstructing the prior
343	        # corpus by `cumulative - new_round_rows` would be wrong now that
344	        # `new_round_rows` holds raw rows not all present in the corpus.
345	        if round_index == 0:
346	            novelty = 1.0
347	        else:
348	            novelty = marginal_novelty(
349	                current.prev_corpus_rows, current.new_round_rows
350	            )
351	        novelty_trajectory.append(novelty)
352	
353	        decision = saturation_decision(
354	            verdict=verdict,
355	            round_index=round_index,
356	            max_rounds=max_rounds,
357	            novelty=novelty,
358	            eps=novelty_eps,
359	        )
360	        _log(
361	            f"[saturation]  round={round_index} verdict={verdict} "
362	            f"novelty={novelty:.3f} -> {decision}"
363	        )
364	
365	        if decision != CONTINUE:
366	            return SaturationResult(
367	                decision=decision,
368	                rounds_fired=round_index,
369	                final_round=current,
370	                cumulative_discovery_calls=cumulative_calls,
371	                novelty_trajectory=novelty_trajectory,
372	                truncated_any_round=truncated_any,
373	            )
374	
375	        # CONTINUE -> fire round N+1, GAP-ONLY. Compute the gap queries from the
376	        # CURRENT round's sufficiency report, then PRE-SPEND budget-truncate.
377	        gaps = gap_sub_queries(current.sufficiency_report, plan)
378	        if not gaps:
379	            # Defensive: a non-proceed verdict with no gap query to fire would
380	            # spin the loop. Treat as budget-terminal (nothing actionable left).
381	            _log(
382	                "[saturation]  no gap sub-queries for under-covered "
383	                "section(s); terminating as STOP_BUDGET"
384	            )
385	            return SaturationResult(
386	                decision=STOP_BUDGET,
387	                rounds_fired=round_index,
388	                final_round=current,
389	                cumulative_discovery_calls=cumulative_calls,
390	                novelty_trajectory=novelty_trajectory,
391	                truncated_any_round=truncated_any,
392	            )
393	
394	        preflight = preflight_round_budget(
395	            gap_queries=gaps,
396	            cumulative_discovery_calls=cumulative_calls,
397	            max_discovery_calls=max_discovery_calls,
398	            cost_per_query=cost_per_query,
399	        )
400	        truncated_any = truncated_any or preflight.truncated
401	        if preflight.exhausted:
402	            _log(
403	                "[saturation]  retrieval budget exhausted "
404	                f"(cumulative={cumulative_calls}/{max_discovery_calls}); "
405	                "STOP_BUDGET before firing"
406	            )
407	            return SaturationResult(
408	                decision=STOP_BUDGET,
409	                rounds_fired=round_index,
410	                final_round=current,
411	                cumulative_discovery_calls=cumulative_calls,
412	                novelty_trajectory=novelty_trajectory,
413	                truncated_any_round=truncated_any,
414	            )
415	
416	        # Charge the WORST-CASE spend PRE-SPEND so the cumulative counter can
417	        # NEVER exceed MAX (INVARIANT P4-14), then fire the round.
418	        cumulative_calls += preflight.fired_cost
419	        _log(
420	            f"[saturation]  firing round {round_index + 1} with "
421	            f"{len(preflight.allowed_queries)} gap queries "
422	            f"(worst-case +{preflight.fired_cost} calls; "
423	            f"cumulative={cumulative_calls}/{max_discovery_calls})"
424	        )
425	        current = run_round_fn(preflight.allowed_queries)
426	        round_index += 1
427

1	"""I-scope-001 — per-SOURCE scope-facet classifier (deterministic, offline).
2	
3	The flexible scope+timeline gate weights/masks sources by SCOPE facet (source-type /
4	jurisdiction) — orthogonal to the T1-T7 CREDIBILITY tier and to the DOCUMENT GENRE. This
5	module answers "which scope facets does THIS source belong to?" using the extensible
6	ontology in ``config/scope_ontology/source_types.yaml``.
7	
8	It REUSES the ``document_type_classifier`` primitives (``_host`` / ``_host_in`` /
9	``_doi_candidate`` / ``classify_document_type`` / ``is_peer_reviewed_journal_article``) as
10	signal providers — the ``peer_reviewed_journal`` facet IS
11	``is_peer_reviewed_journal_article(classify_document_type(...)[0])`` (single source of truth,
12	no duplicate journal logic). The NEW facets (law/patent/gov/central-bank/clinical/analyst/
13	social/standards) resolve via ontology host / DOI-registrant / url-path / genre matchers.
14	
15	DNA §-1.3: this LABELS a source with its scope facets and hands back a WEIGHT. It drops
16	NOTHING and never touches the faithfulness engine. Fail-open: an unresolved source yields an
17	empty facet set + ``basis="unresolved"`` => neutral weight 1.0 (never punished). Pure / no
18	network. All knobs config-driven (LAW VI).
19	"""
20	from __future__ import annotations
21

23	import os
24	from functools import lru_cache
25	from pathlib import Path
26	from typing import Any, Mapping, Optional

33	    is_peer_reviewed_journal_article,
34	)

39	_DEFAULT_ONTOLOGY_REL = "config/scope_ontology/source_types.yaml"

69	@lru_cache(maxsize=4)
70	def _load_ontology_cached(path_str: str, mtime: float) -> dict[str, Any]:
71	    """Cached YAML load keyed on (path, mtime) so an edited ontology is re-read. Fail-soft:
72	    a missing/broken file yields a minimal ontology carrying only the default lexicons."""
73	    fallback: dict[str, Any] = {
74	        "facets": [],
75	        "jurisdictions": {},
76	        "jurisdiction_synonym_suffixes": ["sources", "source", "studies", "research"],
77	        "jurisdiction_host_tlds": {},
78	        "op_lexicon": dict(_DEFAULT_OP_LEXICON),
79	        "default_out_of_scope_weight": 0.5,
80	    }
81	    try:
82	        import yaml  # noqa: PLC0415
83	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - pyyaml is a project dep; fail-soft regardless
84	        logger.warning("[scope_ontology] PyYAML unavailable; using default lexicons only")
85	        return fallback
86	    p = Path(path_str)
87	    if not p.is_file():
88	        logger.warning("[scope_ontology] ontology not found at %s; default lexicons only", p)
89	        return fallback
90	    try:
91	        data = yaml.safe_load(p.read_text(encoding="utf-8")) or {}
92	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - malformed YAML must never abort a run
93	        logger.warning("[scope_ontology] failed to parse %s (%s); default lexicons", p, exc)
94	        return fallback
95	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
96	        return fallback
97	    data.setdefault("facets", [])
98	    data.setdefault("jurisdictions", {})
99	    data.setdefault("jurisdiction_synonym_suffixes",
100	                    ["sources", "source", "studies", "research"])
101	    data.setdefault("jurisdiction_host_tlds", {})
102	    _ol = data.get("op_lexicon")
103	    if not isinstance(_ol, dict) or not _ol:
104	        data["op_lexicon"] = dict(_DEFAULT_OP_LEXICON)
105	    data.setdefault("default_out_of_scope_weight", 0.5)
106	    return data

109	def load_scope_ontology(path: "str | Path | None" = None) -> dict[str, Any]:

114	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001

119	def facet_default_weight(facet_id: str, ontology: dict[str, Any]) -> float:
120	    """The (0,1] out-of-scope demote for a source NOT in ``facet_id`` when the facet is
121	    'prefer'-requested at weight strictness. Per-facet override else the ontology default."""
122	    for f in ontology.get("facets", []):
123	        if isinstance(f, dict) and str(f.get("id")) == facet_id:
124	            try:
125	                w = float(f.get("default_out_of_scope_weight"))
126	                if 0.0 < w <= 1.0:
127	                    return w
128	            except (TypeError, ValueError):
129	                break
130	    try:
131	        w = float(ontology.get("default_out_of_scope_weight", 0.5))
132	        return w if 0.0 < w <= 1.0 else 0.5
133	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
134	        return 0.5

137	def _field(source: "Any", *names: str) -> "Any":
138	    if isinstance(source, Mapping):
139	        for n in names:
140	            if n in source and source.get(n) is not None:
141	                return source.get(n)
142	        return None
143	    for n in names:
144	        v = getattr(source, n, None)
145	        if v is not None:
146	            return v
147	    return None

154	def _matches_facet(
155	    facet: dict[str, Any], *, url: str, low_url: str, host: str, doi: str, genre_value: str
156	) -> Optional[str]:
157	    """Return a basis string iff the source matches this facet's matchers, else None."""
158	    matchers = facet.get("matchers") or {}
159	    if not isinstance(matchers, dict):
160	        return None
161	    # journal facet — single source of truth via the genre classifier.
162	    if matchers.get("journal"):
163	        # handled by the caller (needs the DocumentType, not just its value string)
164	        return None
165	    hosts = matchers.get("hosts") or []
166	    if host and hosts:
167	        try:
168	            if _host_in(host, frozenset(str(h).lower() for h in hosts)):
169	                return f"host:{host}"
170	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
171	            pass
172	        # substring host suffix fallback (e.g. bare 'gov' TLD family)
173	        for h in hosts:
174	            hl = str(h).lower()
175	            if hl and (host == hl or host.endswith("." + hl) or host.endswith(hl)):
176	                return f"host:{hl}"
177	    url_patterns = matchers.get("url_patterns") or []
178	    for pat in url_patterns:
179	        if pat and str(pat).lower() in low_url:
180	            return f"url:{str(pat).lower()}"
181	    doi_prefixes = matchers.get("doi_prefixes") or []
182	    if doi:
183	        for pre in doi_prefixes:
184	            if pre and doi.startswith(str(pre).lower()):
185	                return f"doi:{str(pre).lower()}"
186	    want_genre = matchers.get("genre")
187	    if want_genre and genre_value and str(want_genre).upper() == genre_value.upper():
188	        return f"genre:{genre_value}"
189	    return None
190	
191	
192	def _jurisdiction_facets(host: str, source: "Any", ontology: dict[str, Any]) -> set[str]:
193	    out: set[str] = set()
194	    tlds = ontology.get("jurisdiction_host_tlds") or {}
195	    for suffix, iso in tlds.items():
196	        s = str(suffix).lower()
197	        if host and (host.endswith(s)):
198	            out.add(f"jurisdiction:{iso}")
199	    country = _field(source, "country", "country_code", "geo")
200	    if country:
201	        c = str(country).strip().upper()
202	        if 2 <= len(c) <= 3:
203	            out.add(f"jurisdiction:{c}")
204	    return out
205	
206	
207	def classify_source_facets(
208	    source: "Any", ontology: "dict[str, Any] | None" = None
209	) -> tuple[set[str], str]:
210	    """Classify a source into its scope facet ids (deterministic, offline).
211	
212	    Returns ``(facet_ids, basis)``. Empty set + ``basis='unresolved'`` on a source with no
213	    resolvable signal (fail-open => neutral weight). Multi-signal per facet — OpenAlex
214	    over-marks ~99% as "article", so genre alone is unreliable; the ontology host / DOI /
215	    url-path matchers carry the weight (same discipline the genre classifier uses)."""
216	    ont = ontology if ontology is not None else load_scope_ontology()
217	    url = _source_url(source)
218	    low_url = url.lower()
219	    host = _host(url)
220	    doi = _doi_candidate(str(_field(source, "doi") or ""), low_url)
221	    title = str(_field(source, "title") or "")
222	
223	    # Reuse the genre classifier once (peer_reviewed_journal facet + genre matchers).
224	    try:
225	        dt, _dt_basis = classify_document_type(
226	            openalex_publication_type=str(_field(source, "openalex_publication_type") or ""),
227	            openalex_source_type=str(_field(source, "openalex_source_type") or ""),
228	            source_class=str(_field(source, "source_class") or ""),
229	            url=url,
230	            title=title,
231	            doi=str(_field(source, "doi") or ""),
232	        )
233	        genre_value = dt.value
234	        is_journal = is_peer_reviewed_journal_article(dt)
235	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - never let a classifier error abort scope
236	        genre_value = ""
237	        is_journal = False
238	
239	    facets: set[str] = set()
240	    bases: list[str] = []
241	    for facet in ont.get("facets", []):
242	        if not isinstance(facet, dict):
243	            continue
244	        fid = str(facet.get("id") or "")
245	        if not fid:
246	            continue
247	        matchers = facet.get("matchers") or {}
248	        if isinstance(matchers, dict) and matchers.get("journal"):
249	            if is_journal:
250	                facets.add(fid)
251	                bases.append(f"{fid}=journal_classifier")
252	            continue
253	        basis = _matches_facet(
254	            facet, url=url, low_url=low_url, host=host, doi=doi, genre_value=genre_value
255	        )
256	        if basis:
257	            facets.add(fid)
258	            bases.append(f"{fid}={basis}")
259	
260	    facets |= _jurisdiction_facets(host, source, ont)
261	
262	    if not facets:
263	        return (set(), "unresolved")
264	    return (facets, "; ".join(bases[:8]) or "matched")
265	
266	
267	def resolve_scope_weight(
268	    source: "Any",
269	    requested_facet_id: str,
270	    ontology: "dict[str, Any] | None" = None,
271	) -> float:
272	    """The (0,1] scope weight for a source under a single 'prefer'/weight facet request:
273	    1.0 when the source IS in the requested facet, else the facet's out-of-scope demote."""
274	    ont = ontology if ontology is not None else load_scope_ontology()
275	    facets, _ = classify_source_facets(source, ont)
276	    if requested_facet_id in facets:
277	        return 1.0
278	    return facet_default_weight(requested_facet_id, ont)
279

1	"""I-wire-001 W3 — Weighted Reciprocal Rank Fusion (WRRF) for search candidates.
2	
3	This module implements the W3 winner: fuse the per-engine RANKED candidate
4	lists on their original ranks BEFORE URL-dedup, so a high-authority source
5	(e.g. NEJM/Lancet) that one engine buries below marketing/junk is lifted by
6	its strong rank in another engine.
7	
8	DETERMINISTIC, NO LLM. WRRF is a closed-form scoring function over integer
9	ranks, so the fused order is fully reproducible (standard point 15). It runs
10	on CPU — there is no GPU benefit for a rank arithmetic step (standard point 2).
11	
12	Semantics (CLAUDE.md §-1.3 weight-not-filter): fusion is an ORDERING / WEIGHT.
13	It NEVER hard-drops a source. URL-dedup keeps every distinct URL exactly once
14	(the union of all engine lists); fusion only decides the ORDER in which they
15	flow downstream. A source that ranks low is down-ranked, never deleted.
16	
17	Algorithm (weighted reciprocal rank fusion, Cormack et al. 2009 RRF with
18	per-engine weights):
19	
20	    score(url) = sum over engines e that returned url of:
21	                     weight(e) / (k + rank_e(url))
22	
23	where rank_e(url) is the 1-based position of url in engine e's returned list
24	and k is the RRF smoothing constant. Fused candidates are sorted by score
25	descending; ties broken deterministically by (first-seen engine order, url)
26	so the output is stable across runs.
27	
28	All parameters are env knobs (LAW VI): the smoothing constant
29	``PG_SEARCH_FUSION_WRRF_K`` and the per-engine weights
30	``PG_SEARCH_FUSION_WRRF_WEIGHTS`` (a comma list of ``engine:weight`` pairs;
31	any engine not listed uses ``PG_SEARCH_FUSION_WRRF_DEFAULT_WEIGHT``).
32	"""
33	from __future__ import annotations
34	
35	import logging
36	import os
37	from dataclasses import dataclass
38	from typing import Any
39	
40	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.search_fusion_wrrf")
41	
42	# RRF smoothing constant default (Cormack et al. 2009 use 60). A larger k
43	# flattens the contribution of rank position; a smaller k sharpens the head.
44	_DEFAULT_K = 60.0
45	# Per-engine weight applied to an engine not named in the weights env knob.
46	_DEFAULT_ENGINE_WEIGHT = 1.0
47	
48	
49	def wrrf_enabled() -> bool:
50	    """True iff the W3 search-fusion-WRRF flag is ON.
51	
52	    Default OFF: the legacy inline-dedup / RRF-free merge path runs unchanged
53	    and the candidate ordering is byte-identical to before this wiring.
54	    """
55	    return os.getenv("PG_SEARCH_FUSION_WRRF", "").strip().lower() in {
56	        "1", "true", "yes", "on",
57	    }
58	
59	
60	def _wrrf_k() -> float:
61	    raw = os.getenv("PG_SEARCH_FUSION_WRRF_K", "").strip()
62	    if not raw:
63	        return _DEFAULT_K
64	    try:
65	        val = float(raw)
66	    except ValueError:
67	        logger.warning(
68	            "[wrrf] PG_SEARCH_FUSION_WRRF_K=%r is not a float — using %.1f",
69	            raw, _DEFAULT_K,
70	        )
71	        return _DEFAULT_K
72	    # A non-positive k would divide by <=rank, exploding/negating scores; clamp
73	    # loudly to the default rather than silently corrupt the ordering.
74	    if val <= 0:
75	        logger.warning(
76	            "[wrrf] PG_SEARCH_FUSION_WRRF_K=%r must be > 0 — using %.1f",
77	            raw, _DEFAULT_K,
78	        )
79	        return _DEFAULT_K
80	    return val
81	
82	
83	def _default_engine_weight() -> float:
84	    raw = os.getenv("PG_SEARCH_FUSION_WRRF_DEFAULT_WEIGHT", "").strip()
85	    if not raw:
86	        return _DEFAULT_ENGINE_WEIGHT
87	    try:
88	        val = float(raw)
89	    except ValueError:
90	        return _DEFAULT_ENGINE_WEIGHT
91	    return val if val >= 0 else _DEFAULT_ENGINE_WEIGHT
92	
93	
94	def _engine_weights() -> dict[str, float]:
95	    """Parse ``PG_SEARCH_FUSION_WRRF_WEIGHTS`` into an engine->weight map.
96	
97	    Format: ``serper:1.0,openalex:1.2,europe_pmc:1.2,s2:1.1`` — academic /
98	    registry engines can be weighted above generic web search so a NEJM hit
99	    that openalex ranks #1 outweighs a marketing page serper ranks #1. Any
100	    engine absent from the map uses the default engine weight. Malformed
101	    entries are skipped loudly (never crash the merge).
102	    """
103	    weights: dict[str, float] = {}
104	    raw = os.getenv("PG_SEARCH_FUSION_WRRF_WEIGHTS", "").strip()
105	    if not raw:
106	        return weights
107	    for part in raw.split(","):
108	        part = part.strip()
109	        if not part or ":" not in part:
110	            continue
111	        name, _, val = part.partition(":")
112	        name = name.strip().lower()
113	        try:
114	            weights[name] = float(val.strip())
115	        except ValueError:
116	            logger.warning("[wrrf] skipping malformed weight entry %r", part)
117	    return weights
118	
119	
120	def _url_of(cand: Any) -> str:
121	    url = getattr(cand, "url", "")
122	    return url if isinstance(url, str) else ""
123	
124	
125	@dataclass
126	class WrrfResult:
127	    """Outcome of a WRRF fusion pass (telemetry for the run manifest)."""
128	
129	    fused: list[Any]
130	    # url -> fused score, for the highest-visibility console event + audit.
131	    scores: dict[str, float]
132	    # engine -> count of candidates that engine contributed (pre-dedup).
133	    per_engine_counts: dict[str, int]
134	    # number of distinct URLs in the fused output.
135	    n_unique: int
136	    k_used: float
137	    weights_used: dict[str, float]
138	
139	
140	def wrrf_fuse(
141	    per_engine_lists: dict[str, list[Any]],
142	    *,
143	    k: float | None = None,
144	    weights: dict[str, float] | None = None,
145	) -> WrrfResult:
146	    """Fuse per-engine RANKED candidate lists into one deterministic order.
147	
148	    Args:
149	        per_engine_lists: engine-name -> that engine's candidates in the order
150	            the engine returned them (rank = 1-based index). Each candidate is
151	            any object exposing a ``.url`` attribute (e.g. SearchCandidate).
152	        k: RRF smoothing constant override (defaults to the env knob).
153	        weights: engine->weight override (defaults to the env knob).
154	
155	    Returns:
156	        WrrfResult whose ``fused`` is the deduped, WRRF-ordered candidate list.
157	        The FIRST-SEEN candidate object per URL is retained (engines are
158	        processed in the dict's insertion order, which mirrors the search
159	        loop's engine order, so the retained object is stable). NO URL is
160	        dropped — every distinct URL in the union appears exactly once.
161	    """
162	    if k is None:
163	        k = _wrrf_k()
164	    if weights is None:
165	        weights = _engine_weights()
166	    default_w = _default_engine_weight()
167	
168	    scores: dict[str, float] = {}
169	    # First-seen candidate object + first-seen ordinal per URL (for stable tie
170	    # break). The ordinal is a global monotonically-increasing counter across
171	    # engines in their processing order.
172	    first_seen: dict[str, Any] = {}
173	    first_ordinal: dict[str, int] = {}
174	    per_engine_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
175	    weights_used: dict[str, float] = {}
176	    ordinal = 0
177	
178	    for engine, cands in per_engine_lists.items():
179	        # I-wire-001 W3 (#1310) P1-3: the live retriever namespaces domain/need
180	        # backend engines as ``domain:<name>`` / ``need:<name>`` to avoid colliding
181	        # with the main-loop engines (serper/s2/openalex). Look up the configured
182	        # PG_SEARCH_FUSION_WRRF_WEIGHTS by the FULL key first, then fall back to the
183	        # part AFTER a ``<ns>:`` prefix — so a weight entry ``europe_pmc:1.2``
184	        # matches the ``domain:europe_pmc`` / ``need:europe_pmc`` engine (else the
185	        # academic backends the weights are FOR would silently get the default).
186	        _ekey = engine.lower()
187	        if _ekey in weights:
188	            w = weights[_ekey]
189	        elif ":" in _ekey and _ekey.split(":", 1)[1] in weights:
190	            w = weights[_ekey.split(":", 1)[1]]
191	        else:
192	            w = default_w
193	        weights_used[engine] = w
194	        per_engine_counts[engine] = len(cands)
195	        for rank0, cand in enumerate(cands):
196	            url = _url_of(cand)
197	            if not url:
198	                continue
199	            rank = rank0 + 1  # 1-based rank
200	            scores[url] = scores.get(url, 0.0) + (w / (k + rank))
201	            if url not in first_seen:
202	                first_seen[url] = cand
203	                first_ordinal[url] = ordinal
204	                ordinal += 1
205	
206	    # Sort by score DESC, then by first-seen ordinal ASC (stable, deterministic)
207	    fused_urls = sorted(
208	        first_seen.keys(),
209	        key=lambda u: (-scores[u], first_ordinal[u], u),
210	    )
211	    fused = [first_seen[u] for u in fused_urls]
212	
213	    n_backends = len(per_engine_counts)
214	    logger.info("[wrrf] fused %d backends -> %d ranked candidates", n_backends, len(fused))
215	
216	    return WrrfResult(
217	        fused=fused,
218	        scores=scores,
219	        per_engine_counts=per_engine_counts,
220	        n_unique=len(fused),
221	        k_used=k,
222	        weights_used=weights_used,
223	    )
224
